Cook's Assistant.name: Cook's Assistant
Cook's Assistant.description:
The Lumbridge Castle cook is in a mess. It is the Duke of Lumbridge's birthday and the cook is making the cake.

He needs a lot of ingredients and doesn't have much time.
Cook's Assistant.items_required:
* [[Egg]] (can be obtained during the quest)
* [[Bucket of milk]]
**A [[bucket]] (if obtaining the milk during the quest)
* [[Pot of flour]]
**A [[pot]] (if obtaining the flour during the quest)
Cook's Assistant.start_point: Speak to the [[Cook (Lumbridge)|Cook]] in the kitchen of [[Lumbridge Castle]].

Romeo & Juliet.name: Romeo & Juliet
Romeo & Juliet.description: [[Romeo]] and [[Juliet]] are desperately in love, but Juliet's father doesn't approve. Help them to find a way to get married and live happily ever after.

Demon Slayer.name: Demon Slayer
Demon Slayer.description: A mighty demon is being summoned to destroy the city of Varrock. You find out you are the one destined to stop him (or at least try).
Demon Slayer.items_required:
*A [[water]] container, such as a [[bucket of water]] (obtainable during the quest)
*25 [[bones]] (can't be [[noted]], but can be delivered in increments)
*1 [[coin]]
Demon Slayer.start_point: Speak to [[Aris]] in the tent on the western side of [[Varrock Square]].

Shield of Arrav.name: Shield of Arrav
Shield of Arrav.description: Varrockian literature tells of a valuable shield, stolen long ago from the museum of [[Varrock]], by a gang of professional thieves. See if you can track down this shield and return it to the museum. You will need a friend to help you complete this quest.
Shield of Arrav.items_required:
*Phoenix Gang: 20 [[coins]]
*Black Arm Gang: 2 [[Phoenix crossbow]]s (obtained during the quest)
Shield of Arrav.start_point: [[Varrock]] Palace library. Talk to [[Reldo]], the librarian, located at the northern end of [[Varrock Castle]].

Sheep Shearer.name: Sheep Shearer
Sheep Shearer.description: Fred the Farmer's sheep are getting mighty woolly. He will pay you to shear them.
Sheep Shearer.items_required:
*20 [[balls of wool]] (unnoted)
**[[Shears]] if obtaining [[wool]] to spin into balls of wool (obtained during the quest)
Sheep Shearer.start_point: Speak to [[Fred the Farmer]] in the house by the [[sheep]] pen north of [[Lumbridge Castle]].

The Restless Ghost.name: The Restless Ghost
The Restless Ghost.description: A ghost is haunting [[Lumbridge]] graveyard. The priest of the Lumbridge church of [[Saradomin]] wants you to find out how to get rid of it.
The Restless Ghost.start_point: Speak to [[Father Aereck]] in the chapel south-east of [[Lumbridge Castle]].

Ernest the Chicken.name: Ernest the Chicken
Ernest the Chicken.description: Veronica is very worried. Her fiancé went into the big spooky manor house to ask for directions. An hour later and he's still not out yet.
Ernest the Chicken.items_required:
* [[Spade]] (obtainable during the quest)
* [[Fish food]] (obtainable during the quest)
* [[Poison (item)|Poison]] (obtainable during the quest)
Ernest the Chicken.start_point: Speak to [[Veronica]], south of [[Draynor Manor]] by the gate.

Vampyre Slayer.name: Vampyre Slayer
Vampyre Slayer.description: The people of [[Draynor Village]] live in constant terror. Their numbers are dwindling, all due to the foul creature lurking in the manor to the north known as a vampyre.
Vampyre Slayer.items_required:
*[[Hammer]] (can be bought from the [[general store]] just north of the [[Blue Moon Inn]] for 1 [[coin]])
* [[Beer]] (obtainable during the quest for 2 [[coins]])
* [[Stake]] (obtained during the quest)
Vampyre Slayer.start_point: Speak to [[Morgan]] in his house in [[Draynor Village]], just north of [[Ned]]'s house.

Imp Catcher.name: Imp Catcher
Imp Catcher.description: The Wizard Grayzag has summoned hundreds of little imps. They have stolen a lot of things belonging to the Wizard Mizgog including his magic beads.
Imp Catcher.start_point: Speak to [[Wizard Mizgog]] on the top floor of the [[Wizards' Tower]].

Prince Ali Rescue.name: Prince Ali Rescue
Prince Ali Rescue.description: Prince Ali of Al Kharid has been kidnapped by the scheming Lady Keli. You are hired to stage a rescue mission.
Prince Ali Rescue.items_required:
*[[Soft clay]]
*3 [[balls of wool]]
*[[Yellow dye]] or 2 [[onion]]s and 5 [[coins]]
*[[Redberries]]
*[[Ashes]]
*[[Bucket of water]] or [[jug of water]] (obtainable during quest)
*[[Pot of flour]]
*[[Bronze bar]]
*[[Pink skirt]]
*3 [[beer]]s
*[[Rope]] (can be bought during the quest from [[Ned]] for 18 [[coins]])
*30-40 [[coins]] for the Al Kharid [[toll gate]] if not using teleports to and from Al Kharid
Prince Ali Rescue.start_point: Speak to [[Hassan]] in the palace at [[Al Kharid]].

Doric's Quest.name: Doric's Quest
Doric's Quest.description: Doric the dwarf is happy to let you use his anvils but first he would like you to run an errand for him.
Doric's Quest.ironman_concerns: *A method of obtaining [[iron ore]]: **[[File:Mining icon.png|link=Mining|alt=Mining]] 15 [[Mining]] [boostable] to mine it **As a 1/20 drop from [[Flesh Crawlers]] in the [[Stronghold of Security]] **As a 1/8.667 drop from [[Mind Golem]]s in the [[Ruins of Camdozaal]] (requires [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] [[Below Ice Mountain]]) **As a drop from [[Iron ore#Item sources|other monsters]]
Doric's Quest.start_point: Speak to [[Doric]] in the small house north of [[Falador]], just outside the east gate of [[Taverley]].

Black Knights' Fortress.name: Black Knights' Fortress
Black Knights' Fortress.description: The Black Knights are up to no good. You are hired by the white knights to spy on them and destroy their secret weapon.
Black Knights' Fortress.items_required:
* [[Cabbage]] (can be obtained from the cabbage patch in [[Edgeville Monastery]] and [[South Falador Farm]], as well as [[Lumbridge Castle#Cellar|Lumbridge cellar]]. Cabbages obtained from [[Draynor Manor]] '''CANNOT''' be used)
* [[Iron chainbody]] (can be purchased at [[Horvik's Armour Shop]] in [[Varrock]] and from [[Wayne's Chains - Chainmail Specialist|Wayne's Chains]] in [[Falador]])
* [[Bronze med helm]] (can be purchased in the helmet shop in [[Barbarian Village]], spawns by the backdoor of [[Draynor Manor]])
Black Knights' Fortress.start_point: Speak to [[Sir Amik Varze]] on the 2nd floor[UK]3rd floor[US] of the [[White Knights' Castle]]'s western tower in [[Falador]].

Witch's Potion.name: Witch's Potion
Witch's Potion.description: Become one with your darker side. Tap into your hidden depths of magical potential by making a potion with the help of Hetty the Rimmington witch.
Witch's Potion.items_required:
*[[Burnt meat]]
*[[Eye of newt]]
*[[Onion]]
*[[Rat's tail]] ''(only obtainable after starting the quest)''
Witch's Potion.start_point: Talk to [[Hetty]] in her house in [[Rimmington]], south of [[Falador]] and west of [[Port Sarim]].

The Knight's Sword.name: The Knight's Sword
The Knight's Sword.description: Sir Vyvin's squire is in trouble. He has accidentally lost Sir Vyvin's ceremonial sword. Help him find a replacement without Sir Vyvin finding out.
The Knight's Sword.items_required:
* [[Redberry pie]] (not [[half a redberry pie]])
* 2 or 4 [[iron bar]]s (4 if you want your own [[blurite sword]])
*1-3 [[Blurite ore]] (obtained during the quest) - optionally an additional ore each for your own blurite sword and the Falador easy diary ''(Members only)''
* Any [[pickaxe]] (one spawns in Rimmington)
The Knight's Sword.ironman_concerns: *A method to obtain iron bars: **[[File:Mining icon.png|link=Mining|alt=Mining]] 15 [[Mining]] and [[File:Smithing icon.png|link=Smithing|alt=Smithing]] 15 [[Smithing]] if smelting [[iron ore]] **Spawn in level 18 [[Wilderness]] north-west of the [[Ferox Enclave]], next to the bears west of the Graveyard of Shadows. Accessible via [[File:Minigame icon.png|link=Minigames|alt=Minigames]] Clan Wars [[Minigame Teleport]] **1/42.67 drop from [[dwarf|dwarves]] ([[File:Attack style icon.png|link=Combat level|alt=Combat level]] 10 ) in the mines under Falador **''(Members only)'' Spawn in [[Thurid's Brain Buckets]] on the [[Sunset Coast]] in [[Varlamore]] (requires [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] [[Children of the Sun]] ) **''(Members only)'' 1/34.42 drop from sorting [[small salvage]] with [[File:Sailing icon.png|link=Sailing|alt=Sailing]] 15 [[Sailing]] *A method to obtain a redberry pie: **[[File:Cooking icon.png|link=Cooking|alt=Cooking]] 10 [[Cooking]] to cook one. A pie dish can be found in the [[Varrock Palace]] kitchen, and [[redberries]] can be picked just west of the [[South-east Varrock mine]] **''(Members only)'' Purchased from [[Romily Weaklax]] in the [[Cooks' Guild]] with [[File:Cooking icon.png|link=Cooking|alt=Cooking]] 32 [[Cooking]] and a [[chef's hat]] **''(Members only)'' Partial completion of [[Perilous Moons]] to purchase a Redberry pie from [[Yarnio]]
The Knight's Sword.start_point: Speak to the [[squire]] in the courtyard of the [[White Knights' Castle]] in [[Falador]].

Goblin Diplomacy.name: Goblin Diplomacy
Goblin Diplomacy.description: There's a disturbance in the Goblin Village. Help the goblins solve their dispute so the world doesn't have to worry about rioting goblins. Aim: to help the goblins decide which colour they will wear.
Goblin Diplomacy.items_required:
* 3 sets of [[Goblin mail]] (obtained during the quest)
* 1 [[Blue dye]] (or 2 [[woad leaves]] and 5 coins to obtain a blue dye from [[Aggie]])
** Offer 20 coins to [[Wyson the gardener]] and he will give you two woad leaves
* 1 [[Orange dye]] – created by combining [[red dye]] with [[yellow dye]]
** 1 [[Red dye]] (or 3 [[redberries]] and 5 coins to obtain a red dye from [[Aggie]])
** 1 [[Yellow dye]] (2 [[onion]]s and 5 coins to obtain a yellow dye from [[Aggie]])
Goblin Diplomacy.start_point: Speak to [[General Bentnoze]] or [[General Wartface]] in [[Goblin Village]].

Pirate's Treasure.name: Pirate's Treasure
Pirate's Treasure.description: Redbeard Frank knows the location of pirate treasure, but he'll only part with the knowledge for a bottle of [[Karamjan rum]].
Pirate's Treasure.items_required:
*[[Karamjan rum]] (obtainable during the quest for 30 coins)
*[[White apron]] (obtainable during the quest in Port Sarim at [[Gerrant's Fishy Business.]])
*60 [[coins]] (only 30 are needed if using a [[Ring of charos|Ring of charos(a)]])
*10 [[banana]]s (obtainable during the quest)
*[[Spade]] (obtainable during the quest)
Pirate's Treasure.start_point: Speak to [[Redbeard Frank]] at the northernmost dock at [[Port Sarim]], just outside [[The Rusty Anchor]].

Dragon Slayer I.name: Dragon Slayer I
Dragon Slayer I.description: Prove yourself a true champion. Kill the mighty dragon Elvarg of Crandor and earn the right to buy and wear the Rune platebody.
Dragon Slayer I.items_required: * [[Unfired bowl]] (can be made with [[soft clay]] on a [[Potter's Wheel]] with [[File:Crafting icon.png|link=Crafting|alt=Crafting]] 8 [[Crafting]]) * [[Wizard's mind bomb]] (can be bought in the [[Rising Sun Inn]] in [[Falador]] or [[The Toad and Chicken]] in [[Burthorpe]]) * [[Lobster pot]] (can be bought from any [[Fishing shops|fishing shop]]) * [[Silk]] (can be bought from [[Thessalia]] for 30 coins or the [[Al Kharid]] silk stall for 3 coins -- members can steal it from the [[Ardougne]] [[silk stall]] with [[File:Thieving icon.png|link=Thieving|alt=Thieving]] 20 [[Thieving]]) * [[Magic]]/[[ranged]] [[weapon]] as well as a [[law rune|law]] and [[air rune]] (to cast [[Telekinetic Grab]] if your Magic level is 33 or higher) '''or''' 10,000 coins * [[Hammer]] * [[Anti-dragon shield]] (obtainable during the quest) * 90 [[steel nails]] (Members can buy these from the [[Sawmill operator]] north-east of [[Varrock]], they can also be smithed at [[File:Smithing icon.png|link=Smithing|alt=Smithing]] 34 [[Smithing]] using 6 [[steel bar]]s by all players) * 3 [[plank]]s (only normal planks work) ** [[Free-to-play]]: Buy from [[Sawmill operator]] at the [[sawmill]] or pick up the spawns in the [[Graveyard of Shadows]], level 19 [[Wilderness]] ** [[Member]]: [[Plank#Spawns|Several other spawn locations]] or buy them at the [[sawmill]] * 2,000 [[coins]]
Dragon Slayer I.enemies_to_defeat:
* [[Zombie rat]]s ''(level 3)''*
* [[Ghost (Melzar's Maze)|Ghost]]s ''(level 19)''*
* [[Skeleton (Melzar's Maze)|Skeleton]]s ''(level 22)''*
* [[Zombie (Melzar's Maze)|Zombies]] ''(level 24)''*
* [[Melzar the Mad]] ''(level 43)''
* [[Lesser demon (Melzar's Maze)|Lesser demon]] ''(level 82)''
* [[Wormbrain]] ''(level 2) - optional''
* [[Elvarg]] ''(level 83)''

''An asterisk denotes that there is only one of each monster that drops the required key. They can be noted by small differences in appearance stated later in the guide.''
Dragon Slayer I.ironman_concerns: * [[File:Crafting icon.png|link=Crafting|alt=Crafting]] 8 [[Crafting]] [boostable] to create an [[unfired bowl]] *(Free-to play only) [[File:Smithing icon.png|link=Smithing|alt=Smithing]] 34 [[Smithing]] [boostable] to create [[steel nails]]
Dragon Slayer I.start_point: Speak to the [[Guildmaster]] in the [[Champions' Guild]].

Druidic Ritual.name: Druidic Ritual
Druidic Ritual.description: The Druids north of Taverley need help making a potion to reclaim the stone circle south of Varrock.
Druidic Ritual.items_required:
*[[Raw bear meat]]
*[[Raw rat meat]]
*[[Raw beef]]
*[[Raw chicken]]
Druidic Ritual.start_point: Speak to [[Kaqemeex]] in the stone circle north of [[Taverley]].

Lost City.name: Lost City
Lost City.description: Can you find the legendary lost city of [[Zanaris]]?
Lost City.items_required:
* [[Knife]]
* [[Woodcutting]] [[axe]] (you will need to bank the axe before accessing [[Entrana]] - you cannot use a blessed axe)
Lost City.enemies_to_defeat:
* [[Tree spirit (Lost City)|Tree Spirit]] ''(level 101)'' (Safespottable)  
* Multiple [[Zombie (Entrana Dungeon)|Zombies]] ''(level 25)'' (for a [[bronze axe]])
Lost City.ironman_concerns: [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] The player is required to go to [[Entrana]].
Lost City.start_point: Go to [[Lumbridge Swamp]]. Talk to the campers there.

Witch's House.name: Witch's House
Witch's House.description:
A young boy who lives in Taverley has kicked his ball into the garden of a scary old lady.

He asks you to get it back for him. This proves more difficult than it first sounds.
Witch's House.items_required:
*[[Cheese]] (You may wish to bring several in case you mess up. One source is in [[Draynor Village]] - [[Aggie]]'s house)
*[[Leather gloves]] or any of the gloves listed [[Gate (witch's house)#Gloves|here]] (obtainable during the quest, may take some time)
Witch's House.start_point: Talk to the [[boy]] near the gate entrance of [[Taverley]].

Merlin's Crystal.name: Merlin's Crystal
Merlin's Crystal.description: The wizard [[Merlin]] has been trapped in a magical crystal by the witch [[Morgan Le Faye]]. So far, King Arthur hasn't been able to figure out how to free his mentor from his crystal prison. Can you help?
Merlin's Crystal.items_required:
*[[Bread]]
*[[Tinderbox]]
*[[Bucket of wax]] (obtainable during the quest with either the [[Beekeeper's outfit]] or [[insect repellent]]; a [[bucket]] spawns near the east gate of the beehive enclosure)
*[[Bat bones]] (obtainable during the quest)

Heroes' Quest.name: Heroes' Quest
Heroes' Quest.description: Prove you are worthy to enter the Heroes' Guild. To prove your status as a hero you will need to obtain a number of items. There are many challenges standing between you and these items.
Heroes' Quest.items_required:
* [[Ice gloves]] (obtainable during the quest) or [[Smiths gloves (i)]]
* [[Fishing rod]] (obtainable during the quest)
* [[Fishing bait]] (obtainable during the quest)
* [[Dusty key]] (obtainable during the quest; if travelling to [[Taverley Dungeon]] for the [[lava eel]], without 70 [[Agility]]) '''OR''' a [[knife]] or [[slash weapon]] (if travelling to the [[Lava Maze]] for the [[lava eel]]) '''OR''' 60 [[Sailing]] level (if travelling to the [[Charred Dungeon]])
* [[Harralander potion (unf)]] (or a [[harralander]] and a [[vial of water]])
* [[Pickaxe]]
'''If you are a [[Black Arm Gang]] member''' (Note: If you are returning to the mansion after the quest to help another player, you do not need the black armour anymore):
* [[Black full helm]] (trimmed will not work)
* [[Black platebody]] (trimmed will not work)
* [[Black platelegs]] (plateskirt or trimmed will not work)
'''If you are a [[Phoenix Gang]] member:'''
* Some form of [[Ranged]] or [[Magic]] attack method
Heroes' Quest.enemies_to_defeat:
* [[Ice Queen]] ''(level 111)'' (unless you already have Ice Gloves)
* [[Entrana firebird]] ''(level 2)''
* [[Jailer]] ''(level 47, optional)''
* [[Grip]] ''(level 22)'' (for Phoenix Gang members only)
Heroes' Quest.ironman_concerns:
[[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] The player briefly visits [[Entrana]].
*Obtaining black armour for Black Arm Gang members
**The helm and legs can be purchased from the [[Champions' Guild]] and the platebody can be obtained from [[Horvik's Armour Shop]] in [[Varrock]]
Heroes' Quest.start_point: Speak to [[Achietties]] by the entrance to the [[Heroes' Guild]], between [[Taverley]] and [[Burthorpe]].

Scorpion Catcher.name: Scorpion Catcher
Scorpion Catcher.description: Thormac the Sorcerer has a hobby involving scorpions. Unfortunately, three of them have escaped and managed to run far away. If you manage to find them all, he may just be able to perform an important service for you.
Scorpion Catcher.items_required: *[[Dusty key]] (obtainable during the quest) or [[File:Agility icon.png|link=Agility|alt=Agility]] 70 [[Agility]] [boostable].
Scorpion Catcher.enemies_to_defeat: * [[Jailer]] ''(level 47)'' if you need to acquire the [[dusty key]] (alternatively, you can skip this if you have access to the [[Legends Guild Shop of Useful Items]], where the key is sold)
Scorpion Catcher.start_point: Speak to [[Thormac]] at the top floor of the [[Sorcerer's Tower]], south-east of the [[Ranging Guild]].

Family Crest.name: Family Crest
Family Crest.description: There is a man in [[Varrock]] who needs a bit of help. He is of noble heritage, but without his family's crest, he can't prove it. Unfortunately, his three sons took the crest with them when they left, scattering all across Gielinor.
Family Crest.items_required:
* [[Shrimps]]
* [[Salmon]]
* [[Tuna]]
* [[Bass]]
* [[Swordfish]]
* [[Pickaxe]] - [[Adamant pickaxe|adamant]] or [[Rune pickaxe|rune]] (41+ Mining) highly recommended
* 2 [[Ruby|cut rubies]]
* [[Ring mould]]
* [[Necklace mould]]
* Any [[poison cure]] (you will lose this item). Does not need to contain full doses.
* Means to cast all 4 [[Blast spells]]
Family Crest.ironman_concerns: '''Warning:''' [[File:Hardcore ironman chat badge.png]] The player visits the [[Wilderness]] section of [[Edgeville Dungeon]] *Obtaining the cooked fish: **All raw fish except salmon can be found at [[Frankie's Fishing Emporium]] in Port Piscarilius **[[Salmon]] can be purchased cooked from the [[West Ardougne General Store]] **[[Swordfish]] can be purchased cooked from [[The Shrimp and Parrot]] in [[Brimhaven]], and 5 are received as a reward from [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] [[Tribal Totem]] **[[Bass]] can be obtained cooked as a 1/50 drop from [[Ankou]]s, or from [[Gourmet impling]]s with [[File:Hunter icon.png|link=Hunter|alt=Hunter]] 28 [[Hunter]] [boostable]. Bass can also be purchased cooked from the [[Warrior Guild Food Shop]].

Fishing Contest.name: Fishing Contest
Fishing Contest.description: The mountain Dwarves' home would be an ideal way to get across White Wolf Mountain safely. However, the Dwarves aren't too fond of strangers. They will let you through if you can bring them a trophy. The trophy is the prize for the annual Hemenster fishing competition.
Fishing Contest.items_required:
*[[Spade]]
*5 [[coins]] (10 if you buy a [[fishing rod]] from Jack)
*[[Garlic]] (obtainable during quest)
*[[Fishing rod]] or [[Pearl fishing rod]] ([[oily fishing rod]] does '''not''' work)
*[[Red vine worm]] (obtainable during quest)
Fishing Contest.start_point: Mountain Dwarves at either of the entrances to the tunnel that goes under [[White Wolf Mountain]].

Tribal Totem.name: Tribal Totem
Tribal Totem.description: Lord Handelmort of Ardougne is a collector of exotic artefacts. A recent addition to his private collection is a strange looking totem from Karamja. The Rantuki tribe are not happy about the recent disappearance of their totem.
Tribal Totem.start_point: Speak to [[Kangai Mau]] at [[The Shrimp and Parrot]] restaurant in [[Brimhaven]].

Monk's Friend.name: Monk's Friend
Monk's Friend.description: A monk's child has had their blanket stolen. Find the thieves' den and return the blanket, then help Brother Omad organise the drinks for the child's birthday party.
Monk's Friend.items_required:
* [[Jug of water]]
* Regular [[logs]] or a regular [[plank]]
Monk's Friend.start_point: Speak to [[Brother Omad]] at the [[Ardougne Monastery]] south of [[East Ardougne]].

Temple of Ikov.name: Temple of Ikov
Temple of Ikov.description: A mysterious stranger called Lucien asks you to go on a mission deep under the Temple of Ikov in central Kandarin. He wants you to retrieve an artefact known as the Staff of Armadyl. After you retrieve it, the quest doesn't stop there... Are you on the side of good or evil?
Temple of Ikov.items_required:
* Any [[bow]] capable of firing [[ice arrows]] ([[yew bow]] or better) '''or''' any [[Ranged#Weapons|thrown weapon]]
* 20 unnoted [[limpwurt root]]s
*A method of reducing your weight to at least -1kg temporarily:
**[[Boots of lightness]] (can be obtained during the quest with a [[light source]] and [[slash weapon]], such as a [[lit candle]] and [[knife]])
**Other forms of [[weight-reducing clothing]] such as the [[graceful outfit]]
Temple of Ikov.enemies_to_defeat:
* [[Fire Warrior of Lesarkus]] ''(level 84)''
* [[Guardian of Armadyl]] ''(level 43) (Safespottable)'' - only if siding with Lucien
* [[Lucien]] ''(level 14)'' - only if siding with the Guardians of Armadyl
Temple of Ikov.ironman_concerns: * [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] 20 unnoted items need to be delivered at once. *A method of defeating the [[Fire Warrior of Lesarkus]]: **[[File:Ranged icon.png|link=Ranged|alt=Ranged]] 40 [not boostable] and access to a [[Yew longbow]] or better. [[Yew longbow]]s can be obtained as a rare drop from [[young impling]]s. **'''OR''' [[Darts]] or [[Throwing knives]] to bypass the Ranged requirement.
Temple of Ikov.start_point: Speak to [[Lucien]] at the [[Flying Horse Inn]] in the north-west corner of [[East Ardougne]].

Clock Tower.name: Clock Tower
Clock Tower.description: Help the confused Brother Kojo find the missing cogs and fix his clock tower. Search the dungeon using brawn and brains to correctly place the four cogs.
Clock Tower.items_required:
* [[Bucket of water]] (obtainable during quest) 
** Alternatively, a [[Jug of water]], [[Ice gloves]], or [[Smiths gloves (i)]]
Clock Tower.start_point: Talk to [[Brother Kojo]] in the [[Clock Tower (building)|Clock Tower]] south of [[East Ardougne]].

Holy Grail.name: Holy Grail
Holy Grail.description: King Arthur is sending out his knights on a quest for the famous Holy Grail. If you are a Knight of the Round Table go to King Arthur for further orders.
Holy Grail.ironman_concerns: [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] The player briefly visits [[Entrana]].

Tree Gnome Village.name: Tree Gnome Village
Tree Gnome Village.description:
The tree gnomes are in trouble. General Khazard's forces are hunting them to extinction.

Find your way through the hedge maze to the gnomes secret treetop village.

Then help the gnomes fight Khazard and retrieve the orbs of protection.
Tree Gnome Village.items_required:
* 6 normal [[logs]] (or an [[axe]], obtainable during the quest by killing [[goblin]]s in the Tree Gnome Village maze, or retrievable from the [[canoe station]] near the north entrance to the maze, if you have one stored)
* Combat equipment
* Food
Tree Gnome Village.start_point: Centre of the [[Tree Gnome Village (location)|Tree Gnome Village]] [[Gnome Maze|Maze]], talk to [[King Bolren]].

Fight Arena.name: Fight Arena
Fight Arena.description:
The prosperous Servil family have been abducted by the infamous General Khazard. He plans to have the family battle for his entertainment in the Fight Arena.

Can you rescue the Servils before the tyrant has these innocent (not to mention wealthy) civilians slain?
Fight Arena.start_point: Talk to [[Lady Servil]]. She is west by south-west of the [[Ardougne Monastery|Monastery]] that is south of [[Ardougne]].

Hazeel Cult.name: Hazeel Cult
Hazeel Cult.description: Discover the truth behind the Carnillean family fortune. Decide for yourself whether to aid the Carnilleans in retrieving stolen goods, or join the Hazeel cult members in their mission to resurrect the infamous Lord Hazeel.
Hazeel Cult.start_point: Central [[Ardougne]], south of [[King Lathas]]' [[Ardougne Castle|castle]]. Talk to [[Ceril Carnillean]].

Sheep Herder.name: Sheep Herder
Sheep Herder.description: Dispose of plagued sheep and save Ardougne!
Sheep Herder.start_point: Speak to [[Councillor Halgrive]] outside of the [[East Ardougne]] church.

Plague City.name: Plague City
Plague City.description: The western part of Ardougne has been shut off as a deadly plague sweeps through the people. However, a young woman who went in to try to help those stricken by the illness has disappeared and her parents have become worried.
Plague City.items_required: * [[Dwellberries]] (Spawns in [[McGrubor's Wood]], squeeze through the loose railing, use ALS, or [[Telekinetic Grab]] them. They can be bought from [[Heckel Funch]] or [[Hudo]] in the [[Tree Gnome Stronghold]] or grown with 36 [[Farming]].) *[[Rope]] (Purchasable in the [[East Ardougne]] [[general store]], [[Port Khazard]] [[general store]], [[Ned]], or a spawn in [[Karamja]] general store.) *[[Spade]] (One spawns near Edmond.) *1-4 [[Bucket of water|buckets of water]] (four uses required, there is a [[water source]] and [[bucket]] spawn nearby) *[[Hangover cure]] or the ingredients to make one: **[[Chocolate dust]] ***[[Chocolate bar]] ***[[Knife]] or [[pestle and mortar]] **[[Bucket of milk]] (Obtained by using a bucket on a [[dairy cow]], one is north of [[Ardougne]], or bought from [[Hudo]] or [[Heckel Funch]].) **[[Snape grass]] (Spawns west of the [[Crafting Guild]] or the peninsula north-east of [[Hosidius]]. It can be grown at 61 [[Farming]]. Can also be obtained from killing [[Tribesmen]] in [[Karamja]].) *[[Picture]] of [[Elena]] (Inside Alrena and Edmond's house)
Plague City.ironman_concerns:
* [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] The [[hangover cure]] can be made before the quest starts by combining milk, chocolate dust, and snape grass to conserve inventory space.
* [[File:Group ironman chat badge.png]] The [[hangover cure]] itself is an '''untradeable item''' so if preparing for Group Ironman, do not premake these as you will be unable to share them with your group through Group Storage or Trading.
Plague City.start_point: Talk to [[Edmond]], living next to the wall surrounding [[West Ardougne]], north of [[Ardougne Castle]].

Sea Slug.name: Sea Slug
Sea Slug.description:
Something strange is happening on the [[Fishing Platform]].
Missing fishermen and the presence of dozens of strange sea creatures gives cause for concern.
Investigate the platform, discover the truth before it's too late.
Sea Slug.items_required: * [[Swamp paste]] (made by mixing [[flour]] with [[swamp tar]] then cooking the mixture on a fire; can also be bought from a [[Trader crewmember]] for 75 coins, from [[Rasolo]] for 60 coins west of the [[Fishing Guild]], or from the [[Khazard General Store]] for 42 coins at full stock)

Waterfall Quest.name: Waterfall Quest
Waterfall Quest.description: Investigate the death of elven leaders of old. Search for the elf King Baxtorian's tomb and discover the mysterious hidden treasure of the waterfall.
Waterfall Quest.ironman_concerns: [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] You must briefly visit Glarial's Tomb, which has [[Glarial's Tomb#Restricted items|significant item restrictions]].
Waterfall Quest.start_point: Speak to [[Almera]] in her house north-east of [[Baxtorian Falls]].

Jungle Potion.name: Jungle Potion
Jungle Potion.description: [[Trufitus Shakaya]] of the [[Tai Bwo Wannai Village]] requires that you collect five special jungle herbs for a potion so he can commune with his Gods.

The Grand Tree.name: The Grand Tree
The Grand Tree.description: The [[Grand Tree]], which shelters the majority of the small gnome population of Gielinor, is dying. Is it human sabotage or an inside job? Help [[King Shareen]] to find the true cause and save the tree gnomes from an uncertain fate.
The Grand Tree.items_required: * 1,000 [[coins]] if you previously chose not to help [[Femi]] on your first entry to the [[Tree Gnome Stronghold]] (not required if you never used the gate entrance)
The Grand Tree.enemies_to_defeat: * [[Black demon (The Grand Tree)|Black demon]] ''(level 172)'' (can be safespotted)
The Grand Tree.start_point: Talk to [[King Narnode Shareen]] in the [[Grand Tree]].

Underground Pass.name: Underground Pass
Underground Pass.description: There are rumours of a secret passageway that can be used to get to the other side of the mountains west of Ardougne. Now it's just a matter of finding it.
Underground Pass.items_required:
* [[Rope]] (2 recommended)
* [[Bow]]
* 1-5 [[arrows]] (only non-poisoned arrows with metal [[arrowtips]] will work. [[Craw's bow]] does not work. Crossbows and bolts will not work. Broad arrows,  Ogre arrows, Ice arrows, and Training Arrows will also not work.)
* [[Spade]]
* [[Bucket]] (obtainable during quest)
* [[Tinderbox]] (obtainable during quest)
Underground Pass.enemies_to_defeat:
* 3 demons [[Doomion]], [[Othainian]], and [[Holthion]] ''(level 91)'' (easily [[safespot]]table)
* 3 [[Paladin]]s ''(level 62)'' (easily [[safespot]]table)
* [[Kalrag]] ''(level 89)'' ([[safespot]]table) (won't hit with [[Protect from Melee]])
* [[Disciple of Iban]] ''(level 13)''
Underground Pass.start_point: Talk to [[King Lathas]] on the 1st floor[UK]2nd floor[US] of [[Ardougne Castle]].

Observatory Quest.name: Observatory Quest
Observatory Quest.description: The Observatory in south-west Ardougne has been ransacked by a family of nearby goblins. Can you help the professor to rebuild it?
Observatory Quest.enemies_to_defeat: * [[Goblin guard]] ''(level 42)'' (Optional – can simply be trapped behind a column)
Observatory Quest.start_point: Talk to the [[Observatory professor]] in the [[Observatory]] Reception Room north of [[Castle Wars]].

The Tourist Trap.name: The Tourist Trap
The Tourist Trap.description:
South of Al Kharid a new desert pass has opened up, leading to the dangers of the Kharid desert.
Equip your desert garb and make sure your waterskin is full before heading off into the trackless dunes in search of desert adventure.
The Tourist Trap.items_required:
'''Note that all of the required items can be bought from [[Shantay]] at the [[Shantay Pass]].'''
* [[Desert shirt]], [[Desert robe|robe]], and [[Desert boots|boots]] (black versions do not work)
* A [[hammer]]
* One [[bronze bar]] (recommended to bring at least 2 additional bars, 24-96% chance to succeed based on Smithing level)
* Ten [[feather]]s (30-50+ recommended at lower [[Fletching]] levels)
* Means to enter the desert, such as the [[Shantay pass (item)|Shantay pass]],  [[desert amulet 2]], [[teleport tablet]]s or [[Teleport scrolls|scrolls]], [[fairy rings]] or the [[pharaoh's sceptre]]
The Tourist Trap.ironman_concerns: [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] You need to unequip your armour and weapons, and require two empty slots in addition to that. Failing dialogue will require 15 empty inventory spaces.
The Tourist Trap.start_point: Speak to [[Irena]] by the [[Shantay Pass]], south of [[Al Kharid]].

Watchtower.name: Watchtower
Watchtower.description: The Wizards of the Yanille Watch Tower have been reporting increased ogre sightings recently. Use a wide variety of your skills to help the wizards recover their stolen power crystals and stop the advance of the ogre hordes!
Watchtower.items_required:
* 20 [[coins]]
* [[Gold bar]]
* [[Tinderbox]]
* [[Death rune]] (obtainable during the quest)
* [[Pickaxe]]
* [[Dragon bones]]
* 2 [[rope]]s (2nd rope obtainable during the quest)
* [[Guam potion (unf)]]
**[[Guam leaf]] and [[vial of water]] if making your own potion (vial obtainable during the quest)
* Any [[light source]] (obtainable during the quest)
* [[Pestle and mortar]] (obtainable during the quest)
* [[Bat bones]] (obtainable during the quest with a [[knife]] or [[slash weapon]], grinding the bat bones before starting the quest to leave the pestle and mortar in the bank is also an option)
* [[Jangerberries]] (obtainable during the quest)
Watchtower.enemies_to_defeat:
* [[Gorad]] ''(level 68)'' (safespottable)
* [[Giant bat]] ''(level 27)'' (only if bat bones are needed)
Watchtower.ironman_concerns: *A method to obtain [[dragon bones]]: **Killing [[blue dragons]] in [[Taverley Dungeon]] or the [[Dragon Nest]] near [[Tal Teklan]] ([[Blue dragon#Safespotting|safespottable]]) **Killing [[green dragons]] in the [[Wilderness]] **Killing [[red dragons]] in [[Brimhaven dungeon]] **Killing [[Elvarg]] in the [[Nightmare Zone]] after the completion of [[Dragon Slayer I]] **Killing [[Blue Dragon]] in [[Heroes' Guild]] after the completion of [[Heroes' Quest]] ([[Telekinetic Grab]] optional but recommended) *A method to obtain a [[gold bar]]: **[[File:Smithing icon.png|link=Smithing|alt=Smithing]] 40 [[Smithing]] to smith one **[[File:Magic icon.png|link=Magic|alt=Magic]] 33 [[Magic]] to use [[Telekinetic Grab]] on the spawn in the [[Varrock]] west bank basement **Spawn in the [[Corsair Cove Dungeon]], requiring [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] [[Dragon Slayer I]] unless using [[Telekinetic Grab]] **1/10 from [[eclectic implings]] with [[File:Hunter icon.png|link=Hunter|alt=Hunter]] 50 [[Hunter]] **1/64 from [[cave slime]] with [[File:Slayer icon.png|link=Slayer|alt=Slayer]] 17 [[Slayer]] *A method to obtain [[Guam leaves]]: **1/11.1 drop from [[chaos druid]]s for the grimy herb **Picking the herbs in the [[Sorceress's Garden]] winter garden for [[guam leaves]] (1/3.37). Requires completion of [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] Prince Ali Rescue
Watchtower.start_point: Begin the quest by speaking to [[Watchtower Wizard]] north of [[Yanille]] in the [[Watchtower (building)|Watchtower]]. You must climb the [[trellis]] on the north side of the tower to get up there for the first time.

Dwarf Cannon.name: Dwarf Cannon
Dwarf Cannon.description:
For several years now, the Dwarven Black Guard have been developing the latest in projectile warfare.

With the constant attack of goblin renegades, the dwarven troops who protect the mines need to put this secret weapon into action.

Only with your help can the true power of this cannon be harnesse
Dwarf Cannon.start_point: Talk to [[Captain Lawgof]], south of the [[Coal Trucks]], north-west of the [[Fishing Guild]].

Murder Mystery.name: Murder Mystery
Murder Mystery.description: Old man Sinclair has been found dead in his mansion! Unfortunately, the local authorities are having some trouble figuring out who's the guilty party. Maybe the unbiased eye of a passing adventurer will be of help.
Murder Mystery.start_point: The [[Sinclair Mansion]], north along the path between [[Seers' Village]] and [[Camelot]]. Talk to one of the guards around the mansion.

The Dig Site.name: The Dig Site
The Dig Site.description:
Feeling uneducated? Desperate for buried treasure?

"There's gold in them there hills" (well stream actually) as well as many other items that can be found at the digsite. Join the students in their attempt to be a qualified archaeologist, and become experienced in making the earth move!

Have you g
The Dig Site.items_required:
* [[Pestle and mortar]]
* [[Vial]]
* [[Tinderbox]]
* [[Cup of tea]] (can be stolen from the [[Varrock]] [[Ye Olde Tea Shoppe|tea stall]]) or [[nettle tea]] or [[cup of tea (nettle)]]
* 2 [[rope]]s
* [[Opal]] or uncut opal (small chance to acquire while panning for a quest item)
* [[Charcoal]] (obtainable during the quest, but may take some time)
* [[Specimen brush]] (obtained during the quest)
* [[Specimen jar]] (obtained during the quest)
* [[Panning tray]] (obtained during the quest)
* [[Trowel]] (obtained during the quest)
The Dig Site.start_point: Talk to an [[examiner]] in the [[Exam Centre]] south of the [[digsite]].

Gertrude's Cat.name: Gertrude's Cat
Gertrude's Cat.description: Gertrude has lost her cat Fluffs and desperately wants to find her. Can you help bring her home?
Gertrude's Cat.items_required:
* [[Bucket of milk]]
* 100 [[coins]]
* [[Seasoned sardine]] (Bought from the [[Grand Exchange]]) or alternatively:
** [[Raw sardine]] (can be bought from [[Gerrant's Fishy Business.]])
** [[Doogle leaves]] (obtainable during the quest)
Gertrude's Cat.start_point: Go to [[Gertrude]]'s house which is just west of [[Varrock]].

Legends' Quest.name: Legends' Quest
Legends' Quest.description: Only the elite are allowed to call themselves a member of the Legends' Guild. Do you have what it takes to make it in?
Legends' Quest.items_required:
* Any [[machete (weapon)|machete]] (obtainable during the quest)
* 2 [[gold bar]]s (6 in case you fail or want more [[gold bowl]]s)
* [[Hammer]] or [[Imcando hammer]]
* [[Rope]]
* [[rune axe|Rune]], [[dragon axe|Dragon]] or [[Infernal axe]]
* [[Lockpick]] (2 in case you fail) ([[Strange old lockpick]] does not work)
* [[Pickaxe]]
* [[Vial of water]]
* [[Ardrigal]] (obtainable during the quest)
* [[Snake weed]] (obtainable during the quest)
* One of each cut gem up to diamond:
** [[Sapphire]]
** [[Emerald]]
** [[Ruby]]
** [[Diamond]]
** [[Opal]]
** [[Jade]]
** [[Red topaz]]
* [[Soul rune]]
* [[Mind rune]]
* 2 [[law runes]]
* [[Earth rune]]
* [[Unpowered orb]] and runes to cast any [[Charge Orb]] once (requires the [[standard spellbook]] equipped - note that at Magic level 56 you can only cast the Charge Water Orb spell - two casts are needed if taking the "long path" during the second fight with the demon)
* [[Charcoal]] (2-3 in case it breaks -- obtainable during the quest if travelling via Shilo Village)
* 3 [[Papyrus]] (4-5 in case one is torn -- obtainable during the quest if travelling via Shilo Village or from the spawn on Radimus's desk)
Legends' Quest.enemies_to_defeat:
* [[Ranalph Devere]] ''(level 92)'' — 2 times
* [[Irvig Senay]] ''(level 100)'' — 2 times
* [[San Tojalon]] ''(level 106)'' — 2 times
* [[Nezikchened]] ''(level 187)'' — 3 times
Legends' Quest.start_point: Talk to the [[Legends' Guard]]s just outside of the [[Legends' Guild]].

Rogue Trader.name: Rogue Trader
Rogue Trader.items_required:
*3 [[dyes]] of any colour (to unlock [[Menaphite clothing]]) OR 3 animal pelts of the same kind (to unlock the [[Desert outfit]]). This includes [[cowhide]], [[grey wolf fur]], [[snakeskin]], [[bear fur]], or [[wool]] (not [[balls of wool]])
*100 coins (for blackjack street urchin, optional)

Rune Mysteries.name: Rune Mysteries
Rune Mysteries.description: After the first [[Wizards' Tower]] burned down a hundred years ago, the incantation allowing teleportation to the [[rune essence mine]] was lost. However, after much research from the [[Order of Wizards]], the incantation is on the verge of rediscovery...
Rune Mysteries.start_point: Speak to [[Duke Horacio]] on the 1st floor[UK]2nd floor[US] of [[Lumbridge Castle]].

Death Plateau.name: Death Plateau
Death Plateau.description:
Trolls have come down from the north and are camped on Death Plateau. They are using this base to launch attacks on the principality of Burthorpe!
The Imperial Guard is taking heavy losses. They need your help!
Death Plateau.items_required:
* 60 [[coins]] (bring 1000 for the gambling dice with Harold part)
*10 unnoted [[bread]]
*10 unnoted cooked [[trout]]
*[[Iron bar]]
*[[Asgarnian ale]] (can be found on the bar at the pub in [[Burthorpe]])
Death Plateau.ironman_concerns: [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] Players need 21 empty inventory spaces to deliver items to Tenzing. *A method to obtain an iron bar: **[[File:Mining icon.png|link=Mining|alt=Mining]] 15 [[Mining]] and [[File:Smithing icon.png|link=Smithing|alt=Smithing]] 15 [[Smithing]] if smelting [[iron ore]] **Spawn in [[Thurid's Brain Buckets]] on the [[Sunset Coast]] in [[Varlamore]] (requires [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] [[Children of the Sun]] ) **Spawn in level 18 [[Wilderness]] north-west of the [[Ferox Enclave]], next to the bears west of the Graveyard of Shadows. Accessible via [[File:Minigame icon.png|link=Minigames|alt=Minigames]] Clan Wars [[Minigame Teleport]] **1/42.67 drop from [[dwarf|dwarves]] ([[File:Attack style icon.png|link=Combat level|alt=Combat level]] 10 ) in the mines under Falador **1/34.42 drop from sorting [[small salvage]] with [[File:Sailing icon.png|link=Sailing|alt=Sailing]] 15 [[Sailing]]
Death Plateau.start_point: Talk to [[Denulth]] in his tent in [[Burthorpe]]. Tell him you will discover the path for him.

Eadgar's Ruse.name: Eadgar's Ruse
Eadgar's Ruse.description:
Sanfew requires a rare herb for the next part of the purifying ritual.

Travel back to the Troll Stronghold to find this herb that the trolls use as an ingredient for their favourite dish.
Eadgar's Ruse.items_required:
* [[Climbing boots]] (or 12 [[coins]] to buy from Tenzing)
* [[Vodka]] (sold by [[Funch's Fine Groceries|Heckel Funch]] at the [[Tree Gnome Stronghold]] bar for 5 coins)
* [[Pineapple chunks]] (pineapples are sold by [[Funch's Fine Groceries|Heckel Funch]] at the [[Tree Gnome Stronghold]] bar or any [[Charter_ship|Charter Ship]], use a knife to dice the pineapple into chunks)
* 1 [[Logs|Log]] (2 logs and a [[tinderbox]] are recommended)
* 10 [[Grain]] (obtainable during the quest, just south of [[Tegid]] in Taverley)
* 5 [[Raw chicken]] (there are chickens on the way at [[Tenzing]] the Sherpa's house. Undead chickens do not work)
* [[Pestle and mortar]]
* [[Ranarr potion (unf)]]
Eadgar's Ruse.ironman_concerns: *A method of obtaining a [[ranarr potion (unf)]]: **Killing [[Chaos druids]] for [[grimy ranarr weed]] (1/32.4) **Picking the herbs in the [[Sorceress's Garden]] autumn garden for [[ranarr weed]] (1/11). Requires completion of [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] Prince Ali Rescue and [[File:Thieving icon.png|link=Thieving|alt=Thieving]] 45 [[Thieving]].
Eadgar's Ruse.start_point: Talk to [[Sanfew]] in upper floor of the building where [[Jatix's Herblore Shop]] is in [[Taverley]].

Big Chompy Bird Hunting.name: Big Chompy Bird Hunting
Big Chompy Bird Hunting.description:
Rantz the Ogre needs to feed his demanding children, Fycie and Bugs, so he wants to go Big Chompy Bird hunting. Problem is, he's all fingers and thumbs when it comes to making ogre arrows.

Could someone please give him a hand?
Big Chompy Bird Hunting.items_required: * Any [[axe]] that you can use ([[Blessed axe]] does not work) * [[Feather]]s (100 to be safe; coloured feathers from [[File:Hunter icon.png|link=Hunter|alt=Hunter]] [[Hunter]] work) ** Bringing 50 to buy 25 feathers from [[Fycie]] also works * [[Knife]] (or 10 to buy a knife and chisel from [[Bugs]]) * [[Chisel]] (or 10 to buy a knife and chisel from [[Bugs]]) * 4 [[wolf bones]] (obtainable during the quest from level 65 [[wolves]] or from level 14 [[wolves]] in the [[Stronghold of Security]]) * [[Cabbage]] (obtainable during the quest, Draynor cabbage will not work) * [[Tomato]] (obtainable during the quest) * [[Onion]] (obtainable during the quest) * [[Potato]] (obtainable during the quest) * [[Equa leaves]] (obtainable during the quest) * [[Doogle leaves]] (obtainable during the quest)
Big Chompy Bird Hunting.start_point: Speak to [[Rantz]], far east of [[Feldip Hills]].

Elemental Workshop I.name: Elemental Workshop I
Elemental Workshop I.description:
Hundreds of years ago, a mineral was found that had the ability to change the property of Magic.

The magicians, fearing the effect this may have on their profession, sealed this Workshop forever; or so they had hoped. See if you can rediscover the lost knowledge of elemental ore.
Elemental Workshop I.items_required:
* [[Knife]] (obtainable during the quest) or a [[slash weapon]] (e.g. [[dagger]] or [[scimitar]])
* [[Pickaxe]]
* [[Needle]] (obtainable during the quest, '''[[Costume needle]] does not work''')
* [[Thread]]
* [[Leather]] (obtainable during the quest)
* [[Hammer]]
* 4 [[coal]] (4 more for each additional bar you would like to produce)
Elemental Workshop I.ironman_concerns: * [[File:Mining icon.png|link=Mining|alt=Mining]] 30 [[Mining]] [boostable] (if mining the coal)
Elemental Workshop I.start_point: [[Seers' Village]], the house south-west of the bank. Search the bookcase there.

Nature Spirit.name: Nature Spirit
Nature Spirit.description:
After saving the holy man Drezel, he's seeking some assistance again.
This time he has a special request for any adventurous sorts to search for the Druid 'Filliman Tarlock' and brave the terrors that infest the swamp of Mort Myre.
Nature Spirit.ironman_concerns: * [[File:Crafting icon.png|link=Crafting|alt=Crafting]] 18 [[Crafting]] [boostable] to craft a [[silver sickle]] using a [[silver bar]] on a [[furnace]] with a [[sickle mould]] in your inventory. The [[silver bar]] may be purchased from the [[Silver merchant]] in [[Ardougne]].
Nature Spirit.start_point: Speak to [[Drezel]] under the Paterdomus Temple near the [[River Salve]].

Priest in Peril.name: Priest in Peril
Priest in Peril.description: Recently contact has been lost with the temple to Saradomin on Misthalins' Eastern border. [[King Roald]] would be very interested to know what has caused this lack of communication, and seeks an adventurer willing to report back to him on the situation.
Priest in Peril.items_required:
* 50 unnoted [[rune essence]] or [[pure essence]] (can be mined by talking to [[Aubury]] in [[Varrock]], after completing [[Rune Mysteries]]. All 50 essence do not have to be of the same type) (does not needed to be handed in all at once; multiple trips can be made.)
* [[Bucket]] (obtainable during the quest from Varrock general store for 2 coins)
Priest in Peril.enemies_to_defeat:
* [[Temple guardian]] dog ''(level 30) - can be safespotted or flinched, immune to [[Magic]]
* [[Monk of Zamorak]] ''(level 30) - can be safespotted or flinched''
Priest in Peril.start_point: Speak to [[King Roald]] in the [[Varrock Palace]].

Regicide.name: Regicide
Regicide.description:
Continuing the Plague City series, the Regicide Quest takes you beyond the 'Well of Voyage' to a new realm.

King Lathas will employ you once again, this time for the grim task of disposing of his brother.

Upon travelling to the realm you will find yourself surrounded by new and strange plants, ani
Regicide.items_required: * 5 [[coal]] in the bank (although at least 25 is recommended; you may bring the coal noted to save you a bank trip) * [[Bow]] and some [[arrows]] (or [[fire arrows|lit fire arrows]] if you already have some.) * 2 [[rope]]s (3 in case you fail) * [[Spade]] * [[Limestone]] (or a [[pickaxe]] and [[File:Mining icon.png|link=Mining|alt=Mining]] 10+ [[Mining]]). Note that [[limestone brick]]s will '''not''' work. * [[Tinderbox]] * [[Strip of cloth]] (made on a [[loom]] with 4 [[balls of wool]]; '''not''' a [[bolt of cloth]] - you can use the [[loom]] at the [[Iorwerth Camp]] or at the [[Falador]] farm) * [[Pestle and mortar]] * [[Gloves]], the list on [[Quicklime]] shows all gloves that work. * [[Pot]] (obtainable during quest) * [[Cooked rabbit]] (obtainable during quest) or [[roast rabbit]]
Regicide.start_point: Talk to [[King Lathas]] on the 1st floor[UK]2nd floor[US] of [[Ardougne Castle]].

Tai Bwo Wannai Trio.name: Tai Bwo Wannai Trio
Tai Bwo Wannai Trio.description:
In Jungle Potion, the Shaman Trufitus communed with the gods to determine the fate of his people.

Now, Timfraku, the Chief of Tai Bwo Wannai, wishes to see his people and family return to the abandoned village.

Help a depressed fisherman, encourage a raging hunter and satiate an eccentric priest i
Tai Bwo Wannai Trio.items_required:
* [[Knife]] or bladed weapon, such as a [[Sword]] or a [[Spear]]
* [[Sliced banana]] (obtainable during the quest)
* [[Small fishing net]]
* [[Pestle and mortar]] (obtainable during the quest for 6 gp)
* 2 [[Logs]] to quickly make [[Poison karambwan]] at fishing site and to cook [[Pasty jogre bones]] later in the quest
* [[Iron spear]], [[Steel spear]], [[Mithril spear]], [[Adamant spear]], [[Rune spear]] or [[Dragon spear]] which will be consumed during the quest.
* [[Agility potion|Agility potion(4)]] (must be 4 doses)
* [[Ranged]] [[equipment]], [[Magic]] [[equipment]], or a [[halberd]] to kill a [[monkey]] for a [[monkey corpse]] (adjacent melee does 0 damage to monkeys)
* [[Tinderbox]] (If you wish to avoid running back to the range in Brimhaven)
* [[Jogre bones]] (obtainable during the quest)
* [[Seaweed]] (obtainable during the quest)
* [[Karamjan rum]] or 30 coins to purchase (if obtaining the rum before starting, add the sliced banana or the rum will disappear when you leave the island)
Tai Bwo Wannai Trio.ironman_concerns: * A method of obtaining an [[agility potion(4)]]: ** [[File:Herblore icon.png|link=Herblore|alt=Herblore]] 34 [[Herblore]] [boostable] to create one ** Giving [[Dr Jekyll]] a clean [[irit leaf]] during the [[Jekyll and Hyde]] [[random event]] ** Killing [[Skeleton (Tarn's Lair)|skeletons]] in [[Tarn's Lair]] for a 1/25.6 drop per 2 dose potion, which can then be [[decanting|decanted]] into a 4 dose ** A possible [[Icthlarin's Little Helper#Walkthrough|reward]] from defeating the [[apparition]] during [[Icthlarin's Little Helper]] ** A possible reward from a [[Apprentice potion pack]] obtained through [[Mastering Mixology]] * [[File:Fishing icon.png|link=Fishing|alt=Fishing]] 65 [[Fishing]] [boostable] to catch [[raw karambwan]] (necessary if you burn the one given to you) * The ability to defeat a level 53 enemy (can be safespotted and the bones telegrabbed at the [[Ardougne Zoo]]) * Obtaining an [[iron spear]] or better. A [[steel spear]] spawns in the Ancient Prison in [[Neypotzli]] at the western wall. Alternatively, it may be bought from the [[Mount Karuulm Weapon Shop]], [[Fortis Blacksmith]] in [[Varlamore]], or dropped by enemies such as [[Moss Giant]]s, [[Jogres]], [[Tribesmen]], and [[Hobgoblins]]
Tai Bwo Wannai Trio.start_point: Talk to [[Timfraku]] in his house in [[Tai Bwo Wannai]].

Troll Stronghold.name: Troll Stronghold
Troll Stronghold.description:
The Imperial Guard raid was a failure, and Dunstan's son has been captured by the trolls!

Journey through Trollheim to the Troll Stronghold, and rescue him!
Troll Stronghold.enemies_to_defeat: * [[Dad]] ''(level 101)'' (can be safespotted) * [[Troll General]] ''(level 113)'' (can be safespotted) * [[Berry]] and [[Twig]] ''(both level 71)'' (can be safespotted) '''Only''' if you have less than [[File:Thieving icon.png|link=Thieving|alt=Thieving]] 30 [[Thieving]] or fail to pickpocket them (can be safespotted)

Shades of Mort'ton.name: Shades of Mort'ton
Shades of Mort'ton.description: The townsfolk of [[Mort'ton]] have some form of strange affliction and the town is full of Shades. Can you find some way to reverse this?
Shades of Mort'ton.items_required: * 1000+ [[coins]] (bring plenty of money just in case) * 2 [[Tarromin potion (unf)]] OR 2 [[vials of water]] and 2 [[tarromins]] (both obtainable during the quest) * 2 [[ashes]] *[[Tinderbox]] *[[Logs]] OR an [[axe]] to obtain some * 1 [[hammer]] **Using a [[Flamtaer hammer]] is '''highly recommended''' in case the minigame is unpopulated, as a regular hammer is insufficient to build the temple alone. An additional 13,000 coins (or 5,436 on the [[Grand Exchange]]) allows you to buy a [[Flamtaer hammer]] from Razmire.
Shades of Mort'ton.start_point: Find the [[Diary (Shades of Mort'ton)|Diary of Herbi Flax]] on a shelf in a house at the village of [[Mort'ton]].

The Fremennik Trials.name: The Fremennik Trials
The Fremennik Trials.description:
Council workmen have at last found the time to make repairs to the footbridge that connects Kandarin with the Barbarian town of Rellekka.

Do you have what it takes to impress the barbarians who live there, and perhaps be welcomed as an honorary member of their clan?
The Fremennik Trials.items_required: * 5,252 [[coins]] * [[Beer]] (obtainable during quest) * [[Tinderbox]] * Either a [[raw shark]], [[raw manta ray]], or [[raw sea turtle]] * [[Lyre]], obtainable as a drop from [[Lanzig]], [[Borrokar]], [[Lensa]], or [[Freidir]], or the skills and materials to make one: ** [[File:Fletching icon.png|link=Fletching|alt=Fletching]] 25 [[Fletching]], [[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] 40 [[Woodcutting]], and [[File:Crafting icon.png|link=Crafting|alt=Crafting]] 40 [[Crafting]] (all [[boostable]]) ** Woodcutting [[axe]] ** [[Knife]] ** [[Potato]] (obtainable during quest) ** [[Onion]] (obtainable during quest) ** [[Cabbage]] (obtainable during quest)
The Fremennik Trials.enemies_to_defeat:
* [[Koschei the deathless]] (first three forms)
* [[Draugen]] ''(level 69)''
'''OPTIONAL'''
* [[Lanzig]], [[Borrokar]], [[Lensa]], or [[Freidir]] ''(level 48)'' (multiple times)
The Fremennik Trials.ironman_concerns: * [[File:Fishing icon.png|link=Fishing|alt=Fishing]] 76 [[Fishing]] [boostable] is required to fish for [[raw shark]]s yourself, but there are multiple alternatives: ** [[Rufus' Meat Emporium]] in [[Canifis]] sells a single [[raw shark]]. ** [[Frankie's Fishing Emporium]] in [[Port Piscarilius]] sells [[raw shark]]. ** [[River troll]]s in the [[Enchanted Valley]] drop them commonly, but require access to [[fairy rings]]. ** [[Waterfiend]]s, combat level 115, rarely drop noted [[raw shark]]. ** [[Mogre]]s, combat level 60, require completion of [[Skippy and the Mogres]], [[File:Slayer icon.png|link=Slayer|alt=Slayer]] 32 [[Slayer]] [boostable], and [[fishing explosives]] to attack. They can rarely drop a [[raw shark]]. ** [[Wintertodt]] can drop a [[raw shark]] after [[File:Fishing icon.png|link=Fishing|alt=Fishing]] 43 [[Fishing]], though it will be very rare, but chances increase with Fishing level. ** [[Raw shark]] has a slight chance to drop from salvaging [[Barracuda salvage]]. * [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] Sections of this quest have strict inventory requirements: ** [[#Thorvald the Warrior|Thorvald the Warrior]]: No weapons, armour, or runes. (A [[Looting bag]] is not allowed, regardless of its contents) ** [[#Peer the Seer|Peer the Seer]]: No items '''at all''' equipped or in your inventory.
The Fremennik Trials.start_point: Speak to [[Brundt the Chieftain]] (located in the longhall) in [[Rellekka]].

Horror from the Deep.name: Horror from the Deep
Horror from the Deep.description:
The lighthouse protecting Kandarin's north-western coastline has mysteriously stopped operating, and contact with the lighthouse keeper Jossik has been lost.

The Council would greatly appreciate it if somebody could discover for them what has happened to this most vital landscape feature.
Horror from the Deep.items_required:
*[[Fire rune]]
*[[Air rune]]
*[[Water rune]]
*[[Earth rune]]
*Any [[sword]] or [[longsword]] that you are willing to lose (except [[rusty sword]] and [[prop sword]])
*Any [[arrow]] (except [[ogre arrows]] and [[training arrows]])
*[[Molten glass]]
*[[Tinderbox]]
*[[Hammer]]
* 60 [[steel nails]]
* 2 regular [[plank]]s (can be obtained from [[Barbarian Outpost]])
*[[Swamp tar]]
*At least one [[combat style]] to defeat the [[Dagannoth Mother]] - she cycles every 18 seconds between only being vulnerable to one of six different types of attack
**'''Highly recommended''' to bring means to deal damage with all of the elemental [[Magic]] types ([[air spell|air]], [[water spell|water]], [[earth spell|earth]], and [[fire spells]])
**You may also bring means to deal [[Ranged]] and [[Melee]] damage (she has high resistance to melee)
Horror from the Deep.enemies_to_defeat:
* [[Dagannoth (Horror from the Deep)]] ''(level 100)''  ''(can be completely safespotted)'' 
* [[Dagannoth Mother]] ''(level 100)''  ''(with use of a safespot and [[Protect from Missiles]], all damage can be negated)''
Horror from the Deep.start_point: Speak with [[Larrissa]], who is outside the [[Lighthouse]], north of [[Barbarian Outpost]].

Throne of Miscellania.name: Throne of Miscellania
Throne of Miscellania.description:
Rumours have been coming back from Miscellania that all is not well, it is said that the king has been turned into a monster!

An exceptional hero is required to investigate. Experience in ruling kingdoms, wooing royalty, and negotiating peace would be an advantage.
Throne of Miscellania.items_required: * [[Iron bar]] * Any common, non-silver [[ring]] (such as a [[gold ring]]) that you're willing to lose ''(not [[Onyx ring|onyx]]/[[Dragonstone ring|dragonstone]]/[[Explorer's ring|explorer]]/[[Beacon Ring|beacon]]/[['perfect' ring]]/[[Ring of nature|nature]]/[[Pineapple ring|pineapple]]/[[Ring of charos|Charos]]/[[Fremennik rings|fremennik]]/[[Ancient rings|ancient]])'' *At least one of the following to gain favour during the quest **1,875 to buy flowers from [[Flower Girl]] (fastest method, but click intensive) **An axe and [[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] 45 [[Woodcutting]] to cut [[maple tree]]s (slower, but more AFK than buying flowers manually, since one click yields multiple logs and therefore multiple instances of favour) **A rake and [[File:Farming icon.png|link=Farming|alt=Farming]] 10 [[Farming]] to rake flax **A pickaxe and [[File:Mining icon.png|link=Mining|alt=Mining]] 35 [[Mining]] to mine [[coal]] **A [[harpoon]] or [[lobster pot]] and [[File:Fishing icon.png|link=Fishing|alt=Fishing]] 35 [[Fishing]] to fish [[tuna]], [[lobsters]] or [[swordfish]] * [[Logs]] (obtainable during the quest if you bring an axe)
Throne of Miscellania.start_point: Talk to [[King Vargas]] in the [[Miscellania Castle]]. Take the longboat from [[Rellekka]] to access [[Miscellania]].

Monkey Madness I.name: Monkey Madness I
Monkey Madness I.description:
The King of the northern Gnomes, Narnode Shareen, is once again in need of your help. He recently decided to send an envoy of his Royal Guard, the 10th squad, to oversee the decommissioning of the Gnome owned ship-building facilities on the eastern coast of [[Karamja]].

It has been quite some time
Monkey Madness I.items_required:
* A [[gold bar]]
* Five empty inventory slots
* A [[ball of wool]]
* [[Monkey bones]] or [[Monkey corpse|corpse]] from [[Karamja]] (obtainable during quest)
* Five [[banana]]s (obtainable during quest)
Monkey Madness I.ironman_concerns: * [[File:Smithing icon.png|link=Smithing|alt=Smithing]] 40 [[Smithing]] [boostable] (to make the gold bar if not dropped by a monster) or [[File:Magic icon.png|link=Magic|alt=Magic]] 33 [[Magic]] [boostable] (to telegrab a gold bar from [[Varrock west bank]] basement) or [[File:Thieving icon.png|link=Thieving|alt=Thieving]] 5 [[Thieving]] to steal from a [[crafting stall]] in Keldagrim. A gold bar is also a reward from [[The Digsite]], [[Goblin Diplomacy]], and [[Waterfall Quest]]
Monkey Madness I.start_point: Talk to [[King Narnode Shareen]] in the [[Grand Tree]].

Haunted Mine.name: Haunted Mine
Haunted Mine.description:
Recent forays into Morytania by some of Saradomin's more fanatical supporters have unearthed murky rumours concerning an abandoned mine in the south.

Legend speaks of an unusual material, hidden in the depths of the mines, that is somehow linked to the desolation and fear that now surrounds the pla
Haunted Mine.start_point: Speak to the [[Zealot]] at the mines in [[Morytania]] on the path to Mort'ton.

Troll Romance.name: Troll Romance
Troll Romance.description: Ug loves Aga, but Aga is together with Arrg. Since Arrg is a lot stronger than Ug, Ug can't follow the regular mating ritual, which involves ripping the opponent into pieces. Will you be able to find out what will impress the troll female more than a display of violence? Will you survive the journey
Troll Romance.items_required:
* An [[iron bar]]
* [[Maple logs|Maple]] or [[yew logs]]
* [[Rope]]
* [[Climbing boots]] (not needed if using [[Trollheim Teleport]])
* [[Wax]] (can be made ahead of time)
** A [[bucket of wax]] (use [[insect repellent]] on a beehive followed by a [[bucket]])
** A [[cake tin]]
** [[Swamp tar]]
Troll Romance.enemies_to_defeat: [[Arrg]] ''(level 113)'' ([[safespot]]table; difficult to reach) '''Note:''' This fight is not a safe death.
Troll Romance.ironman_concerns: * [[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] 45 [[Woodcutting]] [boostable] to cut a maple log ** '''OR''' Enough combat stats and supplies to get a 1/128 drop of yew logs from [[Swamp Crab]]s (requires [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] [[Priest in Peril]] ). ** '''OR''' Enough supply of [[Serum 208]] to get a 1/128 drop of maple logs from curing [[Afflicted]] (requires [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] [[Shades of Mort'ton]] ). ** '''OR''' [[Throne of Miscellania]] completed to obtain maple logs from [[managing Miscellania]]. ** '''OR''' [[File:Firemaking icon.png|link=Firemaking|alt=Firemaking]] 50 [[Firemaking]] [not boostable] to get a maple/yew log from [[Reward Cart]] via [[Wintertodt]]. ** '''OR''' Completing [[Hunters' Rumours]] with [[File:Hunter icon.png|link=Hunter|alt=Hunter]] 46 [[Hunter]] [not boostable] to receive [[Hunters' loot sack]] (drop chance based on [[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] level) ** '''OR''' High enough combat stats to kill [[Giant Mole]] for a 1/12.8 drop of yew logs. *A method to obtain an iron bar: **[[File:Mining icon.png|link=Mining|alt=Mining]] 15 [[Mining]] and [[File:Smithing icon.png|link=Smithing|alt=Smithing]] 15 [[Smithing]] if smelting [[iron ore]] **Spawn in [[Thurid's Brain Buckets]] on the [[Sunset Coast]] in [[Varlamore]] (requires [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] [[Children of the Sun]] ) **Spawn in level 18 [[Wilderness]] north-west of the [[Ferox Enclave]], next to the bears west of the Graveyard of Shadows. Accessible via [[File:Minigame icon.png|link=Minigames|alt=Minigames]] Clan Wars [[grouping]] teleport **1/42.67 drop from [[dwarf|dwarves]] ([[File:Attack style icon.png|link=Combat level|alt=Combat level]] 10 ) in the mines under Falador **1/34.42 drop from sorting [[small salvage]] with [[File:Sailing icon.png|link=Sailing|alt=Sailing]] 15 [[Sailing]]
Troll Romance.start_point: Speak to [[Ug]] at the [[Troll Stronghold (location)|Troll Stronghold]].

In Search of the Myreque.name: In Search of the Myreque
In Search of the Myreque.description: Canifis is experiencing an upsurge in new faces and a new stranger has appeared with a job to track down some local heroes known only as "The Myreque". The Myreque are fighting a desperate battle against the darkness of Morytania and he wishes to take them weapons to aid in the battle. Can you help
In Search of the Myreque.items_required:
* A [[steel longsword]]
* 2 [[steel sword]]s
* A [[steel mace]]
* A [[steel warhammer]]
* A [[steel dagger]]
* 225 [[steel nails]]
* A [[Druid pouch]] containing at least 5 charges (3 charges must be added per fill)
**6 [[Mort myre fungus]] OR 3 [[Mort myre pear]] OR 3 [[Mort myre stem]] to fill the pouch
**A [[Silver sickle (b)]] to collect these items for the charges if not buying from the [[Grand Exchange]]
* A [[hammer]] or [[Imcando hammer]]
* 6 normal [[plank]]s
* 10 [[coins]] ''or'' a [[ring of charos (a)]]
In Search of the Myreque.ironman_concerns: All of the required steel weapons can be purchased from [[Briget's Weapons]] in [[Shayzien]], except for the [[steel warhammer]], which can be purchased from [[Vigr's Warhammers]] in [[Keldagrim]], or from [[Mount Karuulm Weapon Shop]] in [[Mount Karuulm]].
In Search of the Myreque.start_point: Speak to [[Vanstrom Klause]] at the [[Hair of the Dog]] tavern in [[Canifis]].

Creature of Fenkenstrain.name: Creature of Fenkenstrain
Creature of Fenkenstrain.description:
[[Dr Fenkenstrain]], master and sole occupant of the castle to the north-east of [[Canifis]], needs a new servant to go on a dark errand for him.

Do you have the stomach to help Fenkenstrain complete his twisted purpose?
Creature of Fenkenstrain.items_required: * [[Ghostspeak amulet]] * [[Silver bar]] * 3 [[bronze wire]] (unnoted) * [[Needle]] ([[costume needle]] does not work) * 5 [[thread]] * [[Spade]] * 50 [[coins]] '''OR''' 1 [[law rune|law]] & 1 [[air rune]] for [[Telekinetic Grab]] (requires [[File:Magic icon.png|link=Magic|alt=Magic]] 33 [[Magic]])
Creature of Fenkenstrain.ironman_concerns: * [[File:Smithing icon.png|link=Smithing|alt=Smithing]] 4 [[Smithing]] [boostable] (to make a [[bronze wire]])
Creature of Fenkenstrain.start_point: Read the [[signpost (Creature of Fenkenstrain)|signpost]] in the middle of [[Canifis]].

Roving Elves.name: Roving Elves
Roving Elves.description: Deep in the elven forests of Isafdar, live a duo of roving elves: Islwyn a grumpy, old elf who dislikes humans and his companion, [[Eluned]], who is a beautiful, friendly, young female elf with a kind heart. In this quest, you are challenged to overcome Islwyn's prejudice against humans and alleviat
Roving Elves.items_required:
* [[Glarial's pebble]] (obtainable during the quest)
* [[Key (Waterfall Dungeon)|Key]] (obtainable during the quest)
* [[Spade]]
* [[Rope]]
Roving Elves.enemies_to_defeat: * [[Moss Guardian]] ''(level 84)'' (has to be killed with [[Glarial's Tomb#Restricted items|restricted items]], can be [[flinched]])
Roving Elves.ironman_concerns: [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] You must briefly visit Glarial's Tomb which has [[Glarial's Tomb#Restricted items|significant item restrictions]]. Items dropped outside the tomb may be picked back up within three minutes.
Roving Elves.start_point: Talk to [[Islwyn]] inside the elven woods of [[Isafdar]].

Ghosts Ahoy.name: Ghosts Ahoy
Ghosts Ahoy.description: East of [[Canifis]] is a ghost port. Not meaning that it's deserted. It's full of ghosts! For some reason, they're unable to pass on to the afterlife, but some aren't happy with that. Can you help?
Ghosts Ahoy.items_required:
* Some amount of [[Ecto-tokens]]:
** 31 [[Ecto-token]]s (16 with [[ring of charos (a)]])
** OR 25 Ecto-tokens (10 with ring) using [[charter ship]]s
** OR 1 bone, 1 pot, and 1 bucket for every 5 ecto-tokens required (see [[Ghosts_Ahoy#Pre-quest: Obtaining Ecto-tokens at the Ectofuntus|Pre-quest]] for more info)
* ~400 [[Coins]]
* [[Ghostspeak amulet]]
* [[Bucket of milk]]
* [[Silk]]
* 3 colours of [[dye]] (The dye colours you will need are random. If you want to save a trip, have 3 [[Red dye|red]], 3 [[Blue dye|blue]], and 3 [[yellow dye]]s. That way, you can mix whatever colours you end up needing.)
*[[Spade]]
*[[Oak longbow]]
*[[Knife]] (can be purchased from the Canifis general store)
*[[Needle]] and [[thread]] (can be purchased from the Canifis general store) or a [[costume needle]]
* One extra [[bucket of slime]] for colouring the bed sheet (can be purchased from a [[Charter ship]] crewmember at the port).
* [[Nettle tea]] (prepared ahead of time, within a [[bowl of water]]) ''or'' items to make nettle tea:
** A [[tinderbox]] and a [[log]] of which you have the [[Firemaking]] level to light. Tinderbox can be purchased from the general store during quest
**[[Gloves]] (must be closed fingered - [[Slayer gloves]], [[Ranger gloves]], [[Infinity gloves]], [[Moonclan gloves]], [[Granite gloves]], [[Lunar gloves]] and [[Beekeeper's gloves]] won't work)
**[[Nettles]] (obtainable during the quest)
**[[Bowl of water]] (empty bowl can be purchased during quest -- the [[gold bowl]] won't work)
Ghosts Ahoy.ironman_concerns: [[File:Ultimate_ironman_chat_badge.png]] [[Nettle tea]] and [[bucket of milk]] can be combined before the quest starts to save inventory space
Ghosts Ahoy.start_point: Talk to [[Velorina]] in [[Port Phasmatys]]. Can use a charter ship for 1,300 gp in Port Sarim talking to [[Trader Stan]] on the southern-most dock.

One Small Favour.name: One Small Favour
One Small Favour.description:
If you're new to Shilo Village after solving the mystery (or even if you did it some time ago), you may like to check out Yanni Salika's antiques shop.

He's a busy chap, revamping antique items, checking out new stock from potential adventurers and maybe he'll have a small<sup>(1)</sup> favour to a
One Small Favour.items_required: * 4 [[steel bar]]s * [[Bronze bar]] * [[Iron bar]] * The ingredients required to make a [[Guthix rest]]: ** 2 [[Guam leaves]] ** [[Marrentill]] ** [[Harralander]] ** [[Cup of hot water]] (obtainable during the quest) * [[Hammer]] (obtainable during the quest) or [[Imcando hammer]] * 5 [[pigeon cage|pigeon cages]] (obtainable during the quest) ** '''Note:''' The pigeons do not need to be let out of the cages. * [[Pot]] (obtainable during the quest) * [[Soft clay]] (obtained during the quest) * Two of each of the following cut gems, or a [[chisel]] to cut the uncut gems received during the quest (high chance to crush them, up to 2 replacements available for 500 coins each during the quest) ** [[Opal]] ** [[Jade (gem)|Jade]] ** [[Red topaz]] ** [[Sapphire]] (cannot be crushed, so you may choose to bring a [[chisel]] instead to cut the uncut sapphires given during the quest)
One Small Favour.ironman_concerns: *A method of acquiring [[Marrentill]]: **1/14.8 drop from [[chaos druid]]s for the grimy herb. **Picking the herbs in the [[Sorceress's Garden]] winter garden for [[marrentill]] (1/3.85). Requires completion of [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] Prince Ali Rescue. *A method of acquiring [[Harralander]]: **1/25.4 drop from [[chaos druid]]s for the grimy herb. Requires [[File:Herblore icon.png|link=Herblore|alt=Herblore]] 20 [[Herblore]] to clean. **Picking the herbs in the [[Sorceress's Garden]] winter garden for [[harralander]] (1/4.09). Requires completion of [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] Prince Ali Rescue. *A method of acquiring [[Guam leaves]]: **1/11.1 drop from [[chaos druid]]s for the grimy herb. **Picking the herbs in the [[Sorceress's Garden]] winter garden for [[guam leaves]] (1/3.37). Requires completion of [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] Prince Ali Rescue.
One Small Favour.start_point: Speak to [[Yanni Salika]] in [[Shilo Village (location)|Shilo Village]].

Mountain Daughter.name: Mountain Daughter
Mountain Daughter.description: Ages ago, a small tribe of people split off from the Fremennik in Rellekka and decided to inhabit the mountains to the east of town. There they lived as nomads for many years, but now the chieftain of the camp has lost his daughter and will not move on before she has been found. Has she run away, ha
Mountain Daughter.items_required:
* [[Rope]]
* [[Pickaxe]]
* [[Axe]]
* [[Plank]]
* [[Staff]] or a [[pole]] (obtainable during the quest, [[Dramen Staff]] does not work, but [[elemental staves]] and [[Lunar staff]] do work)
* [[Gloves]] (excluding: [[Slayer gloves|Slayer]], [[Ranger gloves|Ranger]], [[Moonclan gloves|Moonclan]], [[Lunar gloves|Lunar]], [[vambraces]], [[mystic]] and random event apparel)
Mountain Daughter.start_point: Talk to [[Hamal the Chieftain]] in the [[Mountain Camp]] east of [[Rellekka]]. To get in, take the path north of the [[Golden Apple Tree]] and use a [[rope]] on the boulder behind the guard (see below for more details).

Between a Rock....name: Between a Rock...
Between a Rock....description: Hidden away from the fearsome trolls and the roaming Fremennik, the dwarves delve deep beneath the mountains in search for the ore they need to support their home of Keldagrim. One dwarf in particular, however, has for years been trying to crack open a rock that seems to be impervious to any materia
Between a Rock....items_required:
* A [[pickaxe]]
* 4 [[gold bar]]s (You will need an extra bar and [[Telekinetic Grab|telegrab]] if you want to keep a [[golden cannonball]] for after the quest)
* A [[hammer]]
* An [[ammo mould]] ''(can be bought for 5 coins during the quest)'' or a [[double ammo mould]]
* ~830 [[coins]] ''(for cart and ferry)''
Between a Rock....start_point: Speak to [[Dondakan the Dwarf]] at the [[Keldagrim south-west mine]].

The Feud.name: The Feud
The Feud.description:
Ali Morrisane - Gielinor's self proclaimed 'greatest merchant' - is having difficulties. He is selling things at such a rate that he cannot keep his market stall in stock.

Word has it that he is on the lookout for someone; someone who can recruit some help from his home town Pollnivneach - a somewh
The Feud.items_required:
* 501+ [[coins]]
* [[Gloves]] - (see [[Cactus (The Feud)|this page]] for gloves that '''do''' work; [[Barrows gloves]], [[ice gloves]], [[vambraces]],  and [[Slayer gloves]] do '''not''' work!)
* [[Kharidian headpiece]] (can be bought from [[Ali Morrisane]])
* [[Fake beard]] (can be bought from [[Ali Morrisane]])
* 3 [[beer]]s (obtainable during the quest, bought from [[Ali the Barman]] for 2 coins each)
* [[Bucket]] or [[Ugthanki dung]] (obtainable during the quest, spawns at the location it is needed – bring three extra buckets, four total, if you plan on doing [[My Arm's Big Adventure]] as three Ugthanki dung are needed for that quest). One more might be needed for [[Forgettable Tale...]] (1/25 chance for each player).
* [[Snake charm]] (obtainable during the quest by putting a coin in the [[snake charmer]]'s [[money pot]], if you '''do not''' already have one in your bank)
** If you have the [[snake charm]] in your bank already, you will need to retrieve it.
* [[Snake basket]] (obtainable during quest by putting a coin in the [[snake charmer]]'s [[money pot]], whether or not you have one in your bank)
The Feud.start_point: Talk to [[Ali Morrisane]] east of the Gem Trader in northern [[Al Kharid]].

The Golem.name: The Golem
The Golem.description:
Deep in the desert stand the ruins of the once great city of Uzer, where a lone survivor, an ancient clay golem, forever paces back and forth. It is badly damaged, and its mind cannot rest until it has completed the task for which it was created.

It is up to you to uncover the secret of the lost ci
The Golem.items_required:
* [[Vial]]
* [[Pestle and mortar]]
* [[Papyrus]] (can be bought from [[Ali Morrisane]] for 10gp)
* 4 [[soft clay]] (or a [[bracelet of clay]] and a [[pickaxe]] to mine north-west of the ruins)
* [[Phoenix feather]] (can be obtained north-west of the ruins)
* Means to enter the desert (such as the [[Shantay pass (item)|Shantay pass]], [[Teleport tablets]] or [[Teleport scrolls|scrolls]], [[Fairy rings]] or the [[Pharaoh's sceptre]])
The Golem.start_point: Talk to the [[clay golem]] in the ruins of [[Uzer]], east of the [[Shantay Pass]] in the [[Kharidian Desert]].

Desert Treasure I.name: Desert Treasure I
Desert Treasure I.description:
Rumours abound throughout Varrock and Al Kharid that an archaeologist has discovered clues to a hoard of treasure hidden deep in the desert, south of Al Kharid.

Anybody prepared to offer him assistance in his treasure hunting could well find themselves benefitting from a very large reward indeed...
Desert Treasure I.items_required:
* 10+ [[Shantay pass (item)|Shantay pass]]es '''or''' a way to enter the [[Kharidian Desert]] without one
* 650 [[Coins]]
* 12 [[Magic logs]] (can be noted)
* 6 [[Steel bar]] (can be noted)
* 6 [[Molten glass]] (can be noted)
* [[Ashes]]
* [[Charcoal]]
* 1 [[blood rune]]
* [[Bones]] (can be noted to prevent accidental burial)
* A [[silver bar]]
* [[Garlic powder]] (made by grinding [[garlic]] in a [[pestle and mortar]])
* [[Spice]] ([[gnome spice]] and [[Evil Dave]]'s spices will '''not''' work)
* A sweet ([[cake]], [[chocolate cake]] (part cakes are accepted), [[chocolate bar]], [[cooking apple]], or [[pineapple pizza]])
* [[Spiked boots]] (obtainable during the quest by bringing [[Dunstan]] [[climbing boots]] and an [[iron bar]]; do so '''before''' attempting to obtain the [[ice diamond]])
* [[Climbing boots]] (if [[Eadgar's Ruse]] isn't completed and/or you lack 61 [[Magic]] for the [[Trollheim Teleport]])
* A [[facemask]], [[slayer helmet]], or [[gas mask]]
* A [[tinderbox]]
* 20-50+ [[lockpick]]s
Desert Treasure I.enemies_to_defeat:
* [[Dessous]] ''(level 139)''
* 5 [[ice troll]]s ''(level 120-124)''
* [[Kamil]] ''(level 154)''
* [[Fareed]] ''(level 167)''
* [[Damis]] ''(2 forms, levels 103 and 174)''
* You will also need to tank hits by many high level enemies throughout most parts of the quest.
Desert Treasure I.ironman_concerns:
* [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] The player briefly visits [[Entrana]].
*Player will need to obtain [[Magic logs]]; see the "enchanting the mirrors" section for more information
*Lockpicks and antipoisons may be pickpocketed from the [[Bandit (Bandit Camp)|Bandits]] in the vicinity of the chest where they're needed.
Desert Treasure I.start_point: Speak to [[Asgarnia Smith]] at the [[Bedabin Camp]].

Icthlarin's Little Helper.name: Icthlarin's Little Helper
Icthlarin's Little Helper.description:
In the deep south of the desert, unrest is brewing... The cities of the [[Menaphites]] have barred their gates to all. An unsettling desert wanderer may know of a secret entrance though, to [[Sophanem]] - the city of the dead.

Will you become Icthlarin's Little Helper by entering? Enter into a tita
Icthlarin's Little Helper.items_required:
* [[cat|Regular]] or [[overgrown cat|overgrown cat]], [[hellcat]], or [[kitten]]. No food is required as the kitten can stay in your inventory for the most part, or automatically go in/out of your inventory.
* [[Tinderbox]]
* [[Willow log]]
* [[Bag of salt]] (can be bought from any [[Slayer Master]], other players, or the [[Grand Exchange]]) '''or''' an empty [[bucket]] to obtain a [[pile of salt]] in Sophanem
* [[Bucket of sap]] (use a [[knife]] on an [[evergreen tree]] or a [[Tree#Single_canopy|regular tree]] with the description "a commonly found tree" with a bucket in your inventory)
* Full [[waterskin]] to give away (can be bought for 30 coins at Shantay pass)
* [[Linen (Icthlarin's Little Helper)|Linen]] (Can be bought on The Grand Exchange or bought from [[Raetul]] in Sophanem for 30 coins during the quest)
* Means to enter the desert. (Such as the [[Shantay pass (item)|Shantay pass]], [[Teleport tablets]] or [[Teleport scrolls|scrolls]], [[Fairy rings]] or the [[Pharaoh's sceptre]])
Icthlarin's Little Helper.enemies_to_defeat:
* [[Possessed Priest]] ''(level 91)''
* One of the level 75–81 guardians (chosen based on your stats):
** [[Apparition#Apmeken]] ''(level 75)''—uses [[Magic]]
** [[Apparition#Crondis]] ''(level 75)''—uses Magic
** [[Apparition#Scabaras]] ''(level 75)''—uses [[Melee]]
** [[Apparition#Het]] ''(level 81)''—uses Melee
It is possible to know in advance which of these guardians you're going to get, based on which jar your character obtains during the quest. See the "Second Flashback" section below.
