Icthlarin's Little Helper.ironman_concerns: *A method to obtain [[willow logs]]: **[[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] 30 [[Woodcutting]] [boostable] to cut [[willow trees]] **1/16 drop from [[Vampyre Juvinates]] (requires starting [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] [[In Aid of the Myreque]] ) **1/128 drop from [[Swamp Crab]]s (requires [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] [[Priest in Peril]] )
Icthlarin's Little Helper.start_point: Speak to the [[wanderer]] in the camp west of the [[Agility Pyramid]].

Tears of Guthix.name: Tears of Guthix
Tears of Guthix.description: Deep in the caves in the [[Lumbridge Swamp]] is an enchanted place where the tears shed by [[Guthix]] when it saw the destruction [[Saradomin]] and [[Zamorak]] had caused with their wars flow from the very walls. These tears are said to have magical properties to help players gain deeper understandi
Tears of Guthix.items_required:
* Lit [[sapphire lantern]] or the materials to make one
** [[Bullseye lantern]] or [[bullseye lantern (unf)]] (filled with [[lamp oil]] at the [[chemist]] in [[Rimmington]] or the [[lamp still]] in [[Dorgesh-Kaan]])
** Cut [[sapphire]]
** [[Tinderbox]]
* [[Chisel]]
* [[Pickaxe]]
* [[Rope]] (if you have not yet entered the [[Lumbridge Swamp Caves]])
Tears of Guthix.ironman_concerns: *A [[bullseye lantern]] (to create the required [[sapphire lantern]]), obtainable through one of the following: **Purchase from [[Miltog]] for 600 gp (after [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] [[Death to the Dorgeshuun]]) **[[File:Smithing icon.png|link=Smithing|alt=Smithing]] 49 [[Smithing]] [boostable] to create a [[bullseye lantern (unf)]] **[[File:Crafting icon.png|link=Crafting|alt=Crafting]] 35 [[Crafting]] To complete [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] [[Haunted Mine]] to kill [[Skeleton_(Tarn's_Lair)#Low_level|Skeleton]] in [[Tarn's Lair]] **[[File:Thieving icon.png|link=Thieving|alt=Thieving]] 36 [[Thieving]] [boostable] for pickpocketing [[Cave goblin (Dorgesh-Kaan)|cave goblins]] (after [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] [[Death to the Dorgeshuun]]) for a bullseye lantern. **[[File:Slayer icon.png|link=Slayer|alt=Slayer]] 35 [[Slayer]] [boostable] to kill [[wall beast]]s for a [[bullseye lantern (unf)]] **Item spawn in Darkmeyer (after [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] [[Sins of the Father]])
Tears of Guthix.start_point: Talk to [[Juna]], deep inside the [[Lumbridge Swamp Caves]].

The Lost Tribe.name: The Lost Tribe
The Lost Tribe.description:
Sigmund is the advisor to the [[Duke Horacio|Duke]] of Lumbridge, but the [[Duke Horacio|Duke]] does not always take his advice.

When part of the cellar wall collapses the [[Duke Horacio|Duke]] insists the damage was caused by an earthquake, but Sigmund is worried about a monster attack.

If you in
The Lost Tribe.items_required:
* [[Pickaxe]]
* [[Light source]] ('''Note:''' [[Candle]]s, [[torch]]es, and [[oil lamp]]s can cause [[swamp gas]] explosions if going off-route, and need a tinderbox to stay lit)

The Giant Dwarf.name: The Giant Dwarf
The Giant Dwarf.description:
Five hundred years have passed since the rule of King Alvis, saviour of Keldagrim and victim of his own inventions.

He founded the Consortium: the gathering of Mining companies that were to serve the monarchy. But, eventually, the monarchy came to serve the Consortium.

By the time of his death, th
The Giant Dwarf.items_required:
* 200 [[coins]] for the clothes
* Means to cast [[Telekinetic Grab]] ([[air rune|air]] and [[law rune]])
* [[Logs]] (most logs will work, however, arctic pine, redwood & coloured logs do not work)
* [[Tinderbox]]
* [[Coal]] (obtainable during the quest with 30 [[Mining]])
* [[Iron bar]]
* 3 cut [[sapphire]]s 
* Various [[ores]] and [[bar]]s (mostly obtainable during the quest, see below)
* [[Redberry pie]]
The Giant Dwarf.start_point: The [[Keldagrim]] mines east of [[Rellekka]] and north of the [[Mountain Camp]]. Follow the dwarven statues to the underground river.

Recruitment Drive.name: Recruitment Drive
Recruitment Drive.description: The Temple Knights of Saradomin, a secret organisation founded many centuries ago by Saradomin himself, are currently looking to expand their ranks with some new blood. After the successful thwarting of the Black Knights' plans to take over Asgarnia, and with the personal recommendation of Sir Amik,
Recruitment Drive.ironman_concerns:
[[File:Hardcore ironman chat badge.png]] You will lose your Hardcore ironman status if you die to Sir Leye.
[[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] This quest requires an empty inventory and no items worn.
Recruitment Drive.start_point: Speak to [[Sir Amik Varze]] on the 2nd floor[UK]3rd floor[US] of the [[White Knights' Castle]]'s western tower in [[Falador]].

Mourning's End Part I.name: Mourning's End Part I
Mourning's End Part I.description:
It seems at last Arianwyn has need of your skills.

He believes there is more to the Mourners than meets the eye and he wishes for you to find out just what they're up to. Can you gain the trust of the Mourners?
Mourning's End Part I.items_required: *[[Bear fur]] (obtainable during the quest near Jorral's Outpost) *2 [[silk]] *Each of these [[dye]]s: [[red dye|red]], [[yellow dye|yellow]], [[green dye|green]], and [[blue dye|blue]] (can be bought during the quest for 6 coins each at the [[Lletya Seamstress]]). ** You may want to bring additional dyes to collect multiple coloured toads in case you miss when firing the device. *[[Bucket of water]] (can be bought from the general store in Lletya during the quest, and used on the well) *[[Feather]] *[[Rotten apple]] ('''must''' be the rotten apple on the ground north of the [[Mourner Headquarters]] in [[West Ardougne]], obtainable during the quest) *[[Toad crunchies]] or [[Premade t'd crunch]] *[[Magic logs]] *[[Leather]] *[[Ogre bellows]] (obtainable during the quest) *[[Barrel of naphtha]], or 5-9 [[coal]] to make one *7 empty inventory spaces for the [[Mourner gear]]
Mourning's End Part I.enemies_to_defeat: [[Mourner]] ''(level 11) (will decrease all combat skills to 20, including [[Hitpoints]])''
Mourning's End Part I.ironman_concerns: *A method to obtain [[magic logs]]: **[[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] 75 [[Woodcutting]] [boostable] for chopping [[magic tree]]s **[[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] 50 [[Woodcutting]] and [[File:Firemaking icon.png|link=Firemaking|alt=Firemaking]] 50 [[Firemaking]] [not boostable] to obtain magic logs as a possible reward for defeating the [[Wintertodt]] skilling boss ** [[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] 50 [[Woodcutting]] and [[File:Hunter icon.png|link=Hunter|alt=Hunter]] 46 [[Hunter]] [not boostable] to obtain magic logs as a possible reward for completing [[Hunters' Rumours]] ** [[File:Hunter icon.png|link=Hunter|alt=Hunter]] 58 [[Hunter]][boostable] to obtain magic logs as a possible drop from [[nature implings]]
Mourning's End Part I.start_point: Talk to [[Eluned]] inside the elven woods of [[Isafdar]].

Forgettable Tale....name: Forgettable Tale...
Forgettable Tale....description: Forgettable Tale of a Drunken Dwarf is part 2 in the [[Rise of the Red Axe]] series, focusing on the dwarves and their capital city of [[Keldagrim]]. After the events in [[The Giant Dwarf]], the [[Red Axe]] mining company has departed the city and set up a new headquarters in a remote underground lo
Forgettable Tale....items_required:
*~400 [[coins]]
*2 [[barley malt]] (no range in Keldagrim, plain barley has to be cooked elsewhere)
*2 [[buckets of water]]
*[[Seed dibber]] (no Tool leprechaun in Keldagrim) or partial completion of the [[Farming]] portion of the [[Barbarian Training]] [[miniquest]]
*[[Rake]] (obtainable during the quest)
*[[Ale yeast]] (or a [[Pot]] (obtainable during the quest) + 25 coins)
*[[Kebab]] (purchasable during quest for 1 coin)
*2 [[beer]]s (purchasable during quest for 1-2 coins each)
*[[Beer glass]] (obtainable during the quest)
*A random item from the list below (see list for details)
Forgettable Tale....start_point: Talk to Commander [[Veldaban]] in the [[Dwarven Black Guard]] headquarters in [[Keldagrim]].

Garden of Tranquillity.name: Garden of Tranquillity
Garden of Tranquillity.description:
'Queen of Varrock seeks experienced gardener to help construct a garden of peace and serenity as surprise for husband.

Only serious Farmers need apply. Peasants welcome (although not that welcome). Tools not supplied.

Please note: under no circumstances are the contents of this message to be discl
Garden of Tranquillity.items_required:
*[[Ring of charos]]
*All [[tool leprechaun]] tools
**A [[rake]]
**A [[seed dibber]] (or partial completion of the [[Farming]] portion of the [[Barbarian Training]] [[miniquest]])
**A [[spade]]
**[[Secateurs]] ([[magic secateurs]] work)
**A [[watering can]]
**A [[gardening trowel]]
*2 [[plant cure]]s
*A [[marigold seed]] (2 recommended in case of failure)
*3+ [[cabbage seed]]s (6 recommended to plant in both allotments to increase chance of survival)
*3+ [[onion seed]]s (6 recommended to plant in both allotments to increase chance of survival)
*A [[hammer]]
*A [[rune essence|rune]]/[[pure essence]]
*A [[pestle and mortar]]
*A [[filled plant pot]]
*2 [[ultracompost]]/[[supercompost]]/[[compost]] (note: [[Bottomless compost bucket]] will not work for these two required composts. The quality of these 2 composts do not matter.)
Garden of Tranquillity.ironman_concerns: *([[File:Ironman chat badge.png|link=Ironman mode]]) All seeds needed for the quest can be bought from [[Olivia]] in Draynor. * [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] Inventory space can be saved by creating the [[rune dust]] ahead of time: use the [[hammer]] on the [[rune essence|rune]]/[[pure essence]] to create [[rune shards]], then use the [[pestle and mortar]] on those to create [[rune dust]]. The [[hammer]] and [[pestle and mortar]] can then be safely discarded since they play no further role in the quest.
Garden of Tranquillity.start_point: Speak to Queen [[Ellamaria]] in the garden of [[Varrock Palace]].

A Tail of Two Cats.name: A Tail of Two Cats
A Tail of Two Cats.description:
Missing: small black cat, answers to the name of Bob.

Last seen on the 19th of Bennath. Please contact Unferth in Burthorpe.
A Tail of Two Cats.items_required: *[[Cat]] or [[kitten]] ([[Hellcat]]s work. Be sure to bring [[fish]] for your kitten if you're using one.) *[[Catspeak amulet]] *5 [[death rune]]s *[[Chocolate cake]] (made with [[cake]] and [[chocolate bar]] or [[chocolate dust]] with 50 [[File:Cooking icon.png|link=Cooking|alt=Cooking]] ) *[[Logs]] *[[Tinderbox]] *[[Bucket of milk]] (or one use of the [[Bottomless milk bucket]]) *[[Shears]] *4 [[potato seed]]s *[[Rake]] *[[Seed dibber]] (or partial completion of the [[Farming]] portion of [[Barbarian Training]]) *[[Vial of water]] *[[Desert shirt]] (obtainable during quest) or [[Druid's robe top]] (The [[desert outfit]] does not work) *[[Desert robe]] (obtainable during quest) or [[Druid's robe]] (The [[desert outfit]] does not work)
A Tail of Two Cats.start_point: Talk to [[Unferth]] at his house in [[Burthorpe]].

Wanted!.name: Wanted!
Wanted!.description: A wanted man is on the loose. Can you track him down?
Wanted!.items_required:
*10,000 [[coins]], or:
** [[Law rune]]
** [[Enchanted gem]] (bought from a [[Slayer Master]])
** [[Molten glass]]
*20 un-noted [[Rune essence]] or [[pure essence]] (you'll get them back)
**[[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] The player must deliver all 20 rune essence at once
* [[Light source]] ('''Note:''' [[Candle]]s, [[torch]]es and [[oil lamp]]s can cause [[swamp gas]] explosions if going off-route, and need a tinderbox to stay lit)
Wanted!.ironman_concerns: [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] The player must have 20 inventory spots to deliver all 20 rune essence at once. If the miniquest [[Enter the Abyss]] has been completed, the table in the rune shop may be used to hold rune essence from multiple trips to bring to the nearby chaos altar while inventory items are dropped without the worry of them despawning.
Wanted!.start_point: Speak to [[Sir Tiffy Cashien]] in [[Falador Park]].

Mourning's End Part II.name: Mourning's End Part II
Mourning's End Part II.description:
This chapter of the quest takes our hero deep under the mountains of Arandar to put a stop to the evil plans of the mourners.

If our hero is to succeed, they must solve one of the most complicated puzzles in all of Gielinor.
Mourning's End Part II.items_required:
*Full [[mourner gear|mourner clothes]]
*A [[chisel]] (one can be found in the house south of the Mourner HQ)
*A [[death talisman]]/[[death tiara|tiara]], [[catalytic talisman]]/[[catalytic tiara|tiara]], [[Runecraft cape]]  or [[Mourning's End Part II#The Death Altar|50 junk items]]
*[[Rope]]
Mourning's End Part II.enemies_to_defeat: None (but powerful level 73 [[Shadow (Temple of Light)|shadows]] are aggressive to players inside the temple)
Mourning's End Part II.ironman_concerns: *A method of reaching the [[Death Altar]] at the end of the quest: **Handing in [[Mourning's End Part II#The Death Altar|50 junk items]] during the quest for a [[death talisman]] '''OR''' **[[File:Slayer icon.png|link=Slayer|alt=Slayer]] 90 [[Slayer]] to kill [[dark beasts]] for a [[death talisman]] '''OR''' **Completion of [[Temple of the Eye]] to be able to participate in [[Guardians of the Rift]], where a [[catalytic talisman]] may be obtained randomly from the [[rewards guardian]]

Rum Deal.name: Rum Deal
Rum Deal.description: Zombie pirates have arisen on [[Braindeath Island]], and they don't look friendly! Production of 'rum' has stopped, and the pirate brewers are running scared. Can you overcome the horde of drunken, undead pirates? Can you even get to the island without getting a splitting headache?
Rum Deal.items_required:
*[[Rake]] (obtainable during the quest)
*[[Seed dibber]] (obtainable during the quest) or partial completion of the [[Farming]] portion of the [[Barbarian Training]] [[miniquest]]
*[[Bucket]] (obtainable during the quest)
*At least 47 [[prayer points]] for the ''[[Rum Deal#Evil spirits|Evil spirits]]'' part of the quest
Rum Deal.start_point: [[Pirate Pete]], at a dock due north of [[Port Phasmatys]].

Shadow of the Storm.name: Shadow of the Storm
Shadow of the Storm.description:
A sinister group of dark wizards is attempting to summon Agrith-Naar, a demon of exceptional mystical power. Rather than try to prevent them, a Saradominist priest asks the player to help summon the demon and then kill it once and for all.

But all is not as it seems...
Shadow of the Storm.items_required:
*[[Silverlight]] (obtainable at start of quest)
*[[Strange implement]] (obtainable during quest)
*Any black outfit (minimum of 3 pieces, see [[Shadow_of_the_Storm#Starting_Out|below]] for the allowed gear — the dyed [[Silverlight]] '''does not''' count as a piece of black clothing)
**'''Note:''' You can make a [[black desert robe]] and [[black desert shirt|shirt]] during the quest if you brought a [[desert robe]] and [[desert shirt|shirt]]
*[[Black dye]] (from grinding a black mushroom using a pestle and mortar alongside a vial)
*[[Silver bar]]
*Means to enter the desert (such as the [[Shantay pass (item)|Shantay pass]], [[Desert amulet 2]], [[Teleport tablets]] or [[Teleport scrolls|scrolls]], [[Fairy rings]] or the [[Pharaoh's sceptre]])
Shadow of the Storm.start_point: Speak to [[Father Reen]], south of [[Al Kharid]] bank.

Making History.name: Making History
Making History.description: A secluded outpost has served the City of Ardougne for generations, playing stage to many exceptional events. Its current occupant wants to employ your skills to construct its true history before time runs out and history itself is wiped away!
Making History.items_required:
*[[Spade]]
*[[Sapphire amulet]]
*[[Ghostspeak amulet]] or [[Morytania legs 2]] or higher
*A way to enter [[Port Phasmatys]]
**Completion of [[Ghosts Ahoy]] ''or''
**2 [[ecto-token]] ''or''
**1 [[bone]], 1 [[pot]] and 1 [[bucket]] to earn 5 ecto-tokens (you still only need two) ''or''
**5,200 gold for a [[Charter ship]] to [[Port Phasmatys]] (cheaper depending upon where you travel from and if you use [[ring of charos]]).
Making History.ironman_concerns: *[[File:Crafting icon.png|link=Crafting|alt=Crafting]] 24 [[Crafting]] (if making own [[sapphire amulet (u)]])
Making History.start_point: Talk to [[Jorral]] in the [[outpost]] north-west of [[West Ardougne]].

Ratcatchers.name: Ratcatchers
Ratcatchers.description:
Gertrude knows of some ratcatchers in Varrock, pay her a visit and she may tell you of their whereabouts. Perhaps they could show you and your cat a few pointers about rat catching.

Enter the subterranean world of the rat catchers and discover more of your cat's true potential.
Ratcatchers.items_required:
*Any type of [[cat]] or [[hellcat]] (for most of the quest, a cat with a high successful [[rat catch rate]] is recommended, and [[overgrown cat]]s cannot be used there -- during the [[Ratcatchers#Hooknosed Jack|Hooknosed Jack part]] of the quest, any cat with strong [[Cat#Combat_stats|combat stats]] is recommended)
*4 [[cheese]] (or 4 [[Poisoned cheese]])
*[[Rat poison]] (if cheese is not already poisoned; can be found in the [[Clock Tower Basement]]), or made during the quest with the following items:
**[[Vial|Empty vial]]
**Cleaned [[kwuarm]] (grimy will not work; [[Zahur]] in [[Nardah]] cleans grimy herbs for 200 coins)
**[[Red spiders' eggs]]
*Cleaned [[marrentill]] (grimy will not work)
*[[Unicorn horn dust]]
*[[Bucket of milk]] or [[Bottomless milk bucket]]
*[[Catspeak amulet]], '''even if''' [[Dragon Slayer II]] is completed
*[[Pot of weeds]] (use an [[empty pot]] with [[weeds]])
*[[Tinderbox]]
*101 [[coins]] (or 51 [[coins]] with a [[ring of charos (a)]])
*[[Snake charm]] (obtained from [[The Feud]] or during the quest)
*1 free inventory space
Ratcatchers.enemies_to_defeat: None, but your cat will have to fight the [[King rat]]

Spirits of the Elid.name: Spirits of the Elid
Spirits of the Elid.description:
The people in the desert village of Nardah have a problem. Their town well has dried up and any water they try to bring back to the town from the river mysteriously disappears before they can get it back.

The villagers suspect they have been cursed in some way. Can you unravel what is going on and
Spirits of the Elid.items_required:
*Runes for [[Telekinetic Grab]] (One law and one air rune)
*[[Needle]] (Note: a [[costume needle]] will not work)
*1-2 [[thread]]
*[[Crush weapon]]
*[[Stab weapon]]
*[[Slash weapon]]
*[[Light source]]
*[[Knife]] (or [[fletching knife]])
*[[Rope]]
*Any [[pickaxe]] that you have the Mining level to use (a [[bronze pickaxe]] can be obtained during the quest)
*Some form of Ranged or Magic attack method, (a [[shortbow]] and [[bronze arrow]]s can be obtained during the quest)

Devious Minds.name: Devious Minds
Devious Minds.description: No-one likes to miss a good ceremony, least of all a poor lowly monk who needs your assistance! Will you be able to aid him and make his dreams come true?
Devious Minds.items_required:
*A [[mithril 2h sword]]
*A [[bow string]]
*A non-degraded [[large pouch|large]] or [[colossal pouch]]
:''(You '''will''' lose all 3 items!)'' unless a colossal pouch is used.
Devious Minds.enemies_to_defeat: [[Abyssal creature]]s level 41+ (if not using [[Guardians of the Rift]] to obtain a large pouch)
Devious Minds.ironman_concerns: *[[Mithril 2h sword]] can be purchased at [[Gaius' Two Handed Shop]] in [[Taverley]] for 2,600 [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] The player visits [[Entrana]] for a portion of the quest. Plan ahead as the Large Pouch with the orb in it is lost on death. '''Warning:''' [[File:Hardcore_ironman_chat_badge.png]] The quest involves travelling via the [[Abyss]] located in level 4-7 [[Wilderness]], with a chance of getting killed by other players.
Devious Minds.start_point: Talk to the [[Monk (Devious Minds)|monk]] outside [[Paterdomus]], next to the [[River Salve]].

The Hand in the Sand.name: The Hand in the Sand
The Hand in the Sand.description: Bert the Sandman in Yanille is in a spot of bother. A [[Sandy hand|mysterious severed hand]] has appeared in his sand box. Help find out whose hand it is and, more to the point, how it got in Bert's sand box.
The Hand in the Sand.items_required:
*[[Beer]] (obtainable during the quest)
*About 150 [[coins]]
*1 empty [[vial]]
*[[Redberries]] (purchasable during the quest from the [[Food Store]] in [[Port Sarim]])
*[[White berries]]
*[[Lantern lens]]
*5 [[earth rune]]s (can be bought from [[Betty]] for 20 gp)
*[[Bucket of sand]] (obtainable during quest if you bring a [[bucket]])
The Hand in the Sand.ironman_concerns: [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png|Ultimate Ironman]] The player briefly visits [[Entrana]]. *A method to acquire [[White berries]]: **[[File:Farming icon.png|link=Farming|alt=Farming]] 59 [[Farming]] [boostable] and a [[whiteberry seed]] **Spawn on the [[Lava Dragon Isle]] in the [[Wilderness]] **Spawn in [[Isadfar]], north of the [[Elf Tracker]] (requires partial completion of [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] [[Regicide]]) **1/25.6 drop from [[cave crawlers]] (requires [[File:Slayer icon.png|link=Slayer|alt=Slayer]] 10 ) **1/25.6 drop from the [[Chaos druid warrior]]s in the [[Yanille Agility Dungeon]] (requires [[File:Agility icon.png|link=Agility|alt=Agility]] 40 )
The Hand in the Sand.start_point: Talk to [[Bert]] in [[Yanille]], near the [[sand pit]].

Enakhra's Lament.name: Enakhra's Lament
Enakhra's Lament.description:
Lazim the sculptor has a sad tale to tell - his days of making art are over, and now all he can do is carp at adventurers as they make a statue for him in the desert. Although, isn't just south of the Bandit Camp an odd place to be putting up a statue?
Help Lazim out with his artistic endeavours, an
Enakhra's Lament.items_required: *[[Chisel]] *One of the following: [[bread]], any kind of [[pie]], [[cake]] or [[chocolate cake]], any kind of [[pizza]], or a [[baked potato]] with topping (partial pies, cakes and pizzas do '''not''' work, and neither will burnt versions) *[[Tinderbox]] *[[Candle]] (a [[black candle]] will '''not''' work) *[[Logs]] *[[Oak logs]] *[[Willow logs]] *[[Maple logs]] *[[Pickaxe]] (unless you already have the required items) *[[Soft clay]] (obtainable during quest if you bring a pickaxe and bucket or jug of water) *[[Coal]] (obtainable during quest with level [[File:Mining icon.png|link=Mining|alt=Mining]] 30 [[Mining]][boostable]) *52 kg of [[sandstone]] (obtainable during quest with level [[File:Mining icon.png|link=Mining|alt=Mining]] 35 [[Mining]] [boostable]) *Two [[Granite (5kg)|5 kg pieces of granite]] (obtainable during quest with level [[File:Mining icon.png|link=Mining|alt=Mining]] 45 [[Mining]] [boostable]) *Runes to cast [[Fire Bolt]], [[Fire Blast]] or [[Fire Wave]] *Runes to cast [[Wind Bolt]], [[Wind Blast]] or [[Wind Wave]] *Runes to cast [[Crumble Undead]] (multiple casts recommended in case of splashing) *Enough remaining Prayer points to cast [[Protect from Melee]] for about five seconds
Enakhra's Lament.ironman_concerns: *[[File:Mining icon.png|link=Mining|alt=Mining]] 45 [[Mining]] [boostable] to mine [[granite]] *A method to obtain [[maple logs]]: **[[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] 45 [[Woodcutting]] [boostable] to cut [[maple trees]] **Guaranteed from the [[Managing Miscellania]] activity (requires [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] Throne of Miscellania ) **[[File:Firemaking icon.png|link=Firemaking|alt=Firemaking]] 50 [[Firemaking]] [not boostable] for maple logs from the [[Wintertodt]] [[Reward Cart]] (drop chance based on [[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] level). **Completing [[Hunters' Rumours]] with [[File:Hunter icon.png|link=Hunter|alt=Hunter]] 46 [[Hunter]] [not boostable] to receive [[Hunters' loot sack]] (drop chance based on [[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] level) **Using [[Serum 208]] for a 1/128 drop of maple logs from curing [[Afflicted]] (requires [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] Shades of Mort'ton ). *A method to obtain [[willow logs]]: **[[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] 30 [[Woodcutting]] [boostable] to cut [[willow trees]] **1/16 drop from [[Vampyre Juvinates]] (requires starting [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] In Aid of the Myreque ) *A method to obtain [[oak logs]]: **[[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] 15 [[Woodcutting]] to chop [[oak trees]] **1/17.2 drop from sorting [[small salvage]] with [[File:Sailing icon.png|link=Sailing|alt=Sailing]] 15 [[Sailing]], 1/11.6 from [[fishy salvage]] with [[File:Sailing icon.png|link=Sailing|alt=Sailing]] 26 [[Sailing]], 1/5.68 from [[barracuda salvage]] with [[File:Sailing icon.png|link=Sailing|alt=Sailing]] 35 [[Sailing]] **1/16.2 drop from [[Brutus]] ([[File:Attack style icon.png|link=Combat level|alt=Combat level]] 30 ), requiring completion of [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] The Ides of Milk
Enakhra's Lament.start_point: Talk to [[Lazim]] just north-east of the [[Desert Quarry]].

Cabin Fever.name: Cabin Fever
Cabin Fever.description:
It's war on the high seas as Bill Teach tries to make the journey from Port Phasmatys to the pirate enclave of Mos Le'Harmless!
With his crew heading for the hills, he's looking to recruit anyone eager to become a pirate to help him sink the enemy before he ends up swimming home. He's offering a lot
Cabin Fever.items_required: You don't need to bring any items yourself. However, make sure that you have at least '''11''' open inventory spaces for items you will obtain during the quest. A higher amount, like '''14''' open spaces, is recommended however.
Cabin Fever.start_point: Talk to [[Bill Teach]] at [[The Green Ghost]] inn in [[Port Phasmatys]].

Fairytale I - Growing Pains.name: Fairytale I - Growing Pains
Fairytale I - Growing Pains.description: Recently, farmers around Gielinor have been noticing some trouble with their farming patches. They just don't seem to have the same yields as before. What could be the source of this malady?
Fairytale I - Growing Pains.items_required:
*[[Secateurs]] (obtainable in [[Sarah's Farming shop]] north-west of the quest start for 5 coins)
*[[Spade]] (obtainable multiple times during quest)
*[[Ghostspeak amulet]]
*[[Dramen staff]]
*[[Draynor skull]] (obtainable during quest)
*A combination of three items picked for you at random, see [[Fairytale I - Growing Pains#Blessed secateurs|list of potential items]].
Fairytale I - Growing Pains.ironman_concerns:
*Completion of [[Jungle Potion]] (to obtain [[proboscis]] if needed)
*Ironmen who do not have the necessary [[Herblore]] level to clean possible herbs required to bless the [[secateurs]] will need to visit the [[Zahur|herblore shop]] in [[Nardah]] to clean the grimy herbs.
Fairytale I - Growing Pains.start_point: Speak to [[Martin the Master Gardener]] by the pig pen in [[Draynor Village]].

Recipe for Disaster.name: Recipe for Disaster
Recipe for Disaster.description:
The cook in Lumbridge has a surprisingly small number of ingredients on hand in his kitchen, possibly as a result of an endless stream of newcomers with light fingers, taking anything that isn't nailed down from his kitchen.

Luckily, in the past, a kind [[Cook's Assistant]] was willing to help him
Recipe for Disaster.items_required:
'''To start the quest and finish the [[Recipe for Disaster/Another Cook's Quest|first chapter]]:'''
*[[Eye of newt]]
*[[Greenman's ale]]
*[[Rotten tomato]]
*Any [[fruit blast]] excluding a [[premade fr' blast]]
*[[Ashes]]
'''To complete all chapters of the quest:'''
*About 320 [[coins]] ''(or 120 if you have the activated [[Ring of charos]])''
*[[Bowl of water]] ''(an [[empty bowl]] can be bought from the [[Culinaromancer's Chest]])''
*[[Bucket of water]] ''(an [[Bucket|empty bucket]] can be bought from the [[Culinaromancer's Chest]])''
*3 [[buckets of milk]] ''(can be bought from the [[Culinaromancer's Chest]])''
*2 [[eggs]] ''(can be bought from the [[Culinaromancer's Chest]])''
*[[Orange]]
*[[Banana]] ''(obtainable during the quest)''
*Any [[dye]] '''except''' red, yellow, or orange
*[[Spice]] or [[gnome spice]] ''(can be bought from the [[Culinaromancer's Chest]])''
*[[Gloves]] ''([[ice gloves]] are preferred)''
*[[Charcoal]]
*[[Fishing bait]]
*[[Fishbowl]]
*2 [[bread]]
*[[Pestle and mortar]]
*[[Needle]]
*3 [[bronze wire]]s
*[[Cake tin]] ''(can be bought from the [[Culinaromancer's Chest]])''
*4 [[Asgarnian ale]] ''(6 recommended)''
*Any [[cat]] except an [[overgrown cat]] ''(you'll be [[chase|chasing rats]], so a [[wily cat]] is recommended)''
*Several bowls of [[stew]] ''(9 at most)''
*[[Raw chompy]] ''(several recommended in case of burning)''
*[[Raw chicken]]
*[[Raw cod]]
*[[Iron spit]]
*Regular [[logs]]
*[[Tinderbox]]
*[[Ball of wool]]
*[[Ogre bellows]] ''(obtainable during the quest)''
*[[Ogre bow]] and several [[ogre arrows]] ''(10 is enough)''
*2 [[pots of flour]] ''(can be bought from the [[Culinaromancer's Chest]])''
*[[Pot of cornflour]] ''(or raw [[sweetcorn]] and an [[empty pot]])''
*[[Pot of cream]] ''(can be bought from the [[Culinaromancer's Chest]])''
*Any [[axe]]
*Any [[pickaxe]]
*Any [[machete]]
*[[Knife]]
*[[Dramen staff]] and [[Dramen branch|branch]]
*[[M'speak amulet]]
*[[gorilla greegree|Gorilla]], [[ninja greegree|ninja]] and [[zombie greegree]] ''(or 3000 [[coins]] and combat equipment if making the greegrees during the quest)''
*[[Monkey nuts]] ''(or 3 [[coins]])''
*[[Rope]]
Recipe for Disaster.enemies_to_defeat:
*[[Icefiend]] ''(level 13)'' (only if you lack [[ice gloves]])
*5 [[Mudskipper]]s ''(level 30/31)''
*[[Crab]] ''(level 21/23)''
*[[Evil Chicken]] ''(level varies)'' (see below)
*[[Black dragon]] ''(level 227)''
*[[Big Snake]] ''(level 84)''
*[[Zombie monkey]] ''(levels 82/129)''
*[[Monkey guard]] ''(level 167)''
*[[Ninja monkey]] ''(level 86)''
*[[Agrith-Na-Na]] ''(Level 146)''
*[[Flambeed]] ''(level 149)''
*[[Karamel]] ''(level 136)''
*[[Dessourt]] ''(level 121)''
*[[Gelatinnoth Mother]] ''(level 130)''
*[[Culinaromancer]] ''(level 75)''
Recipe for Disaster.start_point: Speak to the [[Cook (Lumbridge)|Cook]] in the kitchen of [[Lumbridge Castle]].

In Aid of the Myreque.name: In Aid of the Myreque
In Aid of the Myreque.description:
After the attack on the Myreque by Vanstrom Klause and with the deaths of both Sani and Harold, Veliaf is counting on you to help. Now that the Myreque freedom fighters' base camp has been revealed, they'll need to move on and set up base somewhere else.

Veliaf has heard of a little known village s
In Aid of the Myreque.items_required:
*A method to damage vampyres, either:
**[[Efaritay's aid]] equipped in combination with any weapon OR
**[[Silver weaponry]], which includes:
***[[Wolfbane dagger]]
***A [[Silver sickle (b)|blessed]] or [[Silver sickle|regular]] silver sickle
***A [[blessed axe]] (obtained during [[Animal Magnetism]])
***[[Silverlight]] (obtained during [[Demon Slayer]]), [[Darklight]] (obtained during Shadow of the Storm), [[Arclight]], or [[Emberlight]]
*1 piece of any [[food]] to enter [[Burgh de Rott]] for the first time
*A [[spade]]
*1-5 [[bucket]]s (5 is '''highly recommended''' to save time)
*Any [[pickaxe]]
*A [[hammer]] ([[Imcando hammer]] works.)
*11 [[plank]]s (Only regular planks will work.)
*44 [[nails]] (42 are obtainable during the quest)
*[[Swamp paste]]
*10 [[raw mackerel]] or 10 [[Snail meat]]s (randomly chosen, but you can manipulate the choice)
*10 [[bronze axe]]s
*4 [[tinderbox]]es
*2 [[steel bar]]s
*[[Coal]]
*[[Soft clay]]
*[[Rope]]
*A [[silver bar]]
*A [[mithril bar]]
*A [[sapphire]]
*On the [[standard spellbook]] for [[Lvl-1 Enchant]]:
**A [[cosmic rune]]
**A [[water rune]]
*Optional to give to [[Ivan Strom]] ('''''Strongly'' recommended if you don't have a high [[combat level]]''' 85+, you will not get the items back):
**A [[steel med helm]]
**A [[steel chainbody]]
**[[Steel platelegs]]
**A [[silver sickle]]
**15 [[stew]] (highest healing), [[salmon]], [[snail]]s, or [[Cooked slimy eel|slimy eel]]s
In Aid of the Myreque.enemies_to_defeat:
*[[Gadderanks]] ''(level 35)'' (safespottable)
*2 [[Vampyre Juvinate]]s ''(level 54)'' (safespottable)
*2 [[Vampyre Juvinate]]s ''(level 75)'' '''or''' 4 level 50s depending on route taken
In Aid of the Myreque.ironman_concerns: *Obtaining 2 steel bars: **[[File:Smithing icon.png|link=Smithing|alt=Smithing]] 30 [[Smithing]] to smith one from 2 [[coal]] and 1 [[iron ore]] **Completing [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] [[Daddy's Home]] rewards 5 steel bars **1/21.33 drop from [[black knight|black knights]] ([[File:Attack style icon.png|link=Combat level|alt=Combat level]] 33 ) **1/21.33 drop from [[moss giants]] ([[File:Attack style icon.png|link=Combat level|alt=Combat level]] 42 ) *Obtaining a mithril bar: **[[File:Smithing icon.png|link=Smithing|alt=Smithing]] 50 [[Smithing]] [boostable] to smith one **1/21.33 drop from [[chaos dwarves]] ([[File:Attack style icon.png|link=Combat level|alt=Combat level]] 48 ) in the [[Taverley Dungeon]] *Obtaining steel equipment to give to Ivan Strom **The steel med helm, chainbody and platelegs can all be bought from [[Blair's Armour]] in [[Shayzien]] *Raw mackerel can be bought from [[Frankie's Fishing Emporium]] in [[Port Piscarilius]]. Note that you can reroll the random requirement to avoid needing to kill snails.
In Aid of the Myreque.start_point: Go to [[Canifis]] and down the ladder behind the pub there, through the secret wall and squeeze past the stalagmite on the east side of the cave. Speak to [[Veliaf Hurtz]].

A Soul's Bane.name: A Soul's Bane
A Soul's Bane.description: A rift in the ground has opened up releasing all manner of strange monsters into the lands near the Digsite. A sorrowful mother waits at the top of the rift for her long lost son, who has been missing for 25 years. You can journey down into the rift for an adventure full of twisted monsters all crea
A Soul's Bane.enemies_to_defeat:
*7-8 [[angry unicorn]]s, [[angry bear]]s, [[angry giant rat]]s, and/or [[angry goblin]]s
*5 [[fear reaper]]s ''(level 42)''
*6 [[confusion beast]]s ''(level 43)'' — must tank them, cannot kill
*5 [[hopeless creature]]s ''(level 40)''
*3 parts of [[Tolna]] ''(level 46)''
A Soul's Bane.start_point: Go east of [[Varrock]], through the gate by the [[Earth Altar]] and just south of there, speak to [[Launa]].

Rag and Bone Man I.name: Rag and Bone Man I
Rag and Bone Man I.description: Everybody needs some body, and the Odd Old Man knows exactly which bits of those bodies he needs. Can you help him finish his extensive and extremely creepy collection?
Rag and Bone Man I.items_required:
*8 [[coins]] for 8 [[pot of vinegar|pots of vinegar]] (bring 68 coins if walking to and from [[Karamja]] for boat fare)
*8 [[pot]]s
*8 [[logs]] of any type (or an [[axe]] to obtain them)
*[[Tinderbox]]
Rag and Bone Man I.start_point: Speak to the [[Odd Old Man]], located at the end of the northern path in central [[Silvarea]].

Swan Song.name: Swan Song
Swan Song.description: Out on the edge of the known world, the Piscatoris Fishing Colony stands on the brink of disaster. Angry sea trolls are emerging from the sea and attacking the colonists, preventing them from harvesting the valuable monkfish that live in those waters. A highly experienced adventurer will be needed t
Swan Song.items_required:
*10 [[mist rune]]s
*10 [[lava rune]]s
*5 [[blood rune]]s
*7 [[bones]] (obtainable during the quest)
*An [[airtight pot]] – created by using a [[pot lid]] on a [[pot]]
**[[Soft clay]] if making your own pot lid during the quest (2 if also making the pot), or a [[pickaxe]] to obtain the clay during the quest
*5 [[iron bar]]s, or
**a [[pickaxe]] (obtainable during the quest) if mining your own iron during quest
*1 set of [[logs]] of any kind, or
**an [[axe]] (obtainable during quest) if chopping your own [[logs]] during quest
*A [[tinderbox]] (obtainable during the quest)
*A [[hammer]] (obtainable during the quest)
*A [[small fishing net]] (obtained during the quest)
*A [[brown apron]] (obtained during the quest)
Swan Song.enemies_to_defeat:
*11 [[Sea troll]]s (1 each of levels 65/87/101 and 8 of level 79)
*[[Sea Troll Queen]] ''(level 170)''
Swan Song.ironman_concerns: Consider purchasing the necessary combination runes from the [[Rewards Guardian (Mage Training Arena)|Rewards Guardian]] in the [[Mage Training Arena]].
Swan Song.start_point: The [[Piscatoris Fishing Colony]]. It can be reached via a boat trip from the river to the west of the [[Tree Gnome Stronghold]].

Royal Trouble.name: Royal Trouble
Royal Trouble.description: It's tough being a regent of a small island kingdom, especially when the [[King Vargas|king]] has declared war while you were adventuring. Something is up in [[Miscellania]] and [[Etceteria]], so you'd better find out what before both kingdoms end up in royal trouble.
Royal Trouble.items_required:
*5 [[coal]] (obtainable during the quest with a [[pickaxe]], which will also be obtainable)
*2 [[rope]] (obtainable during the quest)
*1 [[plank]] (obtainable during the quest)
Royal Trouble.start_point: Go to [[Miscellania]] and speak to [[Advisor Ghrim]] in the castle.

Death to the Dorgeshuun.name: Death to the Dorgeshuun
Death to the Dorgeshuun.description: Humans have been visiting the Dorgeshuun mines for some time now, but no Dorgeshuun has visited the surface since the signing of the Lumbridge-Dorgeshuun treaty. The Dorgeshuun Council fears that the [[Humans Against Monsters|H.A.M.]] group may be planning another attack, and want to send an agent t
Death to the Dorgeshuun.items_required:
*Any [[light source]] ([[lantern]]s are recommended - open light sources such as a [[candle]] can cause [[gas explosion]]s and require a [[tinderbox]])
*2 full sets of [[H.A.M. robes]] (all 7 pieces) (chance to receive robes is significantly higher during the quest)'"`UNIQ--ref-00000007-QINU`"'
*[[Pickaxe]] (if not using a [[games necklace]] to teleport to [[Tears of Guthix (minigame)|Tears of Guthix]] - only usable after you have completed the [[Tears of Guthix]] quest)

Fairytale II - Cure a Queen.name: Fairytale II - Cure a Queen
Fairytale II - Cure a Queen.description:
The Fairy Queen should be awake and back in charge of the Lost City by now, but what is happening in Zanaris? Why hasn't the Fairy Godfather abdicated the throne?

What is happening to all the fairy inhabitants and where has Fairy Nuff gone? Can you rise to the challenge of using the fairy rings to
Fairytale II - Cure a Queen.items_required:
*[[Dramen staff|Dramen]] or [[Lunar staff]]
*[[Vial of water]]
*[[Pestle and mortar]]
*[[Nuff's certificate]] (obtained during quest)
Fairytale II - Cure a Queen.start_point: Speak to [[Martin the Master Gardener]] by the pig pen in [[Draynor Village]].

Lunar Diplomacy.name: Lunar Diplomacy
Lunar Diplomacy.description: Can you make it to Moonclan Island and make peace between the Moonclan and the Fremenniks?
Lunar Diplomacy.items_required:
*A [[tinderbox]]
*A few [[coins]] (1,000 is enough)
*A [[guam leaf]] (common drop from a [[Suqah]] during the quest)
*A [[marrentill]] (common drop from a Suqah during the quest)
*A [[pestle and mortar]]
*A [[hammer]] (or an [[Imcando hammer]])
*A [[needle]] (a [[costume needle]] will also work) and 2 spools of [[thread]].
*Any [[axe]] (bronze axe obtainable during quest)
*Any [[pickaxe]]
*A [[spade]]
*A finished [[bullseye lantern]] ('''NOT [[bullseye lantern (unf)|(unf)]] NOR [[bullseye lantern (empty)|(empty)]]'''. If you cannot obtain one, bring [[Swamp tar]] to a [[lamp oil still]] instead. [[Swamp tar]] can be obtained during the quest.)
*All elemental talismans or their respective tiaras ([[Air talisman|Air]], [[Water talisman|Water]], [[Earth talisman|Earth]], and [[Fire talisman|Fire]], or an [[Elemental talisman]]), or access to the [[Abyss]] or the [[Guardians of the Rift]] minigame
*A [[dramen staff]]
*[[Combat]] equipment
Lunar Diplomacy.start_point: Speak to [[Lokar Searunner]] at the westernmost dock of [[Rellekka]].

The Eyes of Glouphrie.name: The Eyes of Glouphrie
The Eyes of Glouphrie.description: After you foiled a plan to destroy the Grand Tree things have been quiet in the gnome world. Brimstail has found a strange machine, though, built by [[Oaknock]] the Engineer during the Gnomes' Golden Age. What does the weird device do? Why on Gielinor was it built? If you agree to help this bookworm
The Eyes of Glouphrie.items_required:
*[[Bucket of sap]] (If you do not have one, you can use a [[knife]] on any [[Tree#Single canopy|tree]] that looks pointy (evergreen shaped trees) with a [[bucket]] in your inventory)
*[[Mud rune]]
*[[Maple logs]]
*[[Oak logs]]
*[[Hammer]]
*[[Saw]]
*[[Pestle and mortar]]
The Eyes of Glouphrie.enemies_to_defeat: 6 x [[Evil creature]] that each have 1 HP
The Eyes of Glouphrie.ironman_concerns: *A method to obtain [[mud runes]]: **[[File:Runecraft icon.png|link=Runecraft|alt=Runecraft]] 13 [[Runecraft]] to craft them **As a reward from the [[Mage Training Arena]] with [[File:Magic icon.png|link=Magic|alt=Magic]] 33 [[Magic]] **1/100 drop from [[essence implings]] with [[File:Hunter icon.png|link=Hunter|alt=Hunter]] 42 [[Hunter]] *A method to obtain [[maple logs]]: **[[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] 45 [[Woodcutting]] [boostable] to cut [[maple trees]] **Guaranteed from the [[Managing Miscellania]] activity (requires [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] Throne of Miscellania ) **[[File:Firemaking icon.png|link=Firemaking|alt=Firemaking]] 50 [[Firemaking]] [not boostable] for maple logs from the [[Wintertodt]] [[Reward Cart]] (drop chance based on [[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] level). **Completing [[Hunters' Rumours]] with [[File:Hunter icon.png|link=Hunter|alt=Hunter]] 46 [[Hunter]] [not boostable] to receive [[Hunters' loot sack]] (drop chance based on [[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] level) **Using [[Serum 208]] for a 1/128 drop of maple logs from curing [[Afflicted]] (requires [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] Shades of Mort'ton ). *A method to obtain [[oak logs]]: **[[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] 15 [[Woodcutting]] to chop [[oak trees]] **1/17.2 drop from sorting [[small salvage]] with [[File:Sailing icon.png|link=Sailing|alt=Sailing]] 15 [[Sailing]], 1/11.6 from [[fishy salvage]] with [[File:Sailing icon.png|link=Sailing|alt=Sailing]] 26 [[Sailing]], 1/5.68 from [[barracuda salvage]] with [[File:Sailing icon.png|link=Sailing|alt=Sailing]] 35 [[Sailing]] **1/16.2 drop from [[Brutus]] ([[File:Attack style icon.png|link=Combat level|alt=Combat level]] 30 ), requiring completion of [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] The Ides of Milk [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] [[Magic glue]] can be made before starting the quest by grinding the [[mud rune]] with the [[pestle and mortar]] and using it on a [[bucket of sap]], saving inventory space.
The Eyes of Glouphrie.start_point: Speak to [[Brimstail]] in his [[Brimstail's cave|cave]] south-west side of the [[Tree Gnome Stronghold]], directly west of the bank near the [[Stronghold Slayer Cave]].

Darkness of Hallowvale.name: Darkness of Hallowvale
Darkness of Hallowvale.description: Fighting tooth and nail against the vampyre overlord, Drakan, the Myreque have moved their secret base camp to Burgh de Rott. Now the fight starts in earnest! Veliaf needs someone to make their way into the Sanguinesti region, the eastern side of Morytania cut off from the west behind an impenetrabl
Darkness of Hallowvale.items_required:
*8 [[nails]]
*2 [[plank]]s
*[[Hammer]]
*[[Knife]] (obtainable during the quest) or a [[sickle]]
*Means to cast [[Telekinetic Grab]] ([[air rune|air]] and [[law rune]]; both obtainable during the quest)
Darkness of Hallowvale.enemies_to_defeat: None - however, [[Vanstrom_Klause#Vampyre|Vanstrom Klause]] (level 169) needs to be fought until he has attacked you 5 times (whether he hits or misses), and it is very possible to die. However, death can be avoided if you pray protect from melee right after you draw the third sketch.
Darkness of Hallowvale.start_point: Go to [[Burgh de Rott]] and into the basement of the inn there and speak to [[Veliaf Hurtz]].

The Slug Menace.name: The Slug Menace
The Slug Menace.description: The small village of [[Witchaven]] was all but deserted when the [[Fishing Platform]] was built a short ways off the coast. Now, some of the inhabitants of the village have been acting somewhat strangely....
The Slug Menace.items_required:
*[[Commorb]] (can get another by speaking to [[Sir Tiffy]])
*[[Swamp paste]] (not consumed)
*[[Dead sea slug]] (obtainable during the quest)
*At least 5 [[rune essence|rune]]/[[pure essence]] (bring 15–20 in case they shatter; succeeding with 5 is very unlikely)
*[[Chisel]]
*5 open inventory slots
*Access to the [[Air Altar|Air]], [[Water Altar|Water]], [[Earth Altar|Earth]], [[Fire Altar|Fire]], and [[Mind Altar]]s
**[[Elemental talisman]] '''OR''' [[air talisman|Air]], [[water talisman|Water]], [[earth talisman|Earth]], and [[fire talisman|Fire]] [[talisman]]s or [[tiara (disambiguation)|tiara]]s
**[[Mind talisman]] or [[mind tiara|tiara]]
*:'''OR'''
**Travelling through the [[Abyss]] or using a [[Runecraft cape]]
*:'''OR'''
**Access to [[Guardians of the Rift]]
The Slug Menace.start_point: Speak to [[Sir Tiffy Cashien]] in [[Falador Park]].

Elemental Workshop II.name: Elemental Workshop II
Elemental Workshop II.description:
Among the ancient scrolls and papers held at the Dig Site's Examination Centre, scholars have discovered more references to the mysterious elemental ore. Recently they have uncovered a beaten book detailing long-lost uses for this odd material.
Can you decrypt the code that will lead you to the deep
Elemental Workshop II.items_required:
*[[Pickaxe]] (if obtaining elemental ore during quest)
*[[Hammer]]
*2 [[Elemental metal]]s (3 elemental metals if you wish to smith the [[Mind shield]]), or:
**2 [[Elemental ore]] (obtainable during quest) (3 if you wish to smith the mind shield)
**8 [[Coal]] (12 if you wish to smith the mind shield)
*The [[battered key]] from [[Elemental Workshop I]]
Elemental Workshop II.enemies_to_defeat: (If you do not have the elemental metal bars) 2 [[Earth elemental]]s ''(level 35)''
Elemental Workshop II.start_point: Search the bookcases in the [[Exam Centre]]'s library and read the [[beaten book]]

My Arm's Big Adventure.name: My Arm's Big Adventure
My Arm's Big Adventure.description: Trolls love goutweed. They love it so much that they've picked it all, and now they can't get any more. However, in his smelly kitchen in the Troll Stronghold, Burntmeat the cook has heard a rumour that goutweed is growing once again, for anyone who's got the skill to farm it. Can you teach his youn
My Arm's Big Adventure.items_required:
*[[Bucket]] (can be obtained during the quest, south of the cooking pot on [[Death Plateau (location)|Death Plateau]])
*3 [[Ugthanki dung]], or the following materials to gather it:
**[[Red hot sauce]] (obtainable in [[Pollnivneach]]) '''or''' the [[Camulet]] (from [[Enakhra's Lament]] quest)
**[[Spade]]
**3 additional [[bucket]]s
*Several farming supplies, which can be stored by any [[Tool Leprechaun]] and withdrawn from the [[Tool Leprechaun (Larry)|Trollheim Tool Leprechaun]]. He sells all of these '''except''' for supercompost:
**7 [[Supercompost]] (must be ''super''compost; one extra can be used instead of plant cure, making 8 total) Note: Bottomless compost bucket will not work, even one that is containing supercompost.
**[[Spade]] (To give to [[My Arm]] temporarily; he will return it near the end of the quest)
**[[Rake]] (To give to [[My Arm]] temporarily; he will return it near the end of the quest)
**[[Seed dibber]] (To give to [[My Arm]] temporarily; he will return it near the end of the quest. This is still needed even if Barbarian Training is completed)
**[[Plant cure]] (disease can be avoided with an additional supercompost but the plant can get diseased during a cutscene right after planting so plant cure is still recommended)
*[[Climbing boots]] (can be bought from [[Tenzing]] for 12 coins)
My Arm's Big Adventure.start_point: The kitchen, deep in the [[Troll Stronghold (location)|Troll Stronghold]]. Talk to [[Burntmeat]].

Enlightened Journey.name: Enlightened Journey
Enlightened Journey.description: On the peaceful island of Entrana, a lone monk has tired of his life of simplicity and quiet reflection. Auguste wants to boldly write his name in the history books; to craftily defeat the laws of physics; to miraculously sail through the air... preferably not to horribly explode into a ball of flam
Enlightened Journey.items_required:
*TRIP 1 (starting the quest):
**3 unnoted [[papyrus]]
**1 [[ball of wool]]
**A full [[Potatoes|sack of potatoes]] (item is called [[Potatoes(10)]]) (there are potatoes growing in [[Entrana]] in the house east of Auguste, bring another [[empty sack]] if picking here)
**8 [[empty sack]]s – bring 9 sacks if planning to fill one with [[potatoes]] on [[Entrana]].
**1 unlit [[candle]] or [[black candle]] (can be obtained during quest by stealing candles in Entrana church)
**[[Yellow dye]]
**[[Red dye]]
**10 unnoted [[silk]]
**A [[bowl]] (obtainable during quest)
*TRIP 2 (building the third balloon)
**At least 10 normal [[logs]]
**A [[tinderbox]]
**12 unnoted [[willow branch]]es (buy from the [[Grand Exchange]], or plant a [[willow sapling]] and wait 4 hours.)
***Note: The player receives a [[Auguste's sapling|willow sapling]] during the quest, although planting one beforehand may be faster. You will also receive a basket of apples to pay to protect the tree.
***[[Secateurs]] (only if planting the tree) (can also be exchanged from a [[Tool Leprechaun]] if placed there before)
***A [[rake]] (only if planting the tree) (can also be exchanged from a [[Tool Leprechaun]] if placed there before)
***A [[spade]] (only if planting the tree) (can also be exchanged from a [[Tool Leprechaun]] if placed there before)
Enlightened Journey.ironman_concerns: * [[File:Ironman chat badge.png]] [[File:Farming icon.png|link=Farming|alt=Farming]] 30 [[Farming]] [boostable] and a minimum of 4 hours required to grow a [[Willow Tree (Farming)|willow tree]], plus another hour for the willow branches to grow. * [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] The player visits [[Entrana]]
Enlightened Journey.start_point: Speak to [[Auguste]] on the island of [[Entrana]].

Eagles' Peak.name: Eagles' Peak
Eagles' Peak.description:
The Ardougne Zoo is always looking for new specimens, but one of their anticipated new attractions - a ferret from the north - has been proving elusive.

One of the Zoo's more eccentric freelance specimen hunters is reported to be on the case, but after setting out for the far western reaches, somew
Eagles' Peak.ironman_concerns: [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] You need to unequip your headgear and cape and have 6 empty inventory slots in addition to that.
Eagles' Peak.start_point: Speak to [[Charlie]], one of the keepers at [[Ardougne Zoo]].

Animal Magnetism.name: Animal Magnetism
Animal Magnetism.description: Draynor Manor has a new resident and, adventurers rejoice, she is a damsel in distress. Far from being Sleeping Beauty, she finds the Manor's beds not to her liking and is suffering from insomniac nights. If you fancy yourself able to aid Ava in her search for a good night's sleep, who knows what th
Animal Magnetism.items_required:
*[[Mithril axe]] (required to obtain the [[blessed axe]]; no other type of axe will do)
*5 [[iron bar]]s unnoted
*[[Ghostspeak amulet]] ([[Morytania legs]] will not work)
*20 [[ecto-token]]s (or 4 [[bones]], 4 [[bucket]]s, and 4 [[pot]]s to gain the tokens)
*[[Hammer]]
*[[Hard leather]]
*[[Holy symbol]]
*[[Polished buttons]]
Animal Magnetism.ironman_concerns: * [[Holy symbol]] ([[File:Prayer icon.png|link=Prayer|alt=Prayer]] 31 [[Prayer]] [not boostable]) *[[Mithril axe]] can be purchased at [[Lunami's Axe Shop]] in [[Auburnvale]] *A method of obtaining iron bars: **[[File:Mining icon.png|link=Mining|alt=Mining]] 15 [[Mining]] and [[File:Smithing icon.png|link=Smithing|alt=Smithing]] 15 [[Smithing]] if smelting [[iron ore]] **Spawn in [[Thurid's Brain Buckets]] on the [[Sunset Coast]] in [[Varlamore]] (requires [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] [[Children of the Sun]] ) **Spawn in level 18 [[Wilderness]] north-west of the [[Ferox Enclave]], next to the bears west of the Graveyard of Shadows. Accessible via [[File:Minigame icon.png|link=Minigames|alt=Minigames]] Clan Wars [[Minigame Teleport]] **1/42.67 drop from [[dwarf|dwarves]] ([[File:Attack style icon.png|link=Combat level|alt=Combat level]] 10 ) in the mines under Falador **1/34.42 drop from sorting [[small salvage]] with [[File:Sailing icon.png|link=Sailing|alt=Sailing]] 15 [[Sailing]]

Contact!.name: Contact!
Contact!.description: The High Priest of Icthlarin has problems again - this time with the inhabitants of Menaphos. Sophanem is recovering from the plague, yet most of its citizens are still trapped on the other side of the river. Diplomacy has failed and subterfuge beckons. Clearly, this is the sort of job that a shifty
Contact!.items_required:
*A [[Light sources|light source]] ([[Kandarin headgear]], [[Firemaking cape]], [[Abyssal lantern]], or a [[bruma torch]] are strongly recommended as they can't be extinguished by traps in the [[Sophanem Dungeon|dungeon]]). If you don't have one, the [[Sophanem guard]] under the temple of the lesser deities will sell you an [[unlit torch]] for 200 [[coins]].
*A [[tinderbox]] (unless you have a non extinguishable light source such as [[Kandarin headgear]], [[Firemaking cape]], [[Abyssal lantern]], or a [[bruma torch]]). If you don't have one, the [[Sophanem guard]] will sell you one for 50 coins.
Contact!.enemies_to_defeat:
* [[Giant Scarab]] ''(level 191)''
* Summoned by the Giant Scarab:
** [[Locust rider]] ''(level 68)'' - Ranged and melee
** [[Scarab Mage]]s ''(level 119 & 66)''
Contact!.start_point: Speak to the [[High Priest (Sophanem)|High Priest of Icthlarin]] in [[Sophanem]].

Cold War.name: Cold War
Cold War.description:
At the Ardougne Zoo, one of the zookeepers has become a bit preoccupied by penguins. In fact, he's downright paranoid about them. He needs your help to observe their habits on the iceberg in the Lunar Sea.

What begins as a peaceful bird-watching expedition opens a new world of espionage and bad mus
Cold War.items_required:
*10 [[oak plank]]s
*10 [[steel nails]]
*[[Hammer]]
*[[Spade]]
*[[Clockwork]] ''or'' a [[steel bar]] to make one
*[[Plank]]
*[[Silk]]
*[[Raw cod]] ''or'' the [[ring of charos (a)]]
*[[Swamp tar]]
*5 [[feather]]s (only regular chicken feathers work)
*[[Mahogany plank]]
*[[Leather]]
*[[Cowbell]] (obtainable only during quest)
Cold War.ironman_concerns: *A method to obtain a [[mahogany plank]] **[[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] 50 [[Woodcutting]] [boostable] to cut [[mahogany trees]] **1/12.8 drop from the [[gangsters]] in the [[Intelligence Gathering]] activity **1/24.4 drop from salvaging [[Mercenary shipwreck]]s ([[File:Sailing icon.png|link=Sailing|alt=Sailing]] 73 [[Sailing]]) **A possible reward from completing [[Hunters' Rumours]], with the chance scaling by Woodcutting level

The Fremennik Isles.name: The Fremennik Isles
The Fremennik Isles.description:
Sibling rivalry can get messy - more so when the siblings are Fremennik princes with a point to prove. The islands of Jatizso and Neitiznot were set up by two brothers who hated each other, and they have been at loggerheads ever since. But now there is a new threat from trolls in the north...

Can y
The Fremennik Isles.items_required: *A [[raw tuna]] (obtainable during quest, with 35 fishing or bought from the shop) *Either 8 [[tin ore]]s, 7 pieces of [[coal]], or 6 [[mithril ore]]s (depends on your [[Mining]] level; level 1 will require tin ore, 2-54 will require coal, and 55-99 require mithril ore). '''Note:''' The ores can be noted. *9 [[rope]]s (obtainable during quest with [[File:Crafting icon.png|link=Crafting|alt=Crafting]] 30 [[Crafting]]) **If the [[Neitiznot shield]] is already purchased, only 8 ropes are required. *A [[knife]] *8 [[split log]]s (obtainable during the quest with 56 Woodcutting and an axe) *a [[Neitiznot shield]] (or 56 Woodcutting, a [[hammer]] and a [[bronze nail]]) *[[Yak-hide armour (top)|Yak-hide armour top]] and [[Yak-hide armour (legs)|legs]] (or 46 Crafting, a [[needle]] and two [[thread]], or a [[costume needle]]) *5000 [[coins]] (optional) *[[Silly jester costume]] (obtained during quest)
The Fremennik Isles.ironman_concerns: *[[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] 56 [[Woodcutting]] Required to make a Neitiznot shield with a [[hammer]] and one [[bronze nail]] *[[File:Crafting icon.png|link=Crafting|alt=Crafting]] 46 [[Crafting]] Required to make yak-hide armour with a [[needle]] and a [[thread]] *An [[axe]] [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] The player will need to hand 8 items in at once.
The Fremennik Isles.start_point: Speak to [[Mord Gunnars]] at the northernmost dock of [[Rellekka]].

The Great Brain Robbery.name: The Great Brain Robbery
The Great Brain Robbery.description: The peaceful contemplation of the monks of [[Harmony Island]] has been disturbed by such rude interruptions as an invasion of zombie pirates and the theft of their brains! The evil zombie pirates have returned with a new scheme, and it's up to you to try to re-mind these poor priests before the sini
The Great Brain Robbery.items_required:
*Equipment to breathe underwater:
**A [[fishbowl helmet]] and a [[diving apparatus]] (reobtainable from [[Murphy]] in [[Port Khazard]] if you've lost them)
**OR a [[Medallion of the deep]] (counts as both helmet and apparatus)
*10 [[wooden cat]]s – partial completion of The Great Brain Robbery is required to make these yourself, however they are tradeable and available at the [[Grand Exchange]]
**4 [[oak plank]]s, a [[hammer]], and a [[saw]] (if you are building your own [[crafting table 1|crafting table]] and do not already have one; build in the [[Clockmaking space]] of the [[Workshop]])
**10 [[plank]]s (if making your own wooden cats)
**10 pieces of fur: [[fur]], [[bear fur]], or [[grey wolf fur]] (if making your own wooden cats) ''(wolf fur can be obtained during quest by killing [[Canifis townsfolk]]. [[Baraek]] sells them for 20 gold each. Furs from [[Hunter]] monsters will not work)''
*A [[hammer]] (Note: [[Imcando hammer]] does not work)
*About 100 [[nails]] ''(38 are needed, but some—especially [[Bronze nails|bronze]] or [[Iron nails|iron]]—may bend)''
*8 normal [[plank]]s (18 if making the [[wooden cat]]s instead of buying them from the [[Grand Exchange]])
*[[Holy symbol]]
*[[Ring of charos]] ''(if lost, can be re-obtained during quest by pickpocketing [[Fenkenstrain]])''

What Lies Below.name: What Lies Below
What Lies Below.description:
A travelling trader has been stranded south of Varrock after his cart was attacked by local outlaws. He needs the help of a hero to recover his lost documents, but that's not all...

The Varrock Palace Secret Guard (VPSG) are on the trail of treacherous dealings with the Monks of Zamorak. Will you b
What Lies Below.items_required:
*[[Empty bowl]]
*15 [[chaos rune]]s
*Access to the [[Chaos Altar]] ([[Chaos talisman]] or [[Chaos tiara|tiara]], [[Catalytic talisman]] or [[Catalytic tiara|tiara]], or travelling through the [[Abyss]] or Temple of the Eye)

Olaf's Quest.name: Olaf's Quest
Olaf's Quest.description: Poor Olaf Hradson. He has to live up to such expectations. His ancestors sailed all over the world, and now he has to go out and prove himself their equal. If only he could read a map... If only he could sail... If only he hadn't crashed! Can you help a depressed and soggy Fremennik regain his pride
Olaf's Quest.items_required:
*Any [[woodcutting]] [[axe]]
*[[Tinderbox]]
*[[Spade]]
*6 [[Rope]]s (obtainable during the quest)
*4 free [[inventory]] slots (10 if not bringing your own ropes)
Olaf's Quest.start_point: Speak to [[Olaf Hradson]] by the isthmus leading to the [[Rellekka Hunter area]].

Another Slice of H.A.M..name: Another Slice of H.A.M.
Another Slice of H.A.M..description: The dwarves are building a new underground rail link between their city of Keldagrim and the cave goblins' Dorgesh-Kaan, but work is being held up by the discovery of ancient goblin artefacts, and they need the help of a qualified archaeologist. Helping them out should be simple enough - unless you
Another Slice of H.A.M..items_required:
*A [[light source]]
*[[Trowel]] (obtained during the quest)
*[[Specimen brush]] (obtained during the quest)
*Magic or Ranged [[equipment|gear]] for enemy fight ''(cannot use [[Melee]] on them)''
Another Slice of H.A.M..start_point: Go to [[Ur-tag]]'s house in [[Dorgesh-Kaan]]. (Can be reached either via Kazgar from the Lumbridge cellar or via Dartog from the water mill by telling either NPC to take the player to the mines. Afterwards, go south past the sword icon and enter the door.)

Dream Mentor.name: Dream Mentor
Dream Mentor.description: Gielinor is not the safest place to live - dragons, trolls, demons... dangerous and despicable monsters of all sorts roam free. In this quest, you must help a fellow adventurer that doesn't do so well against these threats. With perseverance and the magical help of the Moon Clan, there should be no
Dream Mentor.items_required:
*A [[seal of passage]]
*3 different kinds of [[food]]: 6 of 1 type and 7 of 2 others for a total of 20 pieces (Sacks of [[cabbages]], [[potatoes]], and [[onions]] can be used as they each contain 10 piece of their respective [[food]] and only require a total of 3 inventory slots. Fruit baskets also work.)
*[[Goutweed]]
*An [[astral rune]]
*A [[tinderbox]]
*A [[hammer]]
*A [[pestle and mortar]]
*A good [[weapon]] ''([[trident of the seas]] recommended because they have no magic defence)'', good [[armour]], good food such as [[shark]]s, and [[super potion set]]s.
Dream Mentor.ironman_concerns: [[File:Hardcore ironman chat badge.png]] '''Note''': As opposed to the dream world in Lunar Diplomacy, the dream world in Dream Mentor is not safe and dying here is the same as dying in the rest of Gielinor: you will lose your hardcore status. The lectern is the '''only''' way to leave, as no other teleports will work (including the ring of life).
Dream Mentor.start_point: Talk to the [[Cyrisus|fallen man]] in the small cave in the [[Lunar Isle mine]].

Grim Tales.name: Grim Tales
Grim Tales.description:
"Once upon a time in a land far, far away..." Sylas, a collector of items both wondrous and unusual seeks out the latest additions to his trove with a somewhat unusual prize for the finder.

Terrors and towers await; fierce battles with giant beasts; striding boldly to rescue a dwarf in distress! No
Grim Tales.items_required:
*2 [[Tarromin potion (unf)]] ''or'' 2 [[vials of water]] and 2 [[tarromin]]s
*[[Seed dibber]] ([[Tool leprechaun|leprechaun]] is nearby). Not needed if the [[Farming]] portion of the [[Barbarian Training]] [[miniquest]] is completed
*[[Watering can]] with at least 1 dose (can also be stored with the leprechaun)
*Any [[axe]]
*[[Leather gloves]] (obtainable during quest, optional if willing to take 17 damage)
*High healing [[food]]
*Good [[armour]] and a [[weapon]] ([[Ranged]] or [[Magic equipment]] recommended if you have low melee levels)
Grim Tales.start_point: Talk to [[Sylas]] in [[Taverley]], just south of the [[Portal (Player-owned house)|house portal]].

King's Ransom.name: King's Ransom
King's Ransom.description:
Life has been quiet at the Sinclair Mansion since you discovered the murderer... too quiet. So quiet, in fact, that the family has gone!

Suspicions are running high that the Sinclairs' recent departure is tied to the disappearance of King Arthur and his knights. But time is running short and clues
King's Ransom.items_required:
*[[Granite]] (any size)
*[[Telekinetic Grab]] runes (1 [[air rune]] and 1 [[law rune]]) (obtainable during the quest)
*Full [[black armour]] ([[black full helm]], [[Black platebody|platebody]], and [[Black platelegs|platelegs]]/[[Black plateskirt|plateskirt]])—'''Note:''' Elite and trimmed versions of black armour will work. (h) versions will '''not''' work
*A [[bronze med helm]] (can be purchased from [[Peksa]]'s [[Helmet Shop]] in the [[Barbarian Village]], or picked up inside [[Draynor Manor]] near the exit)
*An [[iron chainbody]] (Both the [[iron chainbody]] and the [[bronze med helm]] can be bought upstairs in the [[Warrior Guild Armoury]]. If you can't access the [[Warriors' Guild]] you can get an [[Iron Chainbody]] from [[Wayne's Chains]] in Southern [[Falador]], [[Horvik's Armour Shop]] in central [[Varrock]], or the [[Grand Exchange]])
*[[Animate rock scroll]] (obtainable during the quest). Note: if you have it in your bank, either destroy it or take it with you. You won't be able to receive another from [[Wizard Cromperty]] otherwise.
King's Ransom.ironman_concerns: *[[File:Mining icon.png|link=Mining|alt=Mining]] 45 [[Mining]] to mine [[granite]] *Obtaining black equipment: **[[Black full helm]] may be purchased from [[Valaine's Shop of Champions]] at the [[Champions Guild]] for 1,372 **[[Black platebody]] may be purchased from [[Horvik's Armour Shop]] in [[Varrock]] for 3,840 **[[Black platelegs]] may be purchased from [[Valaine's Shop of Champions]] at the [[Champions Guild]] for 2,496

Shilo Village.name: Shilo Village
Shilo Village.description: A foul mist and a plague of undead have overtaken Shilo Village. Can you stop the queen of the dead and save the village?
Shilo Village.items_required:
* [[Spade]] (can be purchased at the [[Jiminua's Jungle Store|store]] north of [[Tai Bwo Wannai]] for 4 [[coins]])
* A lit [[torch]] (bruma works), [[candle]] or [[black candle]] (an [[unlit torch]] and [[tinderbox]] can be purchased at the [[Jiminua's Jungle Store|store]] north of [[Tai Bwo Wannai]] for a total of 7 [[coins]])
**'''you will not get this item back!'''
**[[Lanterns]] or [[Firemaking cape]] don't work
* [[Rope]] (can be purchased at the [[Jiminua's Jungle Store|store]] north of [[Tai Bwo Wannai]] for 27 [[coins]])
* [[Bronze wire]]
** [[Bronze bar]] and [[hammer]] to create one (both can be purchased at the [[Jiminua's Jungle Store|store]] north of [[Tai Bwo Wannai]] for a total of 13 [[coins]], there is an [[Anvil]] nearby)
* [[Chisel]] (can be purchased at the [[Jiminua's Jungle Store|store]] north of [[Tai Bwo Wannai]] for 1 [[coins]])
* 3 regular [[bones]] (obtainable during the quest)
Shilo Village.ironman_concerns: [[File:Smithing icon.png|link=Smithing|alt=Smithing]] 4 [[Smithing]] [boostable] (to make a [[bronze wire]])
Shilo Village.start_point: Talk to [[Mosol Rei]] outside of [[Shilo Village (location)|Shilo Village]] in the south part of [[Karamja]].

Biohazard.name: Biohazard
Biohazard.description:
This is the second part of an ongoing adventure. Help Elena discover the truth about the infamous Ardougne plague.

Smuggle test samples across Ardougne to Elena's old mentor.
Biohazard.items_required:
* [[Priest gown top]] and [[Priest gown bottom|bottom]] (can be obtained for free during the quest)
* [[Gas mask]] (can be obtained for free from the cupboard in [[Edmond]] and [[Alrena]]'s house in [[East Ardougne]])
Biohazard.start_point: Talk to [[Elena]] in her house in [[East Ardougne]].

Alfred Grimhand's Barcrawl.name: Alfred Grimhand's Barcrawl
Alfred Grimhand's Barcrawl.description: Go to bars and buy alcohol.
Alfred Grimhand's Barcrawl.items_required: *208 [[coins]] to buy all the required alcohol
Alfred Grimhand's Barcrawl.start_point: Speak to the [[barbarian guard]] at the entrance of the Barbarian Agility Training Course.

Enter the Abyss.name: Enter the Abyss
Enter the Abyss.description: Help the [[Zamorak Magical Institute]] access the [[rune essence mine]]s so that they can craft their own runes, and gain access to [[The Abyss]] in exchange.
Enter the Abyss.ironman_concerns: '''Warning:''' [[File:Hardcore_ironman_chat_badge.png]] Hardcore Ironmen should be extremely cautious as the miniquest is started by travelling to level 4-7 [[Wilderness]], with a chance of getting killed by other players.
Enter the Abyss.start_point: Speak to the [[Mage of Zamorak]] at the end of the [[River Lum]] in the [[Wilderness]] (Roams levels 4-7).

The General's Shadow.name: The General's Shadow
The General's Shadow.description: Assist General Khazard and relay information from his scouts across Gielinor back to him.
The General's Shadow.items_required:
*40 coins (70 coins if using boat to Karamja)
*[[Ghostspeak amulet]] (Morytania legs will not work)
*All pieces of the [[Ghostly robes]] set
*A [[ring of visibility]]
The General's Shadow.start_point: Speak to the ghost of [[General Khazard]] roaming around the woods south-east of [[Rellekka]].

Barbarian Training.name: Barbarian Training
Barbarian Training.description: Otto Godblessed has been researching his history and training people in the ways of his ancestors, and now he's ready to teach you. From his hut on the banks of the lake at the top of Baxtorian Falls, you'll go Fishing with your bare hands, mix fish eggs into your potions, wield one-handed spears an
Barbarian Training.items_required:
*Farming:
** Any [[seed]] that can be planted with a [[seed dibber]] (such as [[Allotment patch|allotment]] and [[Herb patch|herb]] seeds) and a free Farming patch for it (part 1)
** Any [[sapling]] and a free Farming patch for it (part 2)
** A [[spade]] (part 2)
** An [[axe]] or 200 [[coins]] (part 2, to clear a required patch if it is occupied)
*Firemaking:
**Any [[bow]] ''except'' [[Twisted bow|twisted]], [[Cursed goblin bow|cursed]], [[Dark bow|dark]], [[Crystal bow|crystal]], [[ogre bow|ogre]], [[Rain bow|rain]], or the [[signed oak bow]] (part 1)
**[[Oak logs]] (can get some oak logs north of [[Otto's house]], down a hill) (part 1)
**A [[tinderbox]] (part 2)
**An [[axe]] (part 2)
*Fishing:
**Some [[fishing bait]] or [[feather]]s
**A [[knife]]
*Smithing:
**[[Hammer]]
**Two metal [[bars]] of a level you can smith
**Two [[logs]] corresponding to the type of metal bars you are using. [[bronze bar|Bronze]], [[iron bar|iron]], [[steel bar|steel]], [[mithril bar|mithril]], [[adamantite bar|adamantite]], and [[runite bar|runite bars]] require [[logs|normal]], [[Oak logs|oak]], [[Willow logs|willow]], [[Maple logs|maple]], [[Yew logs|yew]], or [[magic logs]], respectively.
*Herblore:
**A normal [[attack potion]] (2 doses)
**[[Roe]] (can be obtained during Barbarian Fishing)
**A [[knife]] for [[roe]] or [[caviar]]
Barbarian Training.start_point: Speak to [[Otto Godblessed]] in [[Otto's Grotto|his grotto]] at the lake atop [[Baxtorian Falls]].

Skippy and the Mogres.name: Skippy and the Mogres
Skippy and the Mogres.description: Help a drunken man haunted by the curse of the Mogres.
Skippy and the Mogres.items_required:
*[[Bucket of water]]
*[[Nettle tea]]
**[[Nettle-water]]
***[[Bowl of water]]
***[[Nettles]] (more than one with low Cooking level, as it can fail due to over boiling)
****[[Gloves]] to pick the nettles ([[leather gloves]] are obtainable during the quest)
*[[Hangover cure]] or the ingredients to make one:
**[[Chocolate dust]]
***[[Chocolate bar]]
***[[Knife]] or [[pestle and mortar]]
**[[Bucket of milk]]
**[[Snape grass]]
Skippy and the Mogres.start_point: Speak to [[Skippy]] located south-east of the town of [[Rimmington]].

Curse of the Empty Lord.name: Curse of the Empty Lord
Curse of the Empty Lord.description: Follow the sad tale of woe from one cursed soul to the next and learn more of Zamorak's betrayal of Zaros.
Curse of the Empty Lord.items_required:
*[[Ring of visibility]]
*[[Ghostspeak amulet]] ([[Morytania legs 2]] or higher may be used instead, except when otherwise noted)
*Some players may need a [[knife]] or [[slash weapon]]
Curse of the Empty Lord.ironman_concerns:
* [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] You may need to visit [[Entrana]]
* [[File:Hardcore ironman chat badge.png]] You will need to go into the [[Wilderness]]. To make this somewhat safer you can also do this in [[F2P]] worlds.
Curse of the Empty Lord.start_point: Talk to the mysterious ghost ([[Valdez]]) near [[Glarial's Tomb]].

Tower of Life.name: Tower of Life
Tower of Life.description: The construction of a mysterious tower is almost complete, but the builders working on it have gone on strike. Losing patience, the alchemists that hired them are looking for anybody to help them. When the world of magic and the world of logic combine you can expect dangerous results, but a true adv
Tower of Life.items_required:
*[[Hammer]] (or [[Imcando hammer]])
*[[Saw]] (or [[crystal saw]])
*[[Beer]] (must be a regular beer - other beers do not work.)
Tower of Life.start_point: Talk to [[Effigy]] at the [[Tower of Life (building)|Tower of Life]] just east of the [[Ardougne Monastery]].

Rag and Bone Man II.name: Rag and Bone Man II
Rag and Bone Man II.description: An archeological bone gathering quest. Requires the start and/or completion of multiple quests. Lots of creatures and animals necessary to be scavenged for specific bones, that will then need to be cleaned and given to the [[Odd Old Man]] for his marvelous collection of bone remains.
Rag and Bone Man II.items_required: *27 [[pot]]s *27 [[jugs of vinegar]] (bought from [[Fortunato]] for 27 coins) *27 sets of [[logs]] (any logs - normal logs can be obtained on the path just below the [[Odd Old Man]]'s hut if you bring an [[axe]]). *[[Tinderbox]] *[[Light source]] *[[Dusty key]] (if under [[File:Agility icon.png|link=Agility|alt=Agility]] 70 [[Agility]]) *[[Rope]] *Combat gear to kill [[zogre]]s (an [[ogre composite bow]] with [[brutal arrow]]s or runes for [[Crumble Undead]] casts is advised) '''Slayer equipment:''' *[[Ice cooler]]s *[[Fishing explosive]]s *[[Mirror shield]] or [[V's shield]] (unless using a workaround - see guide for how to get around using this)
Rag and Bone Man II.start_point: Speak to the [[Odd Old Man]], located at the end of the northern path in central [[Silvarea]].

Land of the Goblins.name: Land of the Goblins
Land of the Goblins.description: The most ancient goblin legends speak of Yu'biusk, the far-off land that saw the origin of the goblin race. Now, one goblin has had a vision of this land and believes it is Zanik's destiny to lead all goblins to live there in peace and safety - but Zanik will not be able to realise her destiny witho
Land of the Goblins.items_required:
* A [[Light sources#Light sources|light source]]
* [[Toadflax potion (unf)]]
* [[Goblin mail]] (obtainable during quest)
** 6 goblin mails are recommended
* [[Yellow dye]]
* [[Blue dye]]
* [[Orange dye]]
* [[Purple dye]]
* [[Black dye]] ([[black mushroom]] obtainable during quest)
** An empty [[vial]] and a [[pestle and mortar]] if making the black dye during the quest
* [[Fishing rod]] ([[oily fishing rod]] will not work)
* [[Raw slimy eel]]
* 5 [[coins]]
Land of the Goblins.ironman_concerns: * [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] The player needs to unequip all items in addition to carrying the required items.

While Guthix Sleeps.name: While Guthix Sleeps
While Guthix Sleeps.description:
This Grandmaster quest sees you continue the Mahjarrat storyline, hunting down one of the most powerful artefacts in existence while ensuring it doesn't fall into the wrong hands.

This artefact is known by many names – most recently, the Stone of Jas. It has shaped the course of Gielinor’s history,
While Guthix Sleeps.items_required:
* [[Ring of charos(a)]] or 500 coins
* [[Knife]]
* [[Seed dibber]] (only if Barbarian Farming Training has not been completed)
* [[Lantern lens]]
* [[Unpowered orb]]
* [[Bronze med helm]]
* [[Iron chainbody]]
* [[Sapphire lantern]]
* Runes to cast the [[NPC Contact]] spell four times (8 [[air rune]]s, 4 [[astral rune]], 4 [[cosmic rune]])
* Runes for any charge orb spell (30 runes of a chosen element and 3 [[cosmic rune]]s)
* Equipment for two different combat styles
* Runes for the following spells: multiple casts of all four elemental spells, [[Snare]]/[[Entangle]], [[Weaken]]/[[Enfeeble]], [[Telekinetic Grab]], and [[Low Level Alchemy|Low]]/[[High Level Alchemy]]; ([[air rune|Air]], [[water rune|Water]], [[earth rune|Earth]], [[fire rune|Fire]], [[body rune|Body]]/[[soul rune|Soul]], [[nature rune|Nature]], [[law rune|Law]], and the highest of [[mind rune|Mind]]/[[chaos rune|Chaos]]/[[death rune|Death]]/[[blood rune|Blood]]/[[wrath rune|Wrath]] that you can use to cast all four elemental spells)
'''Obtainable during quest:'''
* [[Mort myre fungus]]
* 1 [[log]] of any type except redwood (you can obtain one using a bronze axe next to the [[Hunting Expert]], but you must not already have an axe in your inventory (a [[Zombie axe]] counts, even though it can not be used to cut down trees)
* [[Pink dye]] (or 20 coins)
* [[Snapdragon seed]]
* [[Papyrus]] and [[charcoal]]
* [[Restore potion]]
* One of each of the following runes: [[air rune|air]], [[water rune|water]], [[earth rune|earth]], [[fire rune|fire]], [[mind rune|mind]], [[law rune|law]], [[death rune|death]]
While Guthix Sleeps.enemies_to_defeat:
* 2 [[Assassin (While Guthix Sleeps)|Assassins]] ''(level 167)''
* 2 [[Mercenary axeman|Mercenary axemen]] ''(level 131)''
* [[Mercenary mage]] ''(level 112)''
* 3 [[Elite Black Knight]]s ''(level 138)''
* [[Surok Magis]] ''(level 265)''
* [[Balance Elemental]] ''(level 524)''
* 2 [[Tormented Demon]]s ''(level 450)''

Zogre Flesh Eaters.name: Zogre Flesh Eaters
Zogre Flesh Eaters.description:
Run for your lives...the zombie ogres are here for your flesh! An ogre ceremonial dance area, used to communicate with ancient ogrish ancestors, has been overrun with diseased undead ogres.

How could something like this have happened? And what sort of rewards could an enterprising adventurer gain f
Zogre Flesh Eaters.items_required:
* 5 free inventory spaces are needed to receive items near the start of the quest
* [[Knife]] (obtainable during quest)
* A [[comp ogre bow]] (not needed if using [[Crumble Undead]]  in final battle) '''or''':
** [[Wolf bones]]
** [[Achey logs]] (obtainable during quest)
** [[Axe]] of your [[Woodcutting]] level
** [[Bow string]]
* [[Brutal arrows]] (not needed if using [[Crumble Undead]] in final battle) '''or''':
** [[Achey logs]] (obtainable during quest)
** [[Nails]] (the best you can fletch into brutal arrows)
** [[Hammer]]
** [[Feather]]s (4 per 1 brutal arrow)
Zogre Flesh Eaters.start_point: Speak to [[Grish]] in [[Jiggig]], just south of [[Castle Wars]].

Lair of Tarn Razorlor.name: Lair of Tarn Razorlor
Lair of Tarn Razorlor.description: Explore the lair of [[Tarn Razorlor]] within the Haunted Mine and obtain an upgrade to the salve amulet.
Lair of Tarn Razorlor.items_required: *100 [[coins]] required each time you use the banker [[Odovacar]] (optional)
Lair of Tarn Razorlor.enemies_to_defeat: Level 69 mutant [[Tarn]], followed by a level 69 ghost [[Tarn]]
Lair of Tarn Razorlor.start_point: Enter the Lair of Tarn Razorlor.

Architectural Alliance.name: Architectural Alliance
