Architectural Alliance.description: Prove yourself to the five houses of Great Kourend and gain the support of several architects to rebuild the statue of King Rada I.
Architectural Alliance.items_required: Various items depending on the favour activities done
Architectural Alliance.start_point: Speak to [[Hosa]] by the [[Statue (King Rada I)|statue]] outside [[Kourend Castle]].

Monkey Madness II.name: Monkey Madness II
Monkey Madness II.description:
Glough, the war criminal set on eradicating humans and overthrowing Gielinor, has escaped the watch of the Grand Tree gnomes.

With a history of leaving large-scale conflicts and warfare in his wake, Glough must be tracked down and stopped. King Narnode Shareen needs your help.
Monkey Madness II.items_required:
*A [[lemon]]
*[[Grapes]]
*A [[pestle and mortar]]
*A [[pickaxe]] (if you choose the tanking route)
*[[Logs]]
*A [[light source]]
*A [[hammer]] (obtainable during the quest)
*A [[chisel]] (obtainable during the quest)
*An [[m'speak amulet]]
*A [[monkey talisman]] (obtainable during the quest)
*A [[ninja monkey greegree]] (small or medium)
*A [[slash weapon]] (to slash webs if you choose the tanking route - [[Wilderness sword]] of any tier recommended for this due to 100% web slash ability).
*The [[Translation book]] from [[The Grand Tree]]. If you have lost it a replacement can be obtained from King Narnode during the quest.
Monkey Madness II.ironman_concerns: [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] The player briefly visits [[Entrana]].
Monkey Madness II.start_point: Talk to [[King Narnode Shareen]] in the [[Grand Tree]]. (You must have '''all the requirements''' to start the quest).

Bear Your Soul.name: Bear Your Soul
Bear Your Soul.description: Discover and restore an ancient artefact.
Bear Your Soul.items_required: *[[Spade]] *[[Dusty key]] or [[File:Agility icon.png|link=Agility|alt=Agility]] 70/80 [[Agility]] [boostable] or [[Key master teleport]]
Bear Your Soul.start_point: Speak to [[Aretha]] after reading a book called [[soul journey]] in the [[Arceuus Library]].

The Enchanted Key.name: The Enchanted Key
The Enchanted Key.description: Use the enchanted key to find buried treasure across Gielinor.
The Enchanted Key.items_required:
*[[Enchanted key]] (remove from your [[steel key ring]] if you previously put it there)
*[[Spade]]
The Enchanted Key.start_point: Begin this mini-quest by getting your [[enchanted key|enchanted key]]. If lost, it can be reclaimed from the [[silver merchant]] in [[East Ardougne]] for 500 coins.

Mage Arena I.name: Mage Arena I
Mage Arena I.description: Prove your magical might to Kolodion and gain the ability to fight in his arena.
Mage Arena I.items_required:
*Runes or a [[powered staff]] to fight [[Kolodion]]
*Something to cut webs ([[knife]], [[slash weapon]], or any [[Wilderness sword]])
Mage Arena I.enemies_to_defeat: [[Kolodion#Demon|Kolodion]] in the form of a [[Black Demon]] (level 112)
Mage Arena I.ironman_concerns: '''Warning:''' [[File:Hardcore ironman chat badge.png]] This quest requires travel to the deep, level 56 [[Wilderness]]. Caution must be taken not only getting to the arena but also during the [[miniquest]]. [[Player-killers]] are able to attack you while you are doing the minigame fight.
Mage Arena I.start_point: Speak to [[Kolodion]] at the [[Mage Arena]] bank located in level 53 [[Wilderness]].

Family Pest.name: Family Pest
Family Pest.description: You've helped Dimintheis get his family crest back, but what's next? Surely he or his family have another quest for you... right?

Misthalin Mystery.name: Misthalin Mystery
Misthalin Mystery.description: A woman cries as a man lays dying, desperately they beg you to stop a cryptic killer before their friends suffer a similar fate.
Misthalin Mystery.items_required:
* [[Bucket]] (obtainable during quest)
*[[Tinderbox]] (obtainable during quest)
*[[Knife]] (obtainable during quest)
Misthalin Mystery.start_point: Speak to [[Abigale]] (or [[Hewey]], who sits next to her) in the south-eastern corner of [[Lumbridge Swamp]], near the [[fishing spots]].

Client of Kourend.name: Client of Kourend
Client of Kourend.description: Will you help Veos handle a mysterious client?
Client of Kourend.items_required: * [[Feather]] ''(a [[Stripy feather|stripy]] or coloured feather can also be used, but a [[magic gold feather]] cannot)''

The Queen of Thieves.name: The Queen of Thieves
The Queen of Thieves.description: The gangs of Kourend have recently been uniting under a mysterious figure called the Queen of Thieves, somewhere in Port Piscarilius. This novice quest tasks the player with infiltrating the gangs of Kourend in order to find the Queen of Thieves.
The Queen of Thieves.items_required: * [[Stew]] ''(Cooking ingredients can be obtained during quest for 6 [[coins]]. Requires 25 [[Cooking]])''

Bone Voyage.name: Bone Voyage
Bone Voyage.description:
Curator Haig Halen needs your help to finish the construction of a barge fit to take the Varrock museum crew to an unexplored landmass to the east. There have been many attempts to reach the island, but none were successful.

This quest will have you venturing across Gielinor, aiding the Varrock mus
Bone Voyage.start_point: Talk to [[Curator Haig Halen]] in the [[Varrock Museum]].

Dragon Slayer II.name: Dragon Slayer II
Dragon Slayer II.description: 30 years prior to the fateful day a mighty adventurer awakened Elvarg from her slumber, Crandor was a thriving and very much alive island, with a great tradition of mages and adventurers. The history of Crandor is a bold one, enough so that many a Crandorian earned the right to be part of the Champi
Dragon Slayer II.items_required:
*A [[pickaxe]]
*An [[axe]]
*8 [[oak plank]]s
*10 [[swamp paste]]
*At least 12 [[nail]]s of any kind ('''Note:''' Bringing more is recommended; nails may bend!)
*A [[hammer]]
*A [[machete]]
*A [[saw]]
*[[Catspeak amulet]]
*[[Ghostspeak amulet]] ([[Morytania Legs 2]]+ work)
*[[Goutweed]]
*Cut [[dragonstone]]
*2 [[molten glass]]
*[[Glassblowing pipe]]
*[[Spade]]
*[[Astral rune]]
*[[Seal of passage]] (or [[elite Fremennik Diary]] completion)
*[[Tinderbox]]
*[[Pestle and mortar]]
*Runes for 3 [[Fire Wave]] spells '''or''' 3 [[Fire Surge]] spells
Dragon Slayer II.enemies_to_defeat:
*[[Vorkath]] ''(level 392)''
*[[Spawn (Dragon Slayer II)|Spawn]] ''(level 100)''
*[[Robert the Strong]] '' (level 224) ''
*2 [[Green dragon]] ''(level 79)''
*2 [[Blue dragon]] ''(level 111)''
*[[Red dragon]] ''(level 152)''
*[[Iron dragon]] ''(level 189)''
*[[Brutal green dragon]] ''(level 227)''
*[[Black dragon]] ''(level 227)''
*[[Steel dragon]] ''(level 246)''
*[[Brutal red dragon]] ''(level 289)''
*[[Mithril dragon]] ''(level 304)''
*[[Adamant dragon]] ''(level 338)''
*[[Rune dragon]] ''(level 380)''
*[[Galvek]] ''(level 608)''
Dragon Slayer II.start_point: Speak to [[Alec Kincade]] outside the [[Myths' Guild]].

The Depths of Despair.name: The Depths of Despair
The Depths of Despair.description: Artur Hosidius has gone missing, and Lord Kandur Hosidius needs your help to find his son and bring him home.
The Depths of Despair.start_point: Speak to [[Lord Kandur Hosidius]] at his home in [[Hosidius]], north-west of the [[Vinery]].

Recipe for Disaster/Another Cook's Quest.name: Recipe for Disaster/Another Cook's Quest
Recipe for Disaster/Another Cook's Quest.description:
The cook in Lumbridge has a surprisingly small number of ingredients to hand in his kitchen, possibly as a result of an endless stream of newcomers with light fingers taking anything that isn't nailed down from his kitchen.

Luckily, in the past a kind [[Cook's Assistant]] was willing to help him in
Recipe for Disaster/Another Cook's Quest.items_required:
*[[Eye of newt]]
*[[Greenman's ale]]
*[[Rotten tomato]]
*Any [[fruit blast]] excluding a [[premade fr' blast]]
*[[Ashes]]

Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Goblin g.name: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Goblin generals
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Goblin g.description: The goblin generals need a food that they'll both eat. Find a chef in [[Goblin Village]] to learn how to make it.
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Goblin g.items_required:
*[[Bread]]
*[[Orange slices]]
** Use a [[knife]] on an [[orange]]
*Either [[blue dye|blue]], [[black dye|black]], [[green dye|green]], or [[purple dye]] (purple dye spawns directly east of the [[spirit tree]] at the [[Khazard Battlefield]], south of the [[Ardougne Castle]])
*[[Spice]] or [[gnome spice]] (can be bought or stolen from the [[Ardougne market]], bought from the [[Culinaromancer's Chest]] or bought in the [[Tree Gnome Stronghold]])
*[[Fishing bait]]
*[[Bucket of water]]
*[[Charcoal]] or [[ground charcoal]] (buy in [[Shilo Village (location)|Shilo Village]], [[Jiminua's Jungle Store]], or [[Gunslik's Assorted Items]])
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Goblin g.start_point: Inspect one of the goblin generals in the Lumbridge Castle dining room.

Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Mountain.name: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Mountain Dwarf
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Mountain.description: To save the dwarf, you will need a special dwarven rock cake. Only his father in the tunnel under [[White Wolf Mountain]] knows how to make one.
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Mountain.items_required:
*[[Bucket of milk]] (can be bought from the [[Culinaromancer's Chest]])
*[[Pot of flour]] (can be bought from the Culinaromancer's chest)
*[[Egg]] (can be bought from the Culinaromancer's chest)
*[[Bowl of water]] (a [[bowl]] and [[sink]] can be found in the kitchen of [[Lumbridge Castle]])
*4 [[Asgarnian ale]]s (can be bought during the quest at the [[Rising Sun Inn]] in [[Falador]])
**Recommended to bring at least 2 additional ales in case Rohak sobers up 
*306 [[coins]] (300 + 1 coin per ale, 100 instead of 300 if you choose to bring an [[activated ring of charos]], 150 instead of 300 if you choose to bring a [[chocolate bar]])
** Bring additional coins if buying the other ingredients or bringing additional ales
*[[Gloves]], If not using gloves make sure to bring [[Telekinetic Grab]] spell runes (''[[Ice gloves]] are the best, but other gloves will work fine too'')
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Mountain.start_point: Inspect the frozen dwarf in the Lumbridge Castle dining room to start the quest.

Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Evil Dave.name: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Evil Dave
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Evil Dave.description: Evil Dave needs some kind of spicy stew. You will need Evil Dave's help for this one. He lives in his mother's basement...
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Evil Dave.items_required:
*Any [[cat]] except an [[overgrown cat]] (note that each cat has a different effective [[rat catch rate]] -- [[kitten]]s or [[lazy cat]]s are not recommended)
*Several bowls of [[stew]] (9 at most)
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Evil Dave.start_point: Inspect [[Evil Dave]] in the Lumbridge Castle dining room.

Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Pirate Pete.name: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Pirate Pete
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Pirate Pete.description: Pirate Pete needs a fishcake to be protected. Talk to the Lumbridge Cook to learn how to make one.
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Pirate Pete.items_required:
*A [[raw cod]] (It's highly recommended to get at least 3 of all the ingredients, as the Fishcake can burn)
*A [[pestle and mortar]]
*[[Bread]] (at least 3 recommended)
*A [[knife]]
*A [[fishbowl]] (empty)
*A [[needle]] ([[Costume needle]] does not work)
*3 [[bronze wire]]
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Pirate Pete.ironman_concerns: [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] This quest has a weight limit. *[[File:Crafting icon.png|link=Crafting|alt=Crafting]] 42 [[Crafting]] [boostable] (If crafting a [[fishbowl]] yourself or by completing [[Rum Deal]]) *[[File:Smithing icon.png|link=Smithing|alt=Smithing]] 4 [[Smithing]] [boostable] (To make the bronze wire.)

Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Lumbridg.name: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Lumbridge Guide
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Lumbridg.description: You need a [[cake of guidance]] to protect the Lumbridge Guide. Wizard Traiborn in the Wizards' Tower can teach you how to make one.
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Lumbridg.items_required: *A [[bucket of milk]] *An [[egg]] *A [[pot of flour]] *A [[cake tin]] (All required items can be bought from the [[Culinaromancer's Chest]]) '''Recommended:''' *[[Minigame Teleport]] to [[Guardians of the Rift]] *[[Necklace of passage]] or access to [[fairy rings]] (DIS) and a [[Lunar staff|Lunar]]/[[Dramen staff]] (for quick transportation to [[Wizards' Tower]])

Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Sir Amik Var.name: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Sir Amik Varze
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Sir Amik Var.description: Sir Amik Varze requires a crème brulee supreme. Talk to the Cook to learn how to make one in this complicated but fun subquest.
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Sir Amik Var.items_required: *[[Bucket of milk]] (can be bought from the [[Culinaromancer's Chest]]) or [[Bottomless milk bucket]] *[[Pot of cream]] (can be bought from the [[Culinaromancer's Chest]]) *[[Pot of cornflour]] ** [[Sweetcorn]] (Can be picked from the [[sweetcorn (scenery)|sweetcorn field]]s south of [[Civitas illa Fortis]] in [[Varlamore]] without [[File:Farming icon.png|link=Farming|alt=Farming]] 20 [[Farming]] [boostable]) *[[Vanilla pod]] or tools to get one: ** Any [[axe]] (the [[Blessed axe]] will not work!) ** Any [[machete]] (as well as [[Radimus notes]] if you haven't completed [[Legends' Quest]]) *[[Dramen staff]] or [[Lunar staff]] to enter [[Zanaris]] if the [[Lumbridge_&_Draynor_Diary#Elite|Elite Lumbridge & Draynor diary]] has not been completed. *[[Dramen branch]] to make cinnamon *[[Pestle and mortar]] *[[Ice gloves]] (only required if wanting to take no damage from the flamee. Anti-dragon shield will mitigate most of the damage. (<10) *[[Raw chicken]]
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Sir Amik Var.enemies_to_defeat:
*[[Evil Chicken (Recipe for Disaster)|Evil Chicken]] ''(level varies)'' (see below)
*[[Black dragon]] ''(level 227)''
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Sir Amik Var.ironman_concerns:
[[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] The player visits [[Entrana]]
[[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] Advised to collect your [[dramen branch]] beforehand as the [[brulee]] does not appear on death piles.

Recipe for Disaster/Freeing King Awowoge.name: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing King Awowogei
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing King Awowoge.description: Awowogei needs his favourite food, but nobody seems to know what it is. Maybe you should go talk to him in his palace.
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing King Awowoge.items_required:
*[[M'speak amulet]]
*[[Karamjan monkey greegree]] (unless you already have another greegree)
*[[Gorilla greegree|Gorilla]], [[Ninja monkey greegree|Ninja monkey]] and [[Zombie monkey greegree]] (can be obtained during the quest with a [[Monkey Talisman]] each and 3000 coins)
*[[Red Banana]] (obtained during the quest)
*[[Tchiki monkey nuts]] or around 13 [[coins]] to buy some.
*[[Rope]]
*[[Knife]] or [[slashing]] weapon
*[[Pestle and mortar]]
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing King Awowoge.enemies_to_defeat:
*[[Big Snake]] ''(level 84) - (recommended to kill several as they burn often when cooking)''
*'''To obtain the greegrees:'''
**[[Zombie monkey]] ''(levels 82/129)''
**[[Monkey guard]] ''(level 167)''
**[[Monkey archer]] ''(level 86)''

Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Skrach Uglog.name: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Skrach Uglogwee
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Skrach Uglog.description: To protect Skrach Uglogwee from the Culinaromancer, you need to feed him a cooked jubbly bird, the most delicious type of Chompy in the Feldip Hills. You need to talk to [[Rantz]] to learn how to get one.
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Skrach Uglog.items_required:
*[[Axe]] (a [[blessed axe]] will not work)
*[[Raw chompy]] (bring several if you have a low cooking level)
*[[Iron spit]] (made on an anvil with an [[iron bar]])
*[[Logs]] (obtainable during the quest)
*[[Tinderbox]]
*[[Pickaxe]]
*[[Ogre bellows]] (obtainable during the quest)
*[[Ball of wool]]
*[[Ogre bow]] or [[comp ogre bow]] (can be purchased from Rantz during the quest for at most 600 [[coins]])
*[[Ogre arrows]] Chisel, knife, wolf bones, and feathers if you are making your own ogre arrows ([[Brutal arrows]] also work if using a comp ogre bow)
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Skrach Uglog.ironman_concerns: *[[File:Smithing icon.png|link=Smithing|alt=Smithing]] 17 [[Smithing]] [boostable] for an [[iron spit]] without access to [[Weiss]]

Recipe for Disaster/Full guide.name: Recipe for Disaster/Full guide
Recipe for Disaster/Full guide.description: The Culinaromancer has retreated through the portal back to his own dimension, where he'll be ready to cause more trouble. Aris has said that you too must go through the portal to finish him off.
Recipe for Disaster/Full guide.items_required:
*[[Ice gloves]] ''for defeating Flambeed''
*[[Menaphite remedy]] or [[restore potion]]s ''since Karamel will lower your stats''
*Certain armour and weapons for each boss
*Good healing food, such as [[shark]]s
Recipe for Disaster/Full guide.start_point: Release each member of the council from the Culinaromancer's spell.

Mage Arena II.name: Mage Arena II
Mage Arena II.description: Can you defeat the followers of the gods?
Mage Arena II.items_required:
*[[God staves]]
*Runes to cast [[god spells]] ([[Air rune|air]], [[Fire rune|fire]], and [[Blood rune|blood]])
*Item to cut webs ([[knife]], [[slash weapon]] or any [[Wilderness sword]])
Mage Arena II.ironman_concerns: '''Warning:''' [[File:Hardcore ironman chat badge.png]] This quest requires travel to the deep, level 56 Wilderness, and can require travel through various multi-combat Wilderness areas as well. Beware of Player-killers at all times.
Mage Arena II.start_point: Speak to [[Kolodion]] at the [[Mage Arena]] bank located in level 53 [[Wilderness]].

The Corsair Curse.name: The Corsair Curse
The Corsair Curse.description:
From the newly revealed town of Corsair Cove, deep in the south of Feldip Hills, the captain of the Corsairs has sailed to Port Sarim seeking help. His crew is sick, and he suspects that a curse has been laid upon them.

If you can unravel the fearful and conflicting tales of ogre relics, vengeful m
The Corsair Curse.start_point: Speak to [[Captain Tock]] at the crossroads north of [[Port Sarim]].

A Taste of Hope.name: A Taste of Hope
A Taste of Hope.description: For many years, the Myreque have fought back against the reign of the Drakans. To many, they act as a glimmer of hope in an otherwise miserable existence. In recent times, the Myreque have been beset by disaster, with numerous members meeting a gruesome end at the hands of Vanstrom Klause, the bruta
A Taste of Hope.items_required:
*1,000 [[coins]] (More if taking [[Andras]]' boat from the [[Ectofuntus]], 10,000 each trip)
*[[Knife]] or [[sickle]]
*[[Emerald]]
*[[Chisel]]
*Either:
**3 [[air rune]]s, 1 [[cosmic rune]], and having your magic set to the [[standard spells|standard Spellbook]]
**An [[enchant emerald or jade]] [[tablet]]
*[[Rod of ivandis]] (obtainable during the quest)
*[[Pestle and mortar]] (obtainable during the quest)
*[[Vial of water]] (obtained during the quest)
*[[Weapons]] and [[armour]]
A Taste of Hope.start_point: Speak to [[Garth (Theatre of Blood)|Garth]] in [[Ver Sinhaza]] by the entrance to the [[Theatre of Blood]].

Tale of the Righteous.name: Tale of the Righteous
Tale of the Righteous.description: Over a thousand years ago, King Shayzien VII ordered an expedition to Mount Quidamortem. The expedition never returned and it was assumed that all members perished in the field. However, a recent discovery suggests this may not have been the case. Phileas Rimor is the descendant of the expedition le
Tale of the Righteous.items_required:
*A [[pickaxe]]
*A [[ranged]] weapon with ammunition. Darts and throwing knives also work.
*Runes for 2-3 casts of any [[magic]] [[combat spell]]
*A [[melee]] weapon of any kind ([[pickaxe]] will work)
*[[Rope]]
Tale of the Righteous.start_point: Speak to [[Phileas Rimor]] in his house west of [[The Cloak and Stagger]] tavern in [[Shayzien]].

Making Friends with My Arm.name: Making Friends with My Arm
Making Friends with My Arm.description: My Arm, the troll from ''My Arm's Big Adventure'', must embark on another journey of discovery. This time, you'll be escorting him to Weiss, a newly discovered troll town in the far north of the world, so he can share his wisdom ''*cough*'' with the inhabitants, in exchange for learning about their
Making Friends with My Arm.items_required:
*[[Saw]]
*[[Bolt of cloth]]
*5 [[mahogany plank]]s
*[[Cadava berries]]
*[[Bucket of water]] (obtainable during quest)
*[[Rope]] (obtainable during quest)
*[[Pickaxe]] (obtainable during quest)
*[[Hammer]] (obtainable during quest)
*[[Combat equipment]] (preferably [[Melee]] or [[Ranged]])
*[[Climbing boots]] (if not using Trollheim teleports)
Making Friends with My Arm.start_point: Talk to [[Burntmeat]] in the [[Troll Stronghold (location)|Troll Stronghold's]] kitchen.

Recipe for Disaster/Defeating the Culina.name: Recipe for Disaster/Defeating the Culinaromancer
Recipe for Disaster/Defeating the Culina.description: The Culinaromancer has retreated through the portal back to his own dimension, where he'll be ready to cause more trouble. Aris has said that you too must go through the portal to finish him off.
Recipe for Disaster/Defeating the Culina.items_required:
*[[Ice gloves]] ''for defeating Flambeed''
*[[Menaphite remedy]] or [[restore potion]]s ''since Karamel will lower your stats''
*Certain armour and weapons for each boss
*Good healing food, such as [[shark]]s
Recipe for Disaster/Defeating the Culina.start_point: Release each member of the council from the Culinaromancer's spell.

Song of the Elves.name: Song of the Elves
Song of the Elves.description: Our story begins with you, the hero, answering the call to adventure when Edmond, a resident of Ardougne, asks for your aid. *[[Steel full helm]]
*[[Steel platebody]]
*[[Steel platelegs]]
*[[Red dye]]
*[[Purple dye]]
*[[Mourner gear]] including [[gas mask]] ([[plague jacket]] and [[plague trousers|trousers]] do not work)
*[[Silk]]
*[[Runite bar]]

*[[Tinderbox]]
*[[Rope]]
*[[Nature rune]]
*[[Flowers]] from [[mithril seeds]] or [[adamant seeds]], or an [[irit leaf]]

*[[Wine of zamorak]] OR [[Zamorak brew|Zamorak brew(4)]]
*[[Cabbage]] (obtainable during the quest)
*[[Black knife]] OR [[black dagger]]
*[[Cadantine seed]]
*[[Seed dibber]] (or partial completion of the [[Farming]] portion of the [[Barbarian Training]] [[miniquest]])
*[[Vial of water]]
*[[Pestle and mortar]]
*[[Hammer]]
*[[Saw]]
*[[Pickaxe]]
*[[Axe]]
*[[Spade]]

Song of the Elves.start_point: Talk to [[Edmond]], who can be found at his home in East Ardougne, north of the [[Flying Horse Inn]].

The Ascent of Arceuus.name: The Ascent of Arceuus
The Ascent of Arceuus.description: A long time ago, the citizens of Arceuus house gave up their human bodies and claimed immortality. This event was known as the Ascent of Arceuus and it marked the end of death within Arceuus house. Or so they believed. For the first time in a thousand years, a member of Arceuus house has fallen. You

The Forsaken Tower.name: The Forsaken Tower
The Forsaken Tower.description: For thousands of years, the Wintertodt has remained safely sealed behind the great Doors of Dinh. Alas, despite the best attempts of the Lovakengj smiths and the Arceuus mages, the doors are failing. Only Dinh himself knew the secrets behind the creation of these doors. Without his knowledge, there

The Fremennik Exiles.name: The Fremennik Exiles
The Fremennik Exiles.description: The Fremennik bards often sing tales of the past. Of great battles, deadly foes and heroes long gone. However, in recent years, things have been much quieter in the Province, as there's increasingly less to sing about. All that is about to change though, as what starts off as a simple investigation
The Fremennik Exiles.items_required: *[[Combat equipment]] *[[Mirror shield]] *2 [[Kegs of beer]] (cannot be obtained in Rellekka during quest, buy from [[Thora the Barkeep]] before starting the quest) *1 [[Molten glass]] *100 [[Astral rune]]s *[[Pet rock]] *[[Fishing rod]] or [[fly fishing rod]] *[[Fremennik shield]] (this is '''not''' the tradeable [[Neitiznot shield]]) **Alternatively, get it from [[Bardur]] AFTER being exiled, for either 150,000 '''or''' for free using a [[Ring of charos (a)]] **Recommended: [[Rune thrownaxe]]s or [[File:Agility icon.png|link=Agility|alt=Agility]] 85 [[Agility]] to get to [[Bardur]] in the [[Waterbirth Island Dungeon]] *[[Ice gloves]] or [[Smiths gloves (i)]] *[[Hammer]] *[[Glassblowing pipe]] *3 [[Lunar bar|Lunar bars]] **Or a [[pickaxe]] to obtain 3 [[Lunar ore|ores]] to turn into [[Lunar bar|Lunar bars]] (requires [[File:Mining icon.png|link=Mining|alt=Mining]] 60 [[Mining]] [boostable]). *[[Seal of Passage]] (if the [[Fremennik diaries|elite Fremennik diaries]] aren't completed)
The Fremennik Exiles.enemies_to_defeat:
*[[Basilisk Youngling]] (level 53)
*[[Basilisk]] (level 61)
*[[Monstrous Basilisk (The Fremennik Exiles)|Monstrous Basilisk]] (level 135)
*[[Typhor]] (level 218)
*[[The Jormungand]] (level 363)

X Marks the Spot.name: X Marks the Spot
X Marks the Spot.description: Veos has long been a lover of treasure hunts and his latest hunt has brought him to Lumbridge. However, not knowing the area well, Veos could use some help finding this treasure.
X Marks the Spot.start_point: Speak to [[Veos]] in [[The Sheared Ram]] in [[Lumbridge]].

The Curse of Arrav.name: The Curse of Arrav
The Curse of Arrav.description: The icy waste to the north of the troll country is as mysterious as any land in Gielinor, no less because of the interest the Mahjarrat take in it. Elias White thinks that he might have found a way for you to discover more about this strange area to the north. Uncover more of the mysteries of the Ma
The Curse of Arrav.items_required:
*3 [[dwellberries]]
*[[Ring of life]]
*Any [[pickaxe]]
*Any [[Crossbow (weapon)|crossbow]] (except the [[love crossbow]])
*[[Mith grapple]] ([[Hallowed grapple]] will not work)
*[[Insulated boots]]
The Curse of Arrav.ironman_concerns: * [[File:Fletching icon.png|link=Fletching|alt=Fletching]] 59 [[Fletching]] [boostable] to make a [[mith grapple]] * [[File:Smithing icon.png|link=Smithing|alt=Smithing]] 59 [[Smithing]] [boostable] to make a [[mith grapple tip]] '''OR''' * [[File:Firemaking icon.png|link=Firemaking|alt=Firemaking]] 35 [[Firemaking]] [not boostable] for [[Barbarian Firemaking]], to access the [[Ancient Cavern]], and the means to defeat level 132 enemies for a 1/42.67 chance at a [[mith grapple]] drop (ideally the [[skeleton brute]]).
The Curse of Arrav.start_point: Speak to [[Elias White]] at the [[Uzer Oasis]].

Defender of Varrock.name: Defender of Varrock
Defender of Varrock.description: Long ago, one man wielding a magic shield stood against the undead armies of the Mahjarrat. His name was Arrav, and he was strong and noble, but the fates had spoken: his downfall at the hands of the evil necromancer Zemouregal was inevitable. In one last desperate act to save his people, Arrav flun
Defender of Varrock.items_required:
* [[Chaos core]] (obtainable during the quest)
*[[Barronite deposit]] or a [[pickaxe]] to mine one
Defender of Varrock.enemies_to_defeat:
* At least six [[Armoured_zombie_(Defender_of_Varrock)|armoured zombie]]s (level 85)
* Multiple [[Chaos Golem]]s (level 70)
Defender of Varrock.start_point: Speak to [[Elias White]] in the [[Jolly Boar Inn]].

In Search of Knowledge.name: In Search of Knowledge
In Search of Knowledge.description: Assist [[Brother Aimeri]] in uncovering the mysteries that lie in the [[Forthos Dungeon]].
In Search of Knowledge.items_required:
* [[Combat equipment]]
* 5 pieces of food (must be meat, fish or vegetables)
* A [[slash weapon]] or [[knife]] to cut through webs
In Search of Knowledge.start_point: [[Brother Aimeri]], towards the south-eastern end of the [[Forthos Dungeon]].

Sins of the Father.name: Sins of the Father
Sins of the Father.description: A darkness has fallen over Morytania. The Myreque freedom fighters have achieved their first major victory over the vampyres with the death of [[Ranis Drakan]]. However, their newly found hope is unlikely to last. After many years of absence, [[Lord Lowerniel Vergidiyad Drakan|Lord Lowerniel Drakan]
Sins of the Father.items_required:
*Any [[axe]]
*[[Vyrewatch outfit]] ([[Vyrewatch top|top]], [[Vyrewatch legs|legs]], and [[Vyrewatch shoes|shoes]]) or 1,950 coins to purchase it from [[Trader Sven's Black-market Goods]] (can be obtained during quest)
*[[Ivandis flail]]
*[[Ruby]]
*[[Knife]] (can be obtained during quest)
*[[Chisel]]
*Runes for [[Lvl-3 Enchant]] (1 [[cosmic rune]], 5 [[fire rune]]s) or an [[enchant ruby or topaz]] tablet
Sins of the Father.enemies_to_defeat:

*[[Damien Leucurte]] (level 204)

A Kingdom Divided.name: A Kingdom Divided
A Kingdom Divided.description:
Great Kourend: a land of wealth, comfort and security. Across its five cities, the people of this Kingdom work to bring peace and prosperity. However, all is not as it seems. Beneath this delicate façade lies an uncomfortable truth: the kingdom of Great Kourend is A Kingdom Divided.

Since the death
A Kingdom Divided.items_required:
* Normal spellbook, and runes to cast a [[Fire spells|fire spell]] ([[fire bolt]] or better)
* Any [[axe]]
* A [[defence potion]] (3 or 4 doses)
* [[Volcanic sulphur]]
* [[Molten glass]]
* A [[dark essence block]] or a [[pickaxe]] and [[chisel]] (the latter is found at the mine) to obtain one
A Kingdom Divided.enemies_to_defeat:
* [[Judge of Yama (A Kingdom Divided)|Judge of Yama]] (Level 168)

* [[Xamphur]] (level 239)
* [[Barbarian Warlord]] (Level 91)
A Kingdom Divided.start_point: Speak to [[Martin Holt]] at the entrance to [[Kourend Castle]] in [[Kingstown]].

Daddy's Home.name: Daddy's Home
Daddy's Home.description: Marlo's father requires someone to refurnish his disastrous house.
Daddy's Home.items_required:
*10 [[plank]]s – (oak/teak/mahogany will '''not''' work)
*5 [[bolts of cloth]] (can be bought from the [[Sawmill]] for 650 coins per bolt)
*At least 16 [[nails]] of any type, e.g. [[steel nails]] (more are recommended as nails bend when building furniture. 100 is recommended at 1 [[Construction]], obtainable from the [[Sawmill]] during the quest)
*[[Hammer]] (Obtainable during the quest from Yarlo)
*[[Saw]] (Obtainable during the quest)
Daddy's Home.start_point: Speak to [[Marlo]] in the Estate Agent's house in north-east Varrock.

A Porcine of Interest.name: A Porcine of Interest
A Porcine of Interest.description: Ah, the Falador Farm - filled with cabbages, cows, and even a sleepy sheepdog. A peaceful place, where you can learn to churn butter, give a dog a bone, or purchase various Farming supplies. What was once a place of happiness, joy and wholesome dairy goods has of late become dominated by a vile pres
A Porcine of Interest.items_required:
* [[Rope]]
* [[Knife]] or a [[slash weapon]] (except [[abyssal whip]], [[abyssal tentacle]], [[noxious halberd]], or [[dragon claws]]) (obtainable during the quest)
A Porcine of Interest.start_point: Read the town [[Notice board (Draynor Village)|notice board]] in [[Draynor Village]], behind [[Fortunato]]'s [[Wine Shop]].

Getting Ahead.name: Getting Ahead
Getting Ahead.description: Gordon and Mary are two small-town farmers with a big problem – some heinous beastie keeps taking their livestock! Luckily they've hit upon a solution. They just need some brave adventurer to venture into the creature's lair and bring them back its head. We can't envisage any complications with such
Getting Ahead.items_required:
*[[Bear fur]], [[grey wolf fur]], or [[Fur]] (a [[Grizzly bear]] and [[bear cub]] spawn west of the farm and two [[black bear]]s spawn east of the farm)
*[[Soft clay]] or a [[pickaxe]] you can use
** [[Clay rocks]] are found in the [[Kebos Lowlands mine]] passed during the quest;
** Gordon's house has a water source and a bucket spawn;
** There is a [[Bronze pickaxe]] spawn at the mine, stuck in some [[Rocks (Getting Ahead)|rocks]].
*[[Hammer]] (obtainable near the cattle gate) or [[Imcando hammer]]
*[[Saw]] (obtainable in the shed north of the house) or [[Amy's saw]]
*2 normal [[plank]]s (obtainable near the cave entrance)
*At least 6 [[nails]] of any kind (iron nails can be found in the workbench north of the house, and more than 6 is recommended in case you bend a few)
*[[Knife]] (obtainable on the kitchen table in the house)
*[[Red dye]] (obtainable from the [[Shelves (Getting Ahead)|shelves]] in the house)
*[[Pot of flour]] (obtainable upstairs in the house)
*[[Needle]] (obtainable upstairs in the house) or [[Costume needle]]
*[[Thread]] (obtainable upstairs in the house) unless you're using the [[Costume needle]]
Getting Ahead.start_point: Speak to [[Gordon]] on [[Gordon and Mary's farm|his farm]] south of the [[Farming Guild]].

Below Ice Mountain.name: Below Ice Mountain
Below Ice Mountain.description: Willow, an energetic archaeologist (perhaps suspiciously so) is hunting for her next big discovery. She believes she has found the entrance to ancient underground ruins found to the west of Ice Mountain. There's a catch though - the entrance is sealed tight and she needs to re-unite her old crew to

* [[Bread]]
* [[Knife]]
* [[Beer]] (obtainable during the quest)

Below Ice Mountain.enemies_to_defeat: [[Ancient Guardian]] ''(level 25)'' (optional with a [[pickaxe]] and [[File:Mining icon.png|link=Mining|alt=Mining]] 10 [[Mining]] [boostable])

A Night at the Theatre.name: A Night at the Theatre
A Night at the Theatre.description: The Mysterious Stranger lurks outside of the mighty Theatre of Blood, the centrepiece of Ver Sinhaza. *[[Saw]] ([[crystal saw]] also works)
*[[Ghostspeak amulet]] ([[Morytania legs 2]] or better also work)
*Any [[axe]] besides the [[blessed axe]]
A Night at the Theatre.enemies_to_defeat:
*[[Vyrewatch]] (Level 105)
*Several venomous [[araxyte]] (Levels 96 and 146) (can be avoided)

*[[The Maiden of Sugadinti]]

*[[Sotetseg]]
*[[Xarpus]]
*[[Verzik Vitur]]

Beneath Cursed Sands.name: Beneath Cursed Sands
Beneath Cursed Sands.description: When Sophanem was beset by a series of plagues, the gates to the golden city of Menaphos quickly closed. The city has been locked down ever since, with no one allowed to leave or enter. However, rumours have escaped of strange happenings inside Menaphos and there are stories of citizens mysteriously
Beneath Cursed Sands.items_required:
* [[Coal]]
* [[Iron bar]]
* [[Tinderbox]] (obtainable during quest)
* [[Spade]] (obtainable during quest) 
* Any cooked or raw meat (fish, raw bird meat, raw yak meat, raw pyre fox, raw chompy, and raw t-bone steak will not work; purchasable from shop during quest)

Beneath Cursed Sands.start_point: Speak to [[Jamila]] at her stall in [[Sophanem]].

The Frozen Door.name: The Frozen Door
The Frozen Door.description: A messenger has an important letter for you. Apparently, a great treasure lies with the God Wars Dungeon beneath Trollheim. Defeat the leaders of each of the four armies and claim your prize behind the frozen door...
The Frozen Door.items_required:
* [[Mith grapple]]
* A [[Crossbow (weapon)|crossbow]]
* 2 [[rope]]s to reach [[Saradomin's Encampment]]; one-time only
* A [[hammer]] or [[dragon warhammer]]

Hopespear's Will.name: Hopespear's Will
Hopespear's Will.description: Help an old goblin spirit return the remains of his successors to the lost goblin homeland.
Hopespear's Will.items_required:
* [[Ghostspeak amulet]] or [[Morytania legs 2]] or better
* [[Ring of visibility]] or [[ring of shadows]]
* [[Goblin potion]]
* [[Dramen staff|Dramen]] or [[lunar staff]] (unless the [[Lumbridge & Draynor Diary]] has been fully completed)
* Food and potions to defeat 5 enemies without armour, weapons, or magic
Hopespear's Will.start_point: Speak to [[Hopespear]] in the crypt of the [[Goblin Temple]].

Temple of the Eye.name: Temple of the Eye
Temple of the Eye.description: The Abyss: a deadly realm filled with ancient power. The secrets of this plane have long eluded the denizens of the Wizards' Tower, though some would give anything to properly uncover them. One of these wizards is Persten. While many in the Tower aren't convinced by her unorthodox methods, a recent
Temple of the Eye.items_required:
* [[Bucket of water]]
* [[Chisel]] (obtainable during quest) 
* [[Pickaxe]] (obtainable during quest)
Temple of the Eye.start_point: [[Wizard Persten]] north of [[Al Kharid]], near the gate from [[Lumbridge]].

The Path of Glouphrie.name: The Path of Glouphrie
The Path of Glouphrie.description: King Bolren has a new pet. Unfortunately, it's another watcher, like the ones unmasked in the Tree Gnome Stronghold during The Eyes of Glouphrie. Has Glouphrie been watching the Tree Gnome Village, too? If you investigate further you'll find out more about Glouphrie, the history of the gnomes (and t
The Path of Glouphrie.items_required:
* [[Key (Tree Gnome Village Dungeon)|Tree Gnome Village Dungeon key]] (unless [[Roving Elves]] has been started) (obtainable during the quest)
* Combat equipment and some food
* [[Mith Grapple]]
* Any [[Crossbow_(weapon)|crossbow]]
The Path of Glouphrie.ironman_concerns: * [[File:Fletching icon.png|link=Fletching|alt=Fletching]] 59 [[Fletching]] [boostable] to make a [[mith grapple]] * [[File:Smithing icon.png|link=Smithing|alt=Smithing]] 59 [[Smithing]] [boostable] to make a [[mith grapple tip]] '''OR''' * [[File:Firemaking icon.png|link=Firemaking|alt=Firemaking]] 35 [[Firemaking]] [not boostable] for [[Barbarian Firemaking]], to access the [[Ancient Cavern]], and the means to defeat level 132 enemies for a 1/42.67 chance at a [[mith grapple]] drop (ideally the [[skeleton brute]]).
The Path of Glouphrie.start_point: Speak to [[King Bolren]] in the [[Tree Gnome Village (location)|Tree Gnome Village]].

Sleeping Giants.name: Sleeping Giants
Sleeping Giants.description:
Ahh, giants. We all know them. Enormous humanoids hanging about Gielinor, simply minding their own business until some adventurer or another comes along to beat them up.

But how much do we really know about these giants? What do they think about? Where did they come from? Would they enjoy making ma

* 3 [[oak logs]]
* 1 [[wool]] ([[ball of wool]] will '''not''' work)
* 10 [[nails]]
* [[Hammer]] or [[Imcando hammer]] (Other types of hammers will not work)
* [[Chisel]]
* 1 [[bucket of water]] (Obtainable during quest) or [[ice gloves]]

Sleeping Giants.start_point: Speak to [[Kovac]] at the [[Giants' Plateau]], east of [[Al Kharid]].

Into the Tombs.name: Into the Tombs
Into the Tombs.description:
Long ago, the people of the Kharidian Desert lived prosperous, luxurious lives, under the watchful eyes of their beloved gods.

All that changed when Amascut, the Goddess of Rebirth, became corrupted. She was reborn as The Devourer, intent on consuming every living soul in existence.

Deep within th
Into the Tombs.enemies_to_defeat:

The Garden of Death.name: The Garden of Death
The Garden of Death.description:
In our newest Twisted Tale, you’ll piece together a long-lost language and unravel the mystery of some ancient ruins. Welcome, adventurer, to The Garden of Death.

This quest contains no dialogue and focuses solely on environmental storytelling, so make sure that you set aside some time and prepare
The Garden of Death.start_point: Search the [[Tent (The Garden of Death)|tent]] at the campsite found east of the chasm at the southern end of the [[Kebos Lowlands]].

Desert Treasure II - The Fallen Empire.name: Desert Treasure II - The Fallen Empire
Desert Treasure II - The Fallen Empire.description: During Gielinor's Second Age, the world was dominated by an empire the likes of which had never been seen. Secrets of the North.name: Secrets of the North
Secrets of the North.description: Our story begins - as all good murder mysteries do - with a mysterious murder, and there's no shortage of suspects - in fact, you're one of them! Be prepared to form an uneasy alliance, dig up secrets best left buried deep, and walk away with more questions than answers...

*100 [[Coins]]
*[[Lockpick]] ([[Strange Old Lockpick]] does NOT work)
*[[Tinderbox]] (obtainable during the quest)

His Faithful Servants.start_point: Speak to the [[Strange Old Man]] wandering in the [[Barrows]], east of [[Mort'ton]].

Children of the Sun.name: Children of the Sun
Children of the Sun.description: Children of the Sun begins on the familiar streets of Varrock, where a delegation from Varlamore has arrived to sign a treaty with the rulers of Misthalin. It’s a day for celebration, but something’s amiss… you’d better find out what’s going on!

Perilous Moons.name: Perilous Moons
Perilous Moons.description:
Beneath the steep and jagged cliffs of Ralos' Rise sits the dwarven town of Cam Torum. Its strong fortifications have kept it safe through all of Varlamore's history... but now the inhabitants face a new challenge.

Lurking below the surface is an ancient temple, built to contain powerful enti
Perilous Moons.items_required:
* [[Knife]] (obtainable during quest)
* [[Big fishing net]] (obtainable during quest)
* [[Rope]] (obtainable during quest)
* [[Pestle and mortar]] (obtainable during quest)
* Combat equipment for multiple [[PvM]] encounters.
Perilous Moons.enemies_to_defeat:
* [[Sulphur Nagua (Perilous Moons)|Sulphur Nagua]] (level 98)
* [[Blue Moon]] (level 329)
* [[Blood Moon]] (level 329)
* [[Eclipse Moon]] (level 329)
Perilous Moons.start_point: Speak to [[Zuma]] or [[Attala]] at the entrance to [[Cam Torum]].

The Ribbiting Tale of a Lily Pad Labour.name: The Ribbiting Tale of a Lily Pad Labour Dispute
The Ribbiting Tale of a Lily Pad Labour.description: Solve a ribbiting dispute and gain access to a new [[Special_patches#Special_tree_patches|hardwood Farming patch]].
The Ribbiting Tale of a Lily Pad Labour.items_required: * Any axe (a [[bronze axe]] is obtainable during the quest)

Twilight's Promise.name: Twilight's Promise
Twilight's Promise.description: Take your first steps into [[Varlamore]] and unlock the [[Quetzal Transport System]].
Twilight's Promise.items_required: * Items for two different [[combat classes]] or a [[poisoned]] weapon (be warned that the poison method is much slower).
Twilight's Promise.start_point: Speak to [[Ennius Tullus]] or [[Furia Tullus]] in front of the [[Sunrise Palace]].

At First Light.name: At First Light
At First Light.description: Learn about the role of the Hunter Guild and unlock higher tier rumours.
At First Light.items_required:
* [[Needle]] or [[costume needle]] (one needle spawns in [[Pellem's Fur Store]] nearby)
* [[Hammer]] (obtainable during quest) or [[Imcando hammer]]
* 2 [[jerboa tail]]s (obtainable during quest)
** or a [[Box trap]] (can be bought from [[Imia's Supplies]] nearby)
At First Light.start_point: Speak to [[Guildmaster Apatura]] at the [[Hunter Guild]].

The Heart of Darkness.name: The Heart of Darkness
The Heart of Darkness.description: Uncover the secrets of a mysterious cult and unlock a new dungeon.
The Heart of Darkness.enemies_to_defeat:
* Several [[Emissary Brawler]]s ''(levels 74 and 86)''
* Several [[Emissary Conjurer]]s ''(level 71)''
* [[Prince Itzla Arkan]] ''(level 167)''
* [[Amoxliatl]] ''(level 263)''
The Heart of Darkness.ironman_concerns: [[File:Ultimate ironman chat badge.png]] You will need 3-4 empty inventory slots at multiple parts of the quest.
The Heart of Darkness.start_point: Speak to [[Prince Itzla Arkan]] at [[the Teomat]].

Death on the Isle.name: Death on the Isle
Death on the Isle.description: Solve a mysterious murder on the island of Aldarin.
Death on the Isle.enemies_to_defeat: Two low-level enemies must be fought with no equipment, but you can’t die to them
Death on the Isle.start_point: Speak to [[Patzi]] by the entrance to [[Villa Lucens]].

Meat and Greet.name: Meat and Greet
Meat and Greet.description: Bring the joy of kebabs to the land of Varlamore.
Meat and Greet.start_point: Talk to [[Emelio]] at his home in [[Outer Fortis]].

Ethically Acquired Antiquities.name: Ethically Acquired Antiquities
Ethically Acquired Antiquities.description: Investigate a mysterious theft from the Fortis Grand Museum.
Ethically Acquired Antiquities.start_point: Inspect the [[Empty display (Xerna's Diadem)|empty display]] on the ground floor of the [[Grand Museum]] in [[Civitas illa Fortis]].

The Blood Moon Rises.name: The Blood Moon Rises
The Blood Moon Rises.description: Long ago, before the vampyres were united as one, they lived in disparate clans in their ancestral home of Vampyrium. For the Drakan clan, strength ruled above all else. Clan leaders were selected during a brutal festival of combat known as the Night of the Blood Moon. Along with leadership of the c

The Final Dawn.name: The Final Dawn
The Final Dawn.description: Bring an end to the plots of a deadly cult and unlock a new boss.
The Final Dawn.items_required:
* [[Emissary robes]]
*Any kind of [[bones]] (except [[sun-kissed bones]]) or [[raw meat]]/[[cooked meat]]
*A [[knife]] (obtainable during the quest)
*A [[beer]] (obtainable during the quest)
The Final Dawn.enemies_to_defeat:
* [[Emissary Enforcer]] (lvl-196)
* [[Chimalli]] (lvl-160) and [[Lucius]] (lvl-160)
* Multiple waves of Emissary cultists (lvl-70 to lvl-90)
* [[Ennius Tullus]] (lvl-306)
* [[Augur Metzli]] (lvl-396)
The Final Dawn.start_point: Speak to [[Servius, Teokan of Ralos|Servius]] in the [[Sunrise Palace]].

Pandemonium.name: Pandemonium
Pandemonium.description: Embark on your first voyage and unlock the Sailing skill.
Pandemonium.items_required:
* [[Hammer]] (obtainable during the quest)
* [[Saw]] (obtainable during the quest)
Pandemonium.start_point: Speak to [[Will]] and [[Anne]] on the docks of Port Sarim.

Prying Times.name: Prying Times
Prying Times.description: Help Steve Beanie recover some 'pirate treasure' and unlock the crowbar, a useful Sailing tool.
Prying Times.items_required:
* [[Hammer]] or [[Imcando hammer]] (obtainable during quest)
* [[Steel bar]]
* [[Redberry pie]]
* [[Captain's log]]
Prying Times.ironman_concerns: *A method to obtain a redberry pie: **[[File:Cooking icon.png|link=Cooking|alt=Cooking]] 10 [[Cooking]] to cook one. A pie dish can be found in the [[Varrock Palace]] kitchen, and [[redberries]] can be picked just west of the [[South-east Varrock mine]] **Purchased from [[Romily Weaklax]] in the [[Cooks' Guild]] with [[File:Cooking icon.png|link=Cooking|alt=Cooking]] 32 [[Cooking]] and a [[chef's hat]] **Partial completion of [[Perilous Moons]] to purchase a Redberry pie from [[Yarnio]]
Prying Times.start_point: [[Steve Beanie]] on the Pandemonium might be able to help you with prying open crates.

Current Affairs.name: Current Affairs
Current Affairs.description: Help [[Arhein]] get past some political red tape and unlock the [[current duck]], a useful Sailing tool.
Current Affairs.start_point: Start the Quest by speaking to [[Arhein]] on the [[Catherby]] docks.

Troubled Tortugans.name: Troubled Tortugans
Troubled Tortugans.description: Help a stranded tortugan and gain access to the Great Conch.
Troubled Tortugans.items_required:
* Any [[axe]] (a bronze axe can be found during the quest, but bringing a better one is recommended for cutting 10 logs)
* [[Hammer]] (obtainable during the quest) or [[Imcando hammer]]
* [[Saw]] (obtainable during the quest) or [[Amy's saw]]
Troubled Tortugans.start_point: Talk to [[Floopa]], an [[Injured Tortugan]], on a [[Remote Island]] in the [[Unquiet Ocean]].

The Red Reef.name: The Red Reef
The Red Reef.description: Investigate the dealings of the Red Reef Trading Company and gain access to some trees to cut.
The Red Reef.items_required:
* A [[boat]]
* [[Cannon (facility)|Cannons]] on your boat for ship combat, or [[Magic]]/[[Ranged]] gear to skip it
The Red Reef.enemies_to_defeat:
* 2 pirate ships (or the crew on the ship)
** [[Oaky Doak]] (level 77)
** [[Boatswain Bill Teak]] (level 77)
** [[Mister U.]] (level 77)
** [[Captain Ruban Acer]] (level 95)
** [[Old Jack]] (level 77)
** [[Mute Jack]] (level 77)
** [[Bloody Jack]] (level 77)
** [[Captain Jack]] (level 95)
* 6 [[Pirate (The Red Reef)|pirates]] (level 52)
* [[Black Eye Bethel]] (level 191)
* [[Giant lobster (The Red Reef)|Giant lobster]] (level 112, safespottable)
The Red Reef.start_point: Talk to [[Elder Raley]] at his home on the [[Great Conch]].

Shadows of Custodia.name: Shadows of Custodia
Shadows of Custodia.description: Solve the case of some missing people and unlock a new dungeon.
Shadows of Custodia.items_required:
*4 [[willow longbow]]s
**Can be purchased from [[Arcuani's Archery Supplies]] in [[Tal Teklan]] or [[Lowe's Archery Emporium]] in [[Varrock]]
*4 [[maple logs]] (obtainable during quest)
*[[Fishing rod]]  (obtainable during quest) ([[Oily fishing rod]], [[Pearl fishing rod]], or [[Barbarian rod]] also works)
*[[Hammer]] (obtainable during quest)
Shadows of Custodia.ironman_concerns: *A method to obtain [[maple logs]]: **[[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] 45 [[Woodcutting]] [boostable] to cut [[maple trees]] **Guaranteed from the [[Managing Miscellania]] activity (requires [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] Throne of Miscellania ) **[[File:Firemaking icon.png|link=Firemaking|alt=Firemaking]] 50 [[Firemaking]] [not boostable] for maple logs from the [[Wintertodt]] [[Reward Cart]] (drop chance based on [[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] level). **Completing [[Hunters' Rumours]] with [[File:Hunter icon.png|link=Hunter|alt=Hunter]] 46 [[Hunter]] [not boostable] to receive [[Hunters' loot sack]] (drop chance based on [[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] level) **Using [[Serum 208]] for a 1/128 drop of maple logs from curing [[Afflicted]] (requires [[File:Quest point icon.png|link=Quest points|alt=Quest points]] Shades of Mort'ton ).
Shadows of Custodia.start_point: Read the [[Noticeboard (Shadows of Custodia)|notice board]] in [[Auburn Pub]].

Scrambled!.name: Scrambled!
Scrambled!.items_required:
All items obtainable during quest:
* [[Bowl of water]] (two spawn in town, fountain next to bank)
* 2 [[planks]] (south of the town near the water)
* 6 [[nails]] (more recommended as they can bend, 20 nails may be found on the workbench)
* [[Saw]] (spawn in range shop) 
* [[Hammer]] (spawn in furnace house)
Scrambled!.enemies_to_defeat:
* [[Large chicken]] ''(level 16)''
* [[Black jaguar (Scrambled!)|Black jaguar]] ''(level 88)'' - can be safespotted
* [[Red dragon (Scrambled!)|Red dragon]] ''(level 106)'' - can be safespotted
Scrambled!.start_point: Speak to [[Alan (Scrambled!)|Alan]] at the temple of [[Tal Teok]] north of [[Tal Teklan]].

Vale Totems (miniquest).name: Vale Totems (miniquest)
Vale Totems (miniquest).description: Vale Totems is a miniquest accompanying the Vale Totems minigame.
Vale Totems (miniquest).items_required:
* [[Oak logs]] or better (obtainable during the quest with an axe)
* [[Knife]] (obtainable during the quest)
* Four fletched items of the same type of wood as the logs, such as [[Oak longbow (u)|unstrung longbows]] or [[Oak shield|shields]] (obtainable during the quest)
Vale Totems (miniquest).ironman_concerns: * [[File:Woodcutting icon.png|link=Woodcutting|alt=Woodcutting]] 15 [[Woodcutting]] (to chop an [[Oak tree]])

Spirit of Adventure.name: Spirit of Adventure
Spirit of Adventure.description: The ghost of Bernina wishes to move on but has unfinished business.
Spirit of Adventure.start_point: Speak to [[Ghost (Bernina)|the ghost]] in [[Auburn Valley]] while wearing a [[ghostspeak amulet]].

Lost Lover.name: Lost Lover
Lost Lover.description: Reunite Fabia and Xilo by solving their miscommunication.
Lost Lover.start_point: Speak to either [[Fabia]] or [[Xilo]] in the [[Tlati Rainforest]]

Learning the Ropes.name: Learning the Ropes
Learning the Ropes.ironman_concerns: You must talk to the [[Ironman tutor (Tutorial Island)|Ironman tutor]] at the end of the quest to choose your [[Ironman Mode]] restrictions.
Learning the Ropes.start_point: Start this quest by talking to the [[Quest Guide]] on [[Tutorial Island]].

The Ides of Milk.name: The Ides of Milk
The Ides of Milk.description: Lumbridge, the iconic city for all the fresh-faced arrivals and budding adventurers. Home to regular citizens of Gielinor, they chop the trees, work the fields, and tend to the animals of the realm, except there has been a disturbance in the nearby cow pen and the steaks have never been higher! Dist
